---
title: "FINAL_18S_pretaxa"
author: "Nicole Pittoors"
date: "2025-08-26"
output: html_document
---

set working directory, do this in console
```{r}

    knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "Path/to/file")
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "Path/to/file")
```

Load required libraries
```{r}
# for data management
library(tidyr)
library(plyr)# when using plyr and dplyr, load plyr first
library(dplyr)
library(reshape2)

# for diversity analyses
library(vegan)
# for manipulations and analysis of linked asv/metadata tables
library(phyloseq)

# for plotting
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(gridExtra)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(scales)
library(ggsci)

library(lme4)
library(multcomp) 
library(dplyr)
library(emmeans)
library(performance)
library(coin)
library(broom.mixed)
library(patchwork)
library(car)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)
library(MuMIn)
library(openxlsx)
```

Load ASV table that only contains marine benthic metazoans
```{r}
# Load .csv file
metazoan_ASVtab_18S = read.csv("/Path/to/csvfile",row.names=1, sep=",")

# Conver to matrix
metazoan_ASVtab_18S=as.matrix(metazoan_ASVtab_18S)
#remove controls and eDNA samples
#Controls: COL 45"primers.trimmed.1392.045.NTC1_F_filt.fastq.gz" col96"primers.trimmed.1392.096.NTC2_F_filt.fastq.gz"
#eDNA samples: col [115-163]
# Indices of columns to be removed
columns_to_remove <- c(115:163)

# Remove the specified columns
metazoan_ASVtab_18S <- metazoan_ASVtab_18S[, -columns_to_remove]

# Verify the changes
dim(metazoan_ASVtab_18S) # 7089  114
print(head(metazoan_ASVtab_18S))
# Check class and check that it is numeric
class(metazoan_ASVtab_18S[1:3, 1:3])
```

Import Metadata
```{r}
metadata_ARMS <- read.csv("/Path/to/csvfile", row.names=1, sep=",")
#It changed the names of fraction columns during import, change column names
#names(ARMS_18S_metadata_R_2024_04) [names(ARMS_18S_metadata_R_2024_04) == 'fraction...4'] <- 'fraction'
#Check that it worked
colnames(metadata_ARMS)

# Convert tibble to data.frame for metadata table and set Sample_ID as row names for the metadata table
metadata_ARMS <- as.data.frame(metadata_ARMS)
#rownames(meta_dr17k) <- meta_dr17k$Sample_ID
#Remove 'Sample_ID' column
#meta_dr17k <- meta_dr17k[ , -which(names(meta_dr17k) == "Sample_ID")]
head(metadata_ARMS)
```

Normalize the fileID in the metadata to match the format of column names in the ASV table 
(Skip if loading R object)
```{r}
# Populate fileID_18S by converting dots to dashes (similar to 18S pattern)
metadata_ARMS$fileID_18S <- gsub("\\.", "-", metadata_ARMS$fileID0_18S)

# Rename ASV table columns using row names (Sample_IDs)
# Create mapping: fileID0_18S -> row names (Sample_IDs)
name_mapping <- setNames(rownames(metadata_ARMS), metadata_ARMS$fileID0_18S)

# Rename the columns, keeping the original column names if no match is found
new_colnames <- name_mapping[colnames(metazoan_ASVtab_18S)]
new_colnames[is.na(new_colnames)] <- colnames(metazoan_ASVtab_18S)[is.na(new_colnames)]

# Apply the new column names
colnames(metazoan_ASVtab_18S) <- new_colnames

# Verify the changes
print("Updated metadata structure:")
str(metadata_ARMS[c("fileID0_18S", "fileID_18S")])

print("Updated ASV table:")
print(head(metazoan_ASVtab_18S))

# Make sure all metadata rownames exist in the ASV table colnames
common_samples <- intersect(rownames(metadata_ARMS), colnames(metazoan_ASVtab_18S))

# Reorder ASV table columns according to metadata rownames
metazoan_ASVtab_18S <- metazoan_ASVtab_18S[, rownames(metadata_ARMS)]

# Double-check
all(colnames(metazoan_ASVtab_18S) == rownames(metadata_ARMS))  # should return TRUE

# Save metadata R object
save(metadata_ARMS, file = "/Path/to/Rdatafile")

# Save 18S metazaon table of ARMS only samples, with SH IDs for column names and in the order of metadata which is site then sample ordered
save(metazoan_ASVtab_18S, file = "/Path/to/Rdatafile")
```


# START HERE

# Skip all dataframe wrangling if starting with R data objects: 
Load data objects for analyses:
1. Metazoan filtered ASV table
2. Metadata
```{r}
load("./metazoan_ASVtab_18S.Rdata")
load("./metadata_ARMS.Rdata")
```

# Create new variable name for ASV table
Normally this is done while re-ordering samples based on a desired plotting order
biotic_18S = ordered ASV table, ARMS only, includes ASVs with zero reads across samples
```{r}
biotic_18S <- (metazoan_ASVtab_18S)
print(biotic_18S)
dim(biotic_18S)
```


# Create Phyloseq object
cycle18Sdat0 = phyloseq object
asvs with zero reads have NOT yet been removed!!
```{r}
otu <- otu_table(biotic_18S, taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
samp <-sample_data(metadata_ARMS)
cycle18Sdat <- phyloseq(otu, samp)
```


#----------------Raw Data Overview-------------------
View some details about the phyloseq object
cross check number of taxa, samples between component tables
**After checking if any asvs have zero reads across all samples and receiving back "TRUE", I removed those ASVs and went from 18645 to 16104 taxa
```{r}
print(cycle18Sdat)
#phyloseq-class experiment-level object
#otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 7089 taxa and 114 samples ]
#sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 114 samples by 44 sample variables ]

# take a look at phyloseq data
sample_names(cycle18Sdat)
sample_variables(cycle18Sdat)

# check out # reads summed per sample
sample_sums(cycle18Sdat)
sort(sample_sums(cycle18Sdat), decreasing = TRUE)
summary(sample_sums(cycle18Sdat))

# check out # reads summed per ASV
taxa_sums(cycle18Sdat)
summary(taxa_sums(cycle18Sdat))

# check if any asvs have zero reads across all samples
any(taxa_sums(cycle18Sdat) == 0)
sum(taxa_sums(cycle18Sdat) == 0) #757 ASVs with zero reads across all samples

#If true, can save original object then remove zero read asvs
cycle18Sdat0 <- cycle18Sdat
cycle18Sdat = prune_taxa(taxa_sums(cycle18Sdat) > 0, cycle18Sdat)

# Check dimenstions of final phyloseq object
print(cycle18Sdat)
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 6332 taxa and 114 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 114 samples by 44 sample variables ]

# Extract OTU table (samples as rows, ASVs as columns)
asv_table <- as.data.frame(t(otu_table(cycle18Sdat)))

# Create 18S specific metadata dataframe
metadata_18S <- metadata_ARMS

# Calculate and add to metadata
metadata_18S$relativeAbundance <- rowSums(asv_table)
metadata_18S$numberASVs <- rowSums(asv_table > 0)

# Save metadata
#save(metadata_18S, file = "./metadata_18S.Rdata")
# Clean up
rm(asv_table)

# Update metadata for phyloseq object
otu <- otu_table(biotic_18S, taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
samp <-sample_data(metadata_18S)
cycle18Sdat <- phyloseq(otu, samp)
```

# Export ASV table and phyloseq object
```{r}
save(biotic_18S, cycle18Sdat, cycle18Sdat0, metadata_18S, file = "./basic_18S_pretaxa_inputs.Rdata")
```

#----------------RAW READS---------------------
```{r}
# Create ordered factor for sites
ordered_sites_we <- c("Stetson_shallow", "EFGB_deep", "EFGB_shallow", 
                  "Bright_shallow", "Bright_deep1", "Bright_deep2",
                  "McGrail_deep", "Alderdice_deep1", "Alderdice_deep2",
                  "Alderdice_shallow", "Diaphus_deep1", "Diaphus_deep2")

# Define color palette for sites
c12_sites <- c("Alderdice_deep1" = "#0E4077",      
               "Alderdice_deep2" = "#0E4077",
               "Alderdice_shallow" = "#9DC9D3",
               "Bright_deep1" = "#3B8C6E",
               "Bright_deep2" = "#3B8C6E",
               "Bright_shallow" = "#B0DBBB",
               "Diaphus_deep2" = "#996599",
               "Diaphus_deep1" = "#996599",
               "EFGB_deep" = "#F68C30",
               "EFGB_shallow" = "#FEBC2C",
               "McGrail_deep" = "#015951",
               "Stetson_shallow" = "#FF595E")

# Melt the phyloseq data for plotting
cDat <- psmelt(cycle18Sdat)

# Remove rows with NA sitelocality
cDat_clean <- cDat[!is.na(cDat$sitelocality), ]

# Sum abundances by sample and site
cDat2 <- cDat_clean %>%
  group_by(Sample, sitelocality) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = 'drop')

# Create sample order grouped by site
sample_info <- data.frame(
  Sample = sample_names(cycle18Sdat),
  sitelocality = sample_data(cycle18Sdat)$sitelocality,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Remove any NA samples
sample_info <- sample_info[!is.na(sample_info$sitelocality), ]

# Order samples by site
sample_info$sitelocality <- factor(sample_info$sitelocality, levels = ordered_sites)
sample_info_ordered <- sample_info[order(sample_info$sitelocality, sample_info$Sample), ]
sampOrder_by_site <- sample_info_ordered$Sample

# Apply site factor levels and sample ordering to plot data
cDat2$sitelocality <- factor(cDat2$sitelocality, levels = ordered_sites)
cDat2$Sample <- factor(cDat2$Sample, levels = sampOrder_by_site)

# Create raw read abundance barplot
rawBar <- ggplot(cDat2, aes(x = Sample, y = Abundance, fill = sitelocality)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "Raw Read Counts: 18S", fill = "Site") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6, angle = 90, hjust = 0, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),  
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),  
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank() 
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c12_sites)

print(rawBar)

# save the plot if you like it, adjust path as needed
ggsave("18S_RawReads_Sample_Site.pdf", plot = last_plot(), device = "pdf", 
       path = "./Final_18S_",
       scale = 1, width = 11, height = 8.5, units = "in", dpi = 300)


# clean up
rm(cDat2, rawBar)
```

# Read Sums Plot Secletion
Look at number of reads per ASV and per sample
```{r}
# library(scales)  # for comma_format()
readsumsdf <- data.frame(nreads = sort(taxa_sums(cycle18Sdat), TRUE), 
                         sorted = 1:ntaxa(cycle18Sdat), type = "ASVs")
readsumsdf <- rbind(readsumsdf, 
                    data.frame(nreads = sort(sample_sums(cycle18Sdat), TRUE), 
                               sorted = 1:nsamples(cycle18Sdat), 
                               type = "Samples"))

readplot <- ggplot(readsumsdf, aes(x = sorted, y = nreads)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "18S: Total Reads by ASV, Sample", y = "Reads", x = "Sorted") +
  scale_y_log10(labels = comma_format()) +
  facet_wrap(~type, 1, scales = "free") +
  theme_light()

print(readplot)

# Save
ggsave("18S_Reads_ASVs_Samples.pdf", plot = readplot, 
       path = "./Final_18S_", width = 12, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

#------------RAREFACTION CURVES---------------------
# 1. Generate rarefaction data
Need to extract ASV table and transpose so that ASVs are rows, and samples are columns
```{r}
# Extract OTU table (samples as rows, ASVs as columns)
asv_table_t <- as.data.frame(t(otu_table(cycle18Sdat)))

# Get sample metadata
#metadata_18S<- as.data.frame(sample_data(cycle18Sdat))

# Generate rarefaction curves
# Set the steps for rarefaction (adjust max based on your data)
max_depth <- min(rowSums(asv_table_t))  # Use minimum sample depth
steps <- seq(100, max_depth, by = 100)  # Adjust step size as needed

# Create rarefaction data
rarefaction_data <- data.frame()

for(step in steps) {
  # Rarefy to current step
  rare_result <- rrarefy(asv_table_t, step)
  
  # Calculate richness for each sample
  richness <- rowSums(rare_result > 0)
  
  # Create temporary dataframe
  temp_df <- data.frame(
    sample = rownames(rare_result),
    step = step,
    richness = richness
  )
  
  # Add to main dataframe
  rarefaction_data <- rbind(rarefaction_data, temp_df)
}

# Add metadata to rarefaction data
rarefaction_data <- merge(rarefaction_data, metadata_18S, by.x = "sample", by.y = "row.names")
```

#2 Create Summary data for plotting
```{r}
# Summary by sitelocality
site_richness_summary <- rarefaction_data %>%
  group_by(step, sitelocality) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_richness = mean(richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_richness = sd(richness, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Summary by fraction
fraction_richness_summary <- rarefaction_data %>%
  group_by(step, fraction) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_richness = mean(richness, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_richness = sd(richness, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )
```

#3 Plot rarefaction curves
```{r}
# Define fraction colors
c3_fractionss <- c("sessile" = "#3B8C6E",
                  "500" = "#F68C30", 
                  "100" = "#FEBC2C")

# Plot by sitelocality
site_plot <- ggplot(site_richness_summary, aes(x = step, y = mean_richness, color = sitelocality, fill = sitelocality)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_richness - se_richness, ymax = mean_richness + se_richness), 
              alpha = 0.3, color = NA) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, alpha = 0.3) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c12_sites) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c12_sites) +
  labs(title = "18S: Rarefaction Curves by Site",
       x = "Sampling Depth (reads)", 
       y = "Average number of ASVs (richness)",
       color = "Site",
       fill = "Site") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 13),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 13),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 12)
  )

print(site_plot)

# Plot by fraction
fraction_plot <- ggplot(fraction_richness_summary, aes(x = step, y = mean_richness, color = fraction, fill = fraction)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_richness - se_richness, ymax = mean_richness + se_richness), 
              alpha = 0.3, color = NA) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, alpha = 0.3) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c3_fractionss) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c3_fractionss) +
  labs(title = "18S: Rarefaction Curves by Fraction",
       x = "Sampling Depth (reads)", 
       y = "Average number of ASVs (richness)",
       color = "Fraction",
       fill = "Fraction") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13, color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 13),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 13),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 12)
  )

print(fraction_plot)

# Save plots
ggsave("18S_rarefaction_by_site.pdf", plot = site_plot, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./Final_18S_", width = 12, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

ggsave("18S_rarefaction_by_fraction.pdf", plot = fraction_plot, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./Final_18S_", width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

# Check reads stats for rarefaction
```{r}
set.seed(42)

# Check your read depths first
summary(sample_sums(cycle18Sdat))
```

# Rarefy to sample with lowest reads
18S ARMS:
Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
  17869   34135   38700   38749   44074   55729 
```{r}
# Conservative - keep all samples
d_r17k <- rarefy_even_depth(cycle18Sdat, sample.size = 17869, rngseed =42)

# Check what we have after rarefaction
print("Before rarefaction:")
print(cycle18Sdat)

print("After rarefaction:")
print(d_r17k)

# Calculate what was removed
asvs_before <- ntaxa(cycle18Sdat)
asvs_after <- ntaxa(d_r17k)
asvs_removed <- asvs_before - asvs_after

cat("ASVs before rarefaction:", asvs_before, "\n")
cat("ASVs after rarefaction:", asvs_after, "\n") 
cat("ASVs removed:", asvs_removed, "\n")
cat("Percentage retained:", round(asvs_after/asvs_before * 100, 1), "%\n")

# Check which ASVs were removed (the rare ones)
removed_asvs <- setdiff(taxa_names(cycle18Sdat), taxa_names(d_r17k))
cat("First 10 removed ASVs:", head(removed_asvs, 10), "\n")

# Look at abundance of removed ASVs in original data
if(length(removed_asvs) > 0) {
  removed_abundances <- taxa_sums(cycle18Sdat)[removed_asvs]
  cat("Abundance summary of removed ASVs:\n")
  print(summary(removed_abundances))
}

# Export rarefied phyloseq object 'd_r17k'
save(d_r17k, file = "./d_r17k.Rdata")

#[1] "Before rarefaction:"
#phyloseq-class experiment-level object
#otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 7089 taxa and 114 samples ]
#sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 114 samples by 39 sample variables ]
#[1] "After rarefaction:"
#phyloseq-class experiment-level object
#otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 5852 taxa and 114 samples ]
#sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 114 samples by 39 sample variables ]
#ASVs before rarefaction: 7089 
#ASVs after rarefaction: 5852 
#ASVs removed: 1237 
#Percentage retained: 82.6 %
#First 10 removed ASVs: ASV_18235 ASV_19592 ASV_22131 ASV_22138 ASV_22175 ASV_22337 ASV_16488 ASV_22339 ASV_16413 ASV_18280 
#Abundance summary of removed ASVs:
#   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
# 0.0000  0.0000  0.0000  0.9968  1.0000 19.0000 
```

# ---------------------- DIVERSITY INDICIES -----------------

# 1: Calculate Diversity Indices from Rarefied Data
```{r}

# Extract ASV table (samples as rows, ASVs as columns)
asv_table_rarefied_18S <- as.data.frame(t(otu_table(d_r17k)))

# Verify dimensions
cat("Rarefied ASV table dimensions:", 
    nrow(asv_table_rarefied_18S), "x", ncol(asv_table_rarefied_18S), "\n")

# Create metadata dataframe from rarefied object
meta_dr17k <- as.data.frame(sample_data(d_r17k))

# Presence/absence transformation
presence_absence_rarefied_18S <- asv_table_rarefied_18S
presence_absence_rarefied_18S[presence_absence_rarefied_18S > 0] <- 1

# Sample codes
sampleCode <- rownames(meta_dr17k)

# Calculate diversity indices
relativeAbundance <- rowSums(asv_table_rarefied_18S)  # all should equal 17869
numberASVs <- rowSums(asv_table_rarefied_18S > 0)
Shannon <- vegan::diversity(asv_table_rarefied_18S, base = 2)
Simpson <- vegan::diversity(asv_table_rarefied_18S, "simpson")
Pielou <- Shannon / log(numberOTUs, base = 2)
Margalef <- (numberOTUs - 1) / log(relativeAbundance)

# Jaccard dissimilarity
jaccard_dist <- vegan::vegdist(presence_absence_rarefied_18S, method = "jaccard")
jaccard_mean <- apply(as.matrix(jaccard_dist), 1, mean, na.rm = TRUE)

# Build diversity dataframe
div_indices_rarefied <- data.frame(
  relativeAbundance = relativeAbundance,
  numberASVs = numberASVs,
  Shannon = Shannon,
  Simpson = Simpson,
  Pielou = Pielou,
  Margalef = Margalef,
  Jaccard_mean_dissim = jaccard_mean,
  row.names = sampleCode   # <--- ensures rownames = sample IDs
)

# Add Chao1 and ACE
richness_estimates_rarefied <- estimate_richness(d_r17k, measures = c("Chao1", "ACE"))
div_indices_rarefied$Chao1 <- richness_estimates_rarefied$Chao1
div_indices_rarefied$ACE <- richness_estimates_rarefied$ACE

# Remove existing columns, merge diversity indices into metadata
meta_dr17k$relativeAbundance <- NULL
meta_dr17k$numberASVs <- NULL
meta_dr17k <- cbind(meta_dr17k, div_indices_rarefied[rownames(meta_dr17k), ])


# Reassign metadata back to phyloseq object
sample_data(d_r17k) <- sample_data(meta_dr17k)

# Check that all objects have the same sample order
cat("Sample order consistency check:\n")
cat("d_r17k samples match meta_dr17k:", identical(sample_names(d_r17k), rownames(meta_dr17k)), "\n")
cat("d_r17k samples match ASV table:", identical(sample_names(d_r17k), rownames(asv_table_rarefied_18S)), "\n")
cat("d_r17k samples match diversity indices:", identical(sample_names(d_r17k), rownames(div_indices_rarefied)), "\n")

# Save s
write.csv(div_indices_rarefied, 
          "./Alpha_Div/18S_diversity_indices_rarefied.csv", 
          quote = FALSE, row.names = TRUE)

write.csv(presence_absence_rarefied_18S, 
          "./Alpha_Div/18S_presence_absence_rarefied.csv", 
          quote = FALSE, row.names = TRUE)

write.csv(asv_table_rarefied_18S, 
          "./Alpha_Div/18S_abundance_table_rarefied.csv", 
          quote = FALSE, row.names = TRUE)

save(meta_dr17k, file = "./meta_dr17k.Rdata")
```



# 2: Violin Plot by SITELOCALITY (using rarefied data)
Simpson's versus Shannon's 
```{r}
# Plot richness using RAREFIED data
rich <- plot_richness(d_r17k, x = "sitelocality",  # Using rarefied phyloseq object
                      measures = c("Shannon", "Simpson"))

# Customize the plot
rich$layers <- rich$layers[-1]  # Remove default points

rich <- rich + 
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_sites) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = sitelocality), color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = sitelocality), size = 1.5, width = 0.3, alpha = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 18, size = 3, color = "black") +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c12_sites, breaks = ordered_sites) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c12_sites, breaks = ordered_sites) +
  labs(title = "18S: Alpha Diversity by Site (Rarefied to 17,869 reads)", 
       fill = "Site", 
       color = "Site", 
       x = "Site") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = 'black', size = 11, angle = -90, vjust = 1, hjust = 0),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 15),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 15),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    strip.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 15),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

print(rich)

# Save plot
ggsave("18S_alphaDiv_Locality_violin_rarefied.pdf", plot = rich, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./Alpha_Div",
       scale = 1, width = 14, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```


# 3: Violin Plot by FRACTION (using rarefied data)
```{r}
# Define fraction colors
#c3_fractionss <- c("sessile" = "#3B8C6E",
#                  "500" = "#F68C30", 
#                  "100" = "#FEBC2C")

# Define order for fractions
ordered_fractions <- c("sessile", "500", "100")

# Plot richness by fraction using RAREFIED data
rich_fractions <- plot_richness(d_r17k, x = "fraction",  # Using rarefied phyloseq object
                                measures = c("Shannon", "Simpson"))

# Customize the plot
rich_fractions$layers <- rich_fractions$layers[-1]  # Remove default points

rich_fractions <- rich_fractions + 
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_fractions) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = fraction), color = "black", alpha = 0.7) + 
  geom_jitter(aes(color = fraction), size = 1.5, width = 0.3, alpha = 1) +  
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 18, size = 3, color = "black") +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c3_fractionss, breaks = ordered_fractions) +  
  scale_color_manual(values = c3_fractionss, breaks = ordered_fractions) + 
  labs(title = "18S: Alpha Diversity by Fraction (Rarefied to 17,869 reads)", 
       fill = "Fraction", 
       color = "Fraction", 
       x = "Fraction") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = 'black', size = 13, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 15),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 15),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    strip.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 15),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 13),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 15),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

print(rich_fractions)

# Save plot
ggsave("18S_alphaDiv_Fraction_violin_rarefied.pdf", plot = rich_fractions, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./Alpha_Div",
       scale = 1, width = 10, height = 7, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

# --------- ALPHA DIVERSITY ANALYSIS: INTEGRATED DIVERSITY INDICES ---------
Using rarefied data: meta_dr17k <- rarefy_even_depth(cycle18Sdat, sample.size = 17000, rngseed = 42)
Primary metrics: Shannon diversity and Simpson diversity indices (integrated measures)
Supplementary metrics: ASV richness and Pielou's evenness (mechanistic components)
Using mixed-effects models because samples are paired across size fractions within ARMS units

(1) Size fraction effects testing sessile vs motile hypothesis with planned contrasts
(2) Environmental gradient effects quantifying depth and turbidity influences 
(3) Spatial variation analysis across 12 sampling sites with conditional post-hoc comparisons

Conditional Post-hoc Site Comparisons:
Post-hoc tests following significant site main effects with family-wise error correction
Method: Tukey's Honestly Significant Difference test via emmeans
Degrees of Freedom: Kenward-Roger approximation
Conditional Logic: Pairwise comparisons performed only when main site effect p < 0.05
Purpose: Identify specific site pairs differing significantly in diversity
Content: Pairwise contrasts, effect sizes, standard errors, t-ratios, and adjusted p-values
Results: Shannon, Simpson, and Pielou's evenness showed significant site effects requiring post-hoc testing

Model Validation and Diagnostics:
Random Effects: ARMS unit as random intercept to account for paired sampling structure
Singular Fits: Richness models showed boundary singular fits (random effect variance ≈ 0)
Model Fit: Marginal R² calculated using MuMIn::r.squaredGLMM() for fixed effects explanatory power
Effect Sizes: Low marginal R² values (0.027-0.093) indicating weak environmental predictive power

# Data frame fixes and chekcs
Linear mixed-effects model with ASV richness and Pielou's evenness as response variables, size fraction (100µm, 500µm, sessile) as fixed effect, and ARMS unit as random effect.
```{r}
# Load libraries
# library(lme4)
# library(multcomp) 
# library(dplyr)
# library(emmeans)
# library(performance)
# library(18Sn)
# library(broom.mixed)
#library(car)
#library(gridExtra)
#library(MuMIn)

# Prepare data for model

# Add ARMS ID corrections to existing metadata (don't recreate it!)
meta_dr17k$ARMS_ID <- meta_dr17k$arms
meta_dr17k$ARMS_ID_corrected <- gsub("b$|c$", "", meta_dr17k$arms)

# Check the data structure (verification only)
print("Fraction distribution:")
print(table(meta_dr17k$fraction))
print("ARMS ID corrected vs fraction:")
print(table(meta_dr17k$ARMS_ID_corrected, meta_dr17k$fraction))

# Check for missing values in key columns
print("Missing values check:")
print(sapply(meta_dr17k[c("numberASVs", "Pielou", "fraction", "ARMS_ID_corrected")], 
             function(x) sum(is.na(x))))

# If you need a clean version without missing data, create it separately
# (but don't overwrite your main metadata!)
if(any(is.na(meta_dr17k[c("numberOTUs", "Pielou", "fraction", "ARMS_ID_corrected")]))) {
  metadata_dr17k_clean <- meta_dr17k[complete.cases(meta_dr17k[c("numberOTUs", "Pielou", "fraction", "ARMS_ID_corrected")]), ]
  cat("Rows removed due to missing data:", nrow(meta_dr17k) - nrow(metadata_dr17k_clean), "\n")
} else {
  metadata_dr17k_clean <- meta_dr17k
  cat("No missing data found\n")
}

# Check for duplicate column names
duplicate_cols <- duplicated(colnames(meta_dr17k))
cat("Duplicate columns found:", sum(duplicate_cols), "\n")
if(sum(duplicate_cols) > 0) {
  cat("Duplicate column names:\n")
  print(colnames(meta_dr17k)[duplicate_cols])
}
# If duplicate columns names in metadata
meta_dr17k <- meta_dr17k[, !duplicate_cols]
```


# Analysis 1: Size Fraction Effects
Model: Linear mixed-effects model with size fraction as fixed effect and ARMS unit as random effect
Formula: Diversity ~ fraction + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected)
Statistical Test: Type II Wald chi-square tests
Purpose: Test for differences in alpha diversity among size fractions (100µm, 500µm, sessile)
Primary Hypothesis: Planned contrast testing sessile vs motile fractions (no multiple comparison adjustment)
Content: Chi-square statistics, degrees of freedom, p-values, and planned contrast results
Metrics Analyzed: Shannon diversity, Simpson diversity (main text); ASV richness, Pielou's evenness (supplementary)
```{r}
print("=== ANALYSIS 1: FRACTION EFFECTS (4 METRICS) ===")

# Fit models for all diversity metrics
richness_model <- lmer(numberOTUs ~ fraction + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)
evenness_model <- lmer(Pielou ~ fraction + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)
shannon_model <- lmer(Shannon ~ fraction + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)
simpson_model <- lmer(Simpson ~ fraction + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)

# Get ANOVA results
rich_anova <- Anova(richness_model, type = "II")
even_anova <- Anova(evenness_model, type = "II")
shannon_anova <- Anova(shannon_model, type = "II")
simpson_anova <- Anova(simpson_model, type = "II")

print("Fraction effects - ANOVA results:")
print("Richness:"); print(rich_anova)
print("Evenness:"); print(even_anova)  
print("Shannon:"); print(shannon_anova)
print("Simpson:"); print(simpson_anova)

# Planned contrasts: Sessile vs Motile for all metrics
rich_emmeans <- emmeans(richness_model, ~ fraction)
even_emmeans <- emmeans(evenness_model, ~ fraction)
shannon_emmeans <- emmeans(shannon_model, ~ fraction)
simpson_emmeans <- emmeans(simpson_model, ~ fraction)

sessile_vs_motile <- list("Sessile vs Motile" = c(-0.5, -0.5, 1))

rich_contrast <- contrast(rich_emmeans, sessile_vs_motile, adjust = "none")
even_contrast <- contrast(even_emmeans, sessile_vs_motile, adjust = "none")
shannon_contrast <- contrast(shannon_emmeans, sessile_vs_motile, adjust = "none")
simpson_contrast <- contrast(simpson_emmeans, sessile_vs_motile, adjust = "none")

print("Planned contrasts - Sessile vs Motile:")
print("Richness:"); print(rich_contrast)
print("Evenness:"); print(even_contrast)
print("Shannon:"); print(shannon_contrast)
print("Simpson:"); print(simpson_contrast)

# Convert to data frames and create results table
rich_contrast_df <- as.data.frame(rich_contrast)
even_contrast_df <- as.data.frame(even_contrast)
shannon_contrast_df <- as.data.frame(shannon_contrast)
simpson_contrast_df <- as.data.frame(simpson_contrast)

analysis1_results <- data.frame(
  Response = c("ASV_Richness", "Pielous_Evenness", "Shannon_Diversity", "Simpson_Diversity"),
  Overall_Chisq = c(rich_anova["fraction", "Chisq"], even_anova["fraction", "Chisq"],
                   shannon_anova["fraction", "Chisq"], simpson_anova["fraction", "Chisq"]),
  Overall_p = c(rich_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], even_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"],
               shannon_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], simpson_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"]),
  Planned_Contrast_Estimate = c(rich_contrast_df$estimate, even_contrast_df$estimate,
                              shannon_contrast_df$estimate, simpson_contrast_df$estimate),
  Planned_Contrast_SE = c(rich_contrast_df$SE, even_contrast_df$SE,
                         shannon_contrast_df$SE, simpson_contrast_df$SE),
  Planned_Contrast_p = c(rich_contrast_df$p.value, even_contrast_df$p.value,
                        shannon_contrast_df$p.value, simpson_contrast_df$p.value),
  Interpretation = c(
    ifelse(rich_contrast_df$p.value < 0.05, "Sessile significantly different", "No fraction effect"),
    ifelse(even_contrast_df$p.value < 0.05, "Sessile significantly different", "No fraction effect"),
    ifelse(shannon_contrast_df$p.value < 0.05, "Sessile significantly different", "No fraction effect"),
    ifelse(simpson_contrast_df$p.value < 0.05, "Sessile significantly different", "No fraction effect")
  )
)

print("ANALYSIS 1 SUMMARY:")
print(analysis1_results)
```

#  Analysis 2: Enivironmental Gradients 
Model: Linear mixed-effects model with environmental predictors and ARMS unit as random effect
Formula: Diversity ~ fraction + depth + turbidity_std_rank + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected)
Statistical Test: Type II Wald chi-square tests
Purpose: Assess environmental gradient effects on metazoan alpha diversity while controlling for fraction effects
Content: Chi-square statistics, p-values, marginal R² values, and environmental relationship visualizations
Environmental Variables: Depth (continuous, meters) and turbidity (standardized rank)

```{r}
# Fit environmental models
rich_env <- lmer(numberOTUs ~ depth + turbidity_std_rank + fraction + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)
even_env <- lmer(Pielou ~ depth + turbidity_std_rank + fraction + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)
shannon_env <- lmer(Shannon ~ depth + turbidity_std_rank + fraction + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)
simpson_env <- lmer(Simpson ~ depth + turbidity_std_rank + fraction + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)

# ANOVA results
rich_env_anova <- Anova(rich_env, type = "II")
even_env_anova <- Anova(even_env, type = "II")
shannon_env_anova <- Anova(shannon_env, type = "II")
simpson_env_anova <- Anova(simpson_env, type = "II")

print("Environmental effects:")
print("Richness:"); print(rich_env_anova)
print("Evenness:"); print(even_env_anova)
print("Shannon:"); print(shannon_env_anova)
print("Simpson:"); print(simpson_env_anova)

# Model fit statistics
rich_r2 <- round(r.squaredGLMM(rich_env)[1], 3)
even_r2 <- round(r.squaredGLMM(even_env)[1], 3)
shannon_r2 <- round(r.squaredGLMM(shannon_env)[1], 3)
simpson_r2 <- round(r.squaredGLMM(simpson_env)[1], 3)

print(paste("Marginal R² - Richness:", rich_r2, "| Evenness:", even_r2, 
            "| Shannon:", shannon_r2, "| Simpson:", simpson_r2))

# Create results table
analysis2_results <- data.frame(
  Response = rep(c("ASV_Richness", "Pielous_Evenness", "Shannon_Diversity", "Simpson_Diversity"), each = 3),
  Variable = rep(c("depth", "turbidity_std_rank", "fraction"), 4),
  Chisq = c(rich_env_anova[1:3, "Chisq"], even_env_anova[1:3, "Chisq"],
           shannon_env_anova[1:3, "Chisq"], simpson_env_anova[1:3, "Chisq"]),
  p_value = c(rich_env_anova[1:3, "Pr(>Chisq)"], even_env_anova[1:3, "Pr(>Chisq)"],
             shannon_env_anova[1:3, "Pr(>Chisq)"], simpson_env_anova[1:3, "Pr(>Chisq)"]),
  Significant = c(rich_env_anova[1:3, "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05, even_env_anova[1:3, "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05,
                 shannon_env_anova[1:3, "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05, simpson_env_anova[1:3, "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05)
)

print("ANALYSIS 2 SUMMARY:")
print(analysis2_results)

# plot stats to earlier fraction violin plot
rich_fractions <- rich_fractions +
  # Add statistics annotation for each panel
  geom_text(data = data.frame(variable = c("Shannon", "Simpson"),
                              label = c("R² = 0.282, p < 0.001", "R² = 0.052, p = 0.069")),
            aes(x = Inf, y = Inf, label = label),
            hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.1, size = 4, color = "black",
            inherit.aes = FALSE)
```

# Only use this plotting chunk if environmental variables are significant
```{r}
# Only use this chunk if environmental variables are significant
# Plotting function
format_p <- function(p) {
  if (p < 0.001) return("p < 0.001")
  else if (p < 0.01) return(paste("p =", round(p, 3)))
  else return(paste("p =", round(p, 2)))
}
custom_theme <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, color = "black", face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# Create plots for all 4 metrics vs depth and turbidity (8 total plots)
create_env_plot <- function(data, x_var, y_var, y_label, title, model_r2, p_vals) {
  # Get the specific p-value for the x variable being plotted
  if(x_var == "depth") {
    specific_p <- p_vals[2]  # depth p-value
  } else if(x_var == "turbidity_std_rank") {
    specific_p <- p_vals[3]  # turbidity p-value
  }
  
  # Calculate R² for this specific relationship (simple correlation)
  cor_r2 <- round(cor(data[[x_var]], data[[y_var]], use = "complete.obs")^2, 3)
  
  p_text <- format_p(specific_p)
  
  ggplot(data, aes_string(x = x_var, y = y_var, color = "fraction")) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, aes(group = 1), color = "black", size = 0.6) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c3_fractionss, labels = c("100 µm", "500 µm", "Sessile")) +
    labs(title = title, x = ifelse(x_var == "depth", "Depth (m)", "Turbidity (rank)"), 
         y = y_label, color = "Size Fraction") +
    annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, 
             label = paste0("Model R² = ", model_r2, "\nCorr. R² = ", cor_r2, "\n", p_text),
             hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.1, size = 3.5, color = "black") +
    custom_theme
}

# Environmental relationship plots
p1 <- create_env_plot(meta_dr17k, "depth", "numberOTUs", "ASV Richness", 
                     "Richness vs Depth", rich_r2,
                     c(rich_env_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       rich_env_anova["depth", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       rich_env_anova["turbidity_std_rank", "Pr(>Chisq)"]))

p2 <- create_env_plot(meta_dr17k, "turbidity_std_rank", "numberOTUs", "ASV Richness", 
                     "Richness vs Turbidity", rich_r2,
                     c(rich_env_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       rich_env_anova["depth", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       rich_env_anova["turbidity_std_rank", "Pr(>Chisq)"]))

p3 <- create_env_plot(meta_dr17k, "depth", "Pielou", "Pielou's Evenness", 
                     "Evenness vs Depth", even_r2,
                     c(even_env_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       even_env_anova["depth", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       even_env_anova["turbidity_std_rank", "Pr(>Chisq)"]))

p4 <- create_env_plot(meta_dr17k, "turbidity_std_rank", "Pielou", "Pielou's Evenness", 
                     "Evenness vs Turbidity", even_r2,
                     c(even_env_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       even_env_anova["depth", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       even_env_anova["turbidity_std_rank", "Pr(>Chisq)"]))

p5 <- create_env_plot(meta_dr17k, "depth", "Shannon", "Shannon Diversity", 
                     "Shannon vs Depth", shannon_r2,
                     c(shannon_env_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       shannon_env_anova["depth", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       shannon_env_anova["turbidity_std_rank", "Pr(>Chisq)"]))

p6 <- create_env_plot(meta_dr17k, "turbidity_std_rank", "Shannon", "Shannon Diversity", 
                     "Shannon vs Turbidity", shannon_r2,
                     c(shannon_env_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       shannon_env_anova["depth", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       shannon_env_anova["turbidity_std_rank", "Pr(>Chisq)"]))

p7 <- create_env_plot(meta_dr17k, "depth", "Simpson", "Simpson Diversity", 
                     "Simpson vs Depth", simpson_r2,
                     c(simpson_env_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       simpson_env_anova["depth", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       simpson_env_anova["turbidity_std_rank", "Pr(>Chisq)"]))

p8 <- create_env_plot(meta_dr17k, "turbidity_std_rank", "Simpson", "Simpson Diversity", 
                     "Simpson vs Turbidity", simpson_r2,
                     c(simpson_env_anova["fraction", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       simpson_env_anova["depth", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
                       simpson_env_anova["turbidity_std_rank", "Pr(>Chisq)"]))

# Display plots in 4x2 grid
grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7, p8, ncol = 2)
```


#  Analysis 3: Site Effects 
Model: Linear mixed-effects model with sampling site as fixed effect and ARMS unit as random effect
Formula: Diversity ~ fraction + sitelocality + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected)
Statistical Test: Type II Wald chi-square tests
Purpose: Test for spatial heterogeneity in alpha diversity among 12 sampling locations
Content: Chi-square statistics, p-values for site and fraction effects, significance classification
```{r}
# Fit site models
rich_site <- lmer(numberOTUs ~ fraction + sitelocality + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)
even_site <- lmer(Pielou ~ fraction + sitelocality + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)
shannon_site <- lmer(Shannon ~ fraction + sitelocality + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)
simpson_site <- lmer(Simpson ~ fraction + sitelocality + (1|ARMS_ID_corrected), data = meta_dr17k)

# ANOVA results
rich_site_anova <- Anova(rich_site, type = "II")
even_site_anova <- Anova(even_site, type = "II")
shannon_site_anova <- Anova(shannon_site, type = "II")
simpson_site_anova <- Anova(simpson_site, type = "II")

print("Site effects:")
print("Richness:"); print(rich_site_anova)
print("Evenness:"); print(even_site_anova)
print("Shannon:"); print(shannon_site_anova)
print("Simpson:"); print(simpson_site_anova)

# Conditional post-hoc tests
site_contrasts_list <- list()

if(rich_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05) {
  print("Richness site comparisons (significant):")
  rich_site_pairs <- emmeans(rich_site, pairwise ~ sitelocality)
  site_contrasts_list[["richness"]] <- as.data.frame(rich_site_pairs$contrasts)
  print(rich_site_pairs$contrasts)
}

if(even_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05) {
  print("Evenness site comparisons (significant):")
  even_site_pairs <- emmeans(even_site, pairwise ~ sitelocality)
  site_contrasts_list[["evenness"]] <- as.data.frame(even_site_pairs$contrasts)
}

if(shannon_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05) {
  print("Shannon site comparisons (significant):")
  shannon_site_pairs <- emmeans(shannon_site, pairwise ~ sitelocality)
  site_contrasts_list[["shannon"]] <- as.data.frame(shannon_site_pairs$contrasts)
}

if(simpson_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05) {
  print("Simpson site comparisons (significant):")
  simpson_site_pairs <- emmeans(simpson_site, pairwise ~ sitelocality)
  site_contrasts_list[["simpson"]] <- as.data.frame(simpson_site_pairs$contrasts)
}

# Site effects summary
analysis3_results <- data.frame(
  Response = c("ASV_Richness", "Pielous_Evenness", "Shannon_Diversity", "Simpson_Diversity"),
  Site_Chisq = c(rich_site_anova["sitelocality", "Chisq"], even_site_anova["sitelocality", "Chisq"],
                shannon_site_anova["sitelocality", "Chisq"], simpson_site_anova["sitelocality", "Chisq"]),
  Site_p = c(rich_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"], even_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"],
            shannon_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"], simpson_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"]),
  Site_Effect = c(
    ifelse(rich_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant"),
    ifelse(even_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant"),
    ifelse(shannon_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant"),
    ifelse(simpson_site_anova["sitelocality", "Pr(>Chisq)"] < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant")
  )
)

print("ANALYSIS 3 SUMMARY:")
print(analysis3_results)
```


#  Export Results
```{r}
#dir.create("./Alpha_Div", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Export analysis results
write.csv(analysis1_results, "./Alpha_Div/18S_Analysis1_Fraction_Effects_4metrics.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(analysis2_results, "./Alpha_Div/18S_Analysis2_Environmental_Effects_4metrics.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(analysis3_results, "./Alpha_Div/18S_Analysis3_Site_Effects_4metrics.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Export site comparisons for significant metrics
for(metric in names(site_contrasts_list)) {
  contrasts_df <- site_contrasts_list[[metric]]
  contrasts_df$Response <- metric
  write.csv(contrasts_df, paste0("./Alpha_Div/18S_Site_Comparisons_", metric, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Export only significant comparisons
  sig_contrasts <- contrasts_df[contrasts_df$p.value < 0.05, ]
  write.csv(sig_contrasts, paste0("./Alpha_Div/18S_Significant_Site_Comparisons_", metric, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}

# Export plots
#ggsave("./Alpha_Div/18S_Environmental_Relationships_4metrics.pdf", 
#       arrangeGrob(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7, p8, ncol = 2), 
#       width = 8, height = 14, dpi = 300)

# Overall summary
print("\n=== OVERALL SUMMARY (4 METRICS) ===")
print("Fraction Effects:")
print(paste("Significant metrics:", paste(analysis1_results$Response[analysis1_results$Planned_Contrast_p < 0.05], collapse = ", ")))

print("Environmental Effects:")
sig_env_metrics <- unique(analysis2_results$Response[analysis2_results$Significant == TRUE])
print(paste("Responsive metrics:", ifelse(length(sig_env_metrics) > 0, paste(sig_env_metrics, collapse = ", "), "None")))

print("Site Effects:")
sig_site_metrics <- analysis3_results$Response[analysis3_results$Site_Effect == "Significant"]
print(paste("Significant metrics:", ifelse(length(sig_site_metrics) > 0, paste(sig_site_metrics, collapse = ", "), "None")))
```


# 18S-ARMS DESCRIPTIVE ALPHA DIVERSITY STATISTICS
# Generates mean ± SE summary tables for Table S2A supplement

Analyses included:
1. Calculate four diversity metrics for each sample:
   - Observed richness (number of ASVs)
   - Shannon diversity (H')
   - Simpson diversity
   - Pielou's evenness (J')
   
2. Summary statistics by size fraction:
   - Mean ± SE for all four metrics
   - Min/Max ranges
   - Sample sizes (N)
   
3. Summary statistics by site:
   - Mean ± SE for all four metrics
   - Sites ranked by richness, evenness, and Shannon diversity
   
4. Site × Fraction breakdown:
   - Richness and evenness for each site-fraction combination
   - Sample sizes per combination
   
5. Export sample-level data:
   - Full dataset for verification and transparency
   - Ready for supplementary materials

Generates mean ± SE tables for Table S2A supplement
```{r}
# ======= 18S-ARMS DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS FOR MANUSCRIPT
# Generates mean ± SE tables for Table S2A supplement =========

#library(phyloseq)
#library(dplyr)
#library(tidyr)
#library(vegan)

# Your phyloseq object
physeq <- d_r17k

# ======  FUNCTION: Calculate all diversity metrics from phyloseq object ======

calculate_diversity_metrics <- function(physeq) {
  # Get OTU table
  otu_tab <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq))
  if(taxa_are_rows(physeq)) {
    otu_tab <- t(otu_tab)
  }
  otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu_tab)
  
  # Calculate observed richness (number of ASVs per sample)
  observed_richness <- rowSums(otu_mat > 0)
  
  # Calculate Shannon diversity
  shannon <- vegan::diversity(otu_mat, index = "shannon")
  
  # Calculate Simpson diversity
  simpson <- vegan::diversity(otu_mat, index = "simpson")
  
  # Calculate Pielou's evenness
  pielou <- shannon / log(observed_richness)
  pielou[is.nan(pielou)] <- 0  # Handle cases where richness = 0 or 1
  
  # Create data frame
  diversity_df <- data.frame(
    sample_id = rownames(otu_mat),
    observed_richness = observed_richness,
    shannon = shannon,
    simpson = simpson,
    pielou = pielou,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  return(diversity_df)
}

# ========== FUNCTION: Calculate summary statistics by group =============

calc_summary_stats <- function(data, group_var, metric) {
  data %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(group_var))) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      Mean = round(mean(.data[[metric]], na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      SD = round(sd(.data[[metric]], na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      SE = round(sd(.data[[metric]], na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()), 1),
      Min = round(min(.data[[metric]], na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      Max = round(max(.data[[metric]], na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
}

# ============ CALCULATE DIVERSITY METRICS ==============

cat("=== CALCULATING 18S-ARMS DIVERSITY METRICS ===\n\n")

# Calculate diversity metrics for all samples
diversity_18S <- calculate_diversity_metrics(physeq)

# Get metadata
metadata_18S <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq))

# Merge diversity metrics with metadata
full_data_18S <- merge(metadata_18S, diversity_18S, 
                       by.x = "row.names", by.y = "sample_id")
rownames(full_data_18S) <- full_data_18S$Row.names
full_data_18S$Row.names <- NULL

cat("Total samples:", nrow(full_data_18S), "\n")
cat("Total ASVs:", ntaxa(physeq), "\n\n")

# ========== TABLE 1: DIVERSITY BY FRACTION ==============

cat("=== TABLE S2A SUPPLEMENT: DIVERSITY BY FRACTION ===\n\n")

# Calculate stats for each metric
fraction_richness_18S <- calc_summary_stats(full_data_18S, "fraction", "observed_richness")
fraction_evenness_18S <- calc_summary_stats(full_data_18S, "fraction", "pielou")
fraction_shannon_18S <- calc_summary_stats(full_data_18S, "fraction", "shannon")
fraction_simpson_18S <- calc_summary_stats(full_data_18S, "fraction", "simpson")

# Combine into single comprehensive table
fraction_stats_18S <- fraction_richness_18S %>%
  rename(Richness_Mean = Mean, Richness_SD = SD, Richness_SE = SE,
         Richness_Min = Min, Richness_Max = Max) %>%
  select(fraction, N, Richness_Mean, Richness_SD, Richness_SE, 
         Richness_Min, Richness_Max) %>%
  left_join(
    fraction_evenness_18S %>% 
      select(fraction, Mean, SD, SE, Min, Max) %>% 
      rename(Evenness_Mean = Mean, Evenness_SD = SD, Evenness_SE = SE,
             Evenness_Min = Min, Evenness_Max = Max),
    by = "fraction"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    fraction_shannon_18S %>% 
      select(fraction, Mean, SD, SE, Min, Max) %>% 
      rename(Shannon_Mean = Mean, Shannon_SD = SD, Shannon_SE = SE,
             Shannon_Min = Min, Shannon_Max = Max),
    by = "fraction"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    fraction_simpson_18S %>% 
      select(fraction, Mean, SD, SE, Min, Max) %>% 
      rename(Simpson_Mean = Mean, Simpson_SD = SD, Simpson_SE = SE,
             Simpson_Min = Min, Simpson_Max = Max),
    by = "fraction"
  )

# Rename fractions for clarity
fraction_stats_18S$fraction <- dplyr::recode(fraction_stats_18S$fraction,
                                      "sessile" = "Sessile",
                                      "100" = "100µm",
                                      "500" = "500µm")

print(fraction_stats_18S)
cat("\n")

# ========= TABLE 2: DIVERSITY BY SITE ==================

cat("=== TABLE: DIVERSITY BY SITE ===\n\n")

site_richness_18S <- calc_summary_stats(full_data_18S, "sitelocality", "observed_richness") %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean))

site_evenness_18S <- calc_summary_stats(full_data_18S, "sitelocality", "pielou") %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean))

site_shannon_18S <- calc_summary_stats(full_data_18S, "sitelocality", "shannon") %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean))

print(site_richness_18S)
cat("\n")

# ============ TABLE 3: SITE × FRACTION BREAKDOWN =============

cat("=== TABLE: SITE × FRACTION RICHNESS ===\n\n")

site_fraction_18S <- full_data_18S %>%
  group_by(sitelocality, fraction) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    Richness_Mean = round(mean(observed_richness, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Richness_SE = round(sd(observed_richness, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()), 1),
    Evenness_Mean = round(mean(pielou, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    Evenness_SE = round(sd(pielou, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()), 3),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(sitelocality, fraction)

print(site_fraction_18S)
cat("\n")

# ========== EXPORT TABLES ============

dir.create("./Alpha_Diversity_Tables", showWarnings = FALSE)

write.csv(fraction_stats_18S, 
          "./Alpha_Diversity_Tables/TableS2A_Supplement_18S_Fraction_Stats.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(site_richness_18S, 
          "./Alpha_Diversity_Tables/TableS2_18S_Site_Richness.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(site_evenness_18S, 
          "./Alpha_Diversity_Tables/TableS2_18S_Site_Evenness.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(site_shannon_18S, 
          "./Alpha_Diversity_Tables/TableS2_18S_Site_Shannon.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(site_fraction_18S, 
          "./Alpha_Diversity_Tables/TableS2_18S_Site_Fraction_Detail.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)

# Also export the full sample-level data for transparency
write.csv(full_data_18S %>% 
            select(sitelocality, fraction, observed_richness, pielou, shannon, simpson), 
          "./Alpha_Diversity_Tables/TableS2_18S_Sample_Level_Data.csv", 
          row.names = TRUE)

cat("\n=== TABLES EXPORTED ===\n")
cat("Files created in ./Alpha_Diversity_Tables/:\n")
cat("  - TableS2A_Supplement_18S_Fraction_Stats.csv (USE THIS FOR TEXT)\n")
cat("  - TableS2_18S_Site_Richness.csv\n")
cat("  - TableS2_18S_Site_Evenness.csv\n")
cat("  - TableS2_18S_Site_Shannon.csv\n")
cat("  - TableS2_18S_Site_Fraction_Detail.csv\n")
cat("  - TableS2_18S_Sample_Level_Data.csv\n\n")
```



============================================================================
---------------------------- 4th Root Transformation -----------------------
============================================================================
```{r}
# Clean data
# Check if there are ASVs with zero abundance across all samples
biotic_sums_18S <- rowSums(biotic_18S)
empty_asvs <- sum(biotic_sums_18S == 0)  # Fixed: was biotic_sums
cat("Number of empty ASVs:", empty_asvs, "\n") 

# If there are empty ASVs, remove them
if(empty_asvs > 0) {
  keep_asvs <- biotic_sums_18S > 0
  biotic_clean_18S <- biotic_18S[keep_asvs, ]  # Fixed: removed double suffix
  cat("ASVs removed:", sum(!keep_asvs), "\n")
  cat("Final ASV count:", nrow(biotic_clean_18S), "\n")
} else {
  biotic_clean_18S <- biotic_18S
  cat("No empty ASVs found - data is clean!\n")
}

# Convert cleaned counts to proportions
biotic_percs_18S <- sweep(biotic_clean_18S, 2, colSums(biotic_clean_18S), "/")

# Transpose for analysis (samples as rows)
if(nrow(biotic_percs_18S) > ncol(biotic_percs_18S)) {  # if ASVs are rows
  biotic_percs_18S_t <- t(biotic_percs_18S)
} else {
  biotic_percs_18S_t <- biotic_percs_18S
}

# Apply 4th root transformation 
biotic_percs_18S_4th <- biotic_percs_18S_t^0.25

# Verify everything looks good
cat("Proportions table dimensions:", paste(dim(biotic_percs_18S_t), collapse = " x "), "\n")
cat("Sample sums (should all be 1):", range(rowSums(biotic_percs_18S_t)), "\n")
cat("4th root range:", range(biotic_percs_18S_4th), "\n")

# Create 4th root transformed phyloseq object
if(ncol(biotic_percs_18S_4th) > nrow(biotic_percs_18S_4th)) {
  cat("Data appears to be Samples x ASVs - transposing...\n")
  biotic_percs_18S_4th <- t(biotic_percs_18S_4th)
}

# Create 4th-root transformed NON-rarefied phyloseq object
otu <- otu_table(biotic_percs_18S_4th, taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
samp <-sample_data(metadata_ARMS)
cycle18Sdat_4th <- phyloseq(otu, samp)

#Create 4th-root transformed rarefied phyloseq object

d_r17k_4th <- d_r17k  # Copy the phyloseq object

# Apply 4th root transformation to the OTU table
otu_table(d_r17k_4th) <- (otu_table(d_r17k))^(1/4)

# Verify the transformation worked
print("Original d_r17k:")
print(d_r17k)
print("Transformed d_r17k_4th:")  
print(d_r17k_4th)

# Check that read counts changed 
print("Original read count range:")
print(range(otu_table(d_r17k)))
print("4th root transformed read count range:")
print(range(otu_table(d_r17k_4th)))

# Verify sample sums changed
print("Original sample sums (should all be 17869):")
print(head(sample_sums(d_r17k)))
print("Transformed sample sums (should be much smaller):")
print(head(sample_sums(d_r17k_4th)))
```

# ----------------- Robust 4th Root Transformation -----------------
```{r}
# Clean data - remove empty ASVs
biotic_sums_18S <- rowSums(biotic_18S)
empty_asvs <- sum(biotic_sums_18S == 0)
cat("Number of empty ASVs:", empty_asvs, "\n") 

if(empty_asvs > 0) {
  keep_asvs <- biotic_sums_18S > 0
  biotic_clean_18S <- biotic_18S[keep_asvs, ]
  cat("ASVs removed:", sum(!keep_asvs), "\n")
  cat("Final ASV count:", nrow(biotic_clean_18S), "\n")
} else {
  biotic_clean_18S <- biotic_18S
  cat("No empty ASVs found - data is clean!\n")
}

# Convert to proportions
biotic_percs_18S <- sweep(biotic_clean_18S, 2, colSums(biotic_clean_18S), "/")

# Robust orientation check using actual sample names
sample_names_ref <- sample_names(cycle18Sdat)
if(all(rownames(biotic_percs_18S) %in% sample_names_ref)) {
  # Samples are rows - no transpose needed
  biotic_percs_18S_t <- biotic_percs_18S
  cat("Data orientation: Samples as rows\n")
} else if(all(colnames(biotic_percs_18S) %in% sample_names_ref)) {
  # ASVs are rows - transpose needed  
  biotic_percs_18S_t <- t(biotic_percs_18S)
  cat("Data orientation: ASVs as rows, transposing to samples as rows\n")
} else {
  stop("ERROR: Cannot determine data orientation - sample names don't match!")
}

# Apply 4th root transformation
biotic_percs_18S_4th <- biotic_percs_18S_t^0.25

# Verification checks
cat("Proportions table dimensions:", paste(dim(biotic_percs_18S_t), collapse = " x "), "\n")
cat("Sample sums (should all be 1):", range(rowSums(biotic_percs_18S_t)), "\n")
cat("4th root range:", range(biotic_percs_18S_4th), "\n")

# Ensure consistent orientation for phyloseq (ASVs as rows)
if(all(rownames(biotic_percs_18S_4th) %in% sample_names_ref)) {
  # Need to transpose - samples are currently rows
  biotic_percs_18S_4th_phyloseq <- t(biotic_percs_18S_4th)
  cat("Transposing for phyloseq: ASVs as rows\n")
} else {
  # Already correct orientation
  biotic_percs_18S_4th_phyloseq <- biotic_percs_18S_4th
  cat("Phyloseq orientation: ASVs already as rows\n")
}

# Create phyloseq objects with CONSISTENT metadata
# Use the same metadata source for both objects
meta_consistent <- meta_dr17k  # Use rarefied metadata as reference

# Non-rarefied 4th root transformed
otu_4th <- otu_table(biotic_percs_18S_4th_phyloseq, taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
samp_4th <- sample_data(meta_consistent)
cycle18Sdat_4th <- phyloseq(otu_4th, samp_4th)

# Rarefied 4th root transformed  
d_r17k_4th <- d_r17k
otu_table(d_r17k_4th) <- (otu_table(d_r17k))^(1/4)

# Final verification
cat("\n=== VERIFICATION ===\n")
cat("Sample order consistency:\n")
cat("Non-rarefied 4th root matches reference:", 
    identical(sample_names(cycle18Sdat_4th), sample_names(d_r17k)), "\n")
cat("Rarefied 4th root matches reference:", 
    identical(sample_names(d_r17k_4th), sample_names(d_r17k)), "\n")

cat("\nTransformation verification:\n")
cat("Original rarefied range:", range(otu_table(d_r17k)), "\n")
cat("4th root rarefied range:", range(otu_table(d_r17k_4th)), "\n")
cat("Expected max (17869^0.25):", 17869^0.25, "\n")
```

# -------------------- Create 4th root data subsets -------------------- FINAL
```{r}
# Get sample names for each fraction
samples18S_100 <- rownames(meta_consistent)[meta_consistent$fraction == "100"]
samples18S_500 <- rownames(meta_consistent)[meta_consistent$fraction == "500"] 
samples18S_sessile <- rownames(meta_consistent)[meta_consistent$fraction == "sessile"]
samples18S_motile <- c(samples18S_100, samples18S_500)

# Confirm sample lists
cat("Sample counts by fraction:\n")
cat("100 micron:", length(samples18S_100), "samples\n")
cat("500 micron:", length(samples18S_500), "samples\n") 
cat("Sessile:", length(samples18S_sessile), "samples\n")
cat("Motile (combined):", length(samples18S_motile), "samples\n")

# Create subsetted RAW COUNT matrices (ASVs as rows, samples as columns)
biotic_100 <- biotic_clean_18S[, samples18S_100]
biotic_500 <- biotic_clean_18S[, samples18S_500]
biotic_sessile <- biotic_clean_18S[, samples18S_sessile] 
biotic_motile <- biotic_clean_18S[, samples18S_motile]

# Remove ASVs absent in ALL samples of each subset (BEFORE transposing)
biotic_100 <- biotic_100[rowSums(biotic_100) > 0, ]
biotic_500 <- biotic_500[rowSums(biotic_500) > 0, ]
biotic_sessile <- biotic_sessile[rowSums(biotic_sessile) > 0, ]
biotic_motile <- biotic_motile[rowSums(biotic_motile) > 0, ]

cat("ASV counts after cleaning:\n")
cat("100 micron:", nrow(biotic_100), "ASVs\n")
cat("500 micron:", nrow(biotic_500), "ASVs\n")
cat("Sessile:", nrow(biotic_sessile), "ASVs\n") 
cat("Motile:", nrow(biotic_motile), "ASVs\n")

# Calculate relative abundance WITHIN each fraction (column-wise)
biotic_percs_18S_100 <- sweep(biotic_100, 2, colSums(biotic_100), "/")
biotic_percs_18S_500 <- sweep(biotic_500, 2, colSums(biotic_500), "/")
biotic_percs_18S_sessile <- sweep(biotic_sessile, 2, colSums(biotic_sessile), "/")
biotic_percs_18S_motile <- sweep(biotic_motile, 2, colSums(biotic_motile), "/")

# Apply 4th root transformation (keeping ASVs as rows)
biotic_percs_18S_100_4th <- biotic_percs_18S_100^0.25
biotic_percs_18S_500_4th <- biotic_percs_18S_500^0.25
biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th <- biotic_percs_18S_sessile^0.25
biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th <- biotic_percs_18S_motile^0.25

# Create presence/absence matrices for each fraction
biotic_pa_100 <- biotic_100
biotic_pa_100[biotic_pa_100 > 0] <- 1

biotic_pa_500 <- biotic_500  
biotic_pa_500[biotic_pa_500 > 0] <- 1

biotic_pa_sessile <- biotic_sessile
biotic_pa_sessile[biotic_pa_sessile > 0] <- 1

biotic_pa_motile <- biotic_motile
biotic_pa_motile[biotic_pa_motile > 0] <- 1

# Create corresponding metadata subsets
meta_100 <- meta_consistent[samples18S_100, ]
meta_500 <- meta_consistent[samples18S_500, ]
meta_sessile <- meta_consistent[samples18S_sessile, ]
meta_motile <- meta_consistent[samples18S_motile, ]

# Verification checks
cat("\nVerification:\n")
cat("Sample order consistency:\n")
cat("100μm samples match metadata:", identical(colnames(biotic_100), rownames(meta_100)), "\n")
cat("500μm samples match metadata:", identical(colnames(biotic_500), rownames(meta_500)), "\n")
cat("Sessile samples match metadata:", identical(colnames(biotic_sessile), rownames(meta_sessile)), "\n")
cat("Motile samples match metadata:", identical(colnames(biotic_motile), rownames(meta_motile)), "\n")

cat("\nRelative abundance verification (should all be 1):\n")
cat("100μm:", range(colSums(biotic_percs_18S_100)), "\n")
cat("500μm:", range(colSums(biotic_percs_18S_500)), "\n") 
cat("Sessile:", range(colSums(biotic_percs_18S_sessile)), "\n")
cat("Motile:", range(colSums(biotic_percs_18S_motile)), "\n")

# Calculate mean ASV richness per sample for each fraction
richness_100 <- colSums(biotic_100 > 0)
richness_500 <- colSums(biotic_500 > 0) 
richness_sessile <- colSums(biotic_sessile > 0)
richness_motile <- colSums(biotic_motile > 0)

cat("\nMean ASV richness per sample:\n")
cat("100μm: mean =", round(mean(richness_100), 1), "± SD =", round(sd(richness_100), 1), 
    "(range:", range(richness_100), ")\n")
cat("500μm: mean =", round(mean(richness_500), 1), "± SD =", round(sd(richness_500), 1),
    "(range:", range(richness_500), ")\n")
cat("Sessile: mean =", round(mean(richness_sessile), 1), "± SD =", round(sd(richness_sessile), 1),
    "(range:", range(richness_sessile), ")\n")
cat("Motile: mean =", round(mean(richness_motile), 1), "± SD =", round(sd(richness_motile), 1),
    "(range:", range(richness_motile), ")\n")

# Calculate unique ASVs in each fraction  
unique_asvs_100 <- sum(rowSums(biotic_100) > 0)
unique_asvs_500 <- sum(rowSums(biotic_500) > 0)
unique_asvs_sessile <- sum(rowSums(biotic_sessile) > 0)
unique_asvs_motile <- sum(rowSums(biotic_motile) > 0)

cat("\nUnique ASVs per fraction:\n")
cat("100μm:", unique_asvs_100, "unique ASVs\n")
cat("500μm:", unique_asvs_500, "unique ASVs\n") 
cat("Sessile:", unique_asvs_sessile, "unique ASVs\n")
cat("Motile:", unique_asvs_motile, "unique ASVs\n")

# Calculate fraction-specific ASVs (found ONLY in that fraction)
all_fractions <- list(
  "100" = rownames(biotic_100)[rowSums(biotic_100) > 0],
  "500" = rownames(biotic_500)[rowSums(biotic_500) > 0], 
  "sessile" = rownames(biotic_sessile)[rowSums(biotic_sessile) > 0]
)

# ASVs exclusive to each fraction
exclusive_100 <- setdiff(all_fractions[["100"]], c(all_fractions[["500"]], all_fractions[["sessile"]]))
exclusive_500 <- setdiff(all_fractions[["500"]], c(all_fractions[["100"]], all_fractions[["sessile"]]))
exclusive_sessile <- setdiff(all_fractions[["sessile"]], c(all_fractions[["100"]], all_fractions[["500"]]))

# ASVs shared between fractions
shared_all <- Reduce(intersect, all_fractions)
shared_motile <- intersect(all_fractions[["100"]], all_fractions[["500"]])

cat("\nASV sharing patterns:\n")
cat("Exclusive to 100μm:", length(exclusive_100), "ASVs\n")
cat("Exclusive to 500μm:", length(exclusive_500), "ASVs\n")
cat("Exclusive to sessile:", length(exclusive_sessile), "ASVs\n") 
cat("Shared between 100μm + 500μm only:", length(setdiff(shared_motile, all_fractions[["sessile"]])), "ASVs\n")
cat("Shared across all 3 fractions:", length(shared_all), "ASVs\n")
cat("Total unique ASVs across all fractions:", length(unique(unlist(all_fractions))), "ASVs\n")

cat("\nFinal dimensions:\n")
cat("100μm - ASVs:", nrow(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th), "Samples:", ncol(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th), "\n")
cat("500μm - ASVs:", nrow(biotic_percs_18S_500_4th), "Samples:", ncol(biotic_percs_18S_500_4th), "\n")
cat("Sessile - ASVs:", nrow(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th), "Samples:", ncol(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th), "\n")
cat("Motile - ASVs:", nrow(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th), "Samples:", ncol(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th), "\n")

# Save with corrected names and defined objects only
save(
  # Sample name vectors
  samples18S_100, samples18S_500, samples18S_sessile, samples18S_motile,
  
  # Raw count matrices by fraction (cleaned)
  biotic_100, biotic_500, biotic_sessile, biotic_motile,
  
  # 4th root transformed matrices by fraction
  biotic_percs_18S_100_4th, biotic_percs_18S_500_4th, 
  biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th, biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th,
  
  # Relative abundance matrices by fraction  
  biotic_percs_18S_100, biotic_percs_18S_500, 
  biotic_percs_18S_sessile, biotic_percs_18S_motile,
  
  # Presence/absence matrices by fraction
  biotic_pa_100, biotic_pa_500, biotic_pa_sessile, biotic_pa_motile,
  
  # Metadata subsets by fraction
  meta_100, meta_500, meta_sessile, meta_motile,
  
  file = "./18S_all_data_subsets.RData"
)

# Export individual objects for downstream analyses
save(biotic_clean_18S, file = "./biotic_clean_18S.RData")
save(biotic_percs_18S_4th, file = "./biotic_percs_18S_4th.RData")
save(meta_consistent, file = "./metadata_18S_FINAL.RData")
write.csv(meta_consistent, file = "./metadata_18S_FINAL.csv", row.names = TRUE)
```


# Ensure sample order matches
#sample_order_check <- identical(rownames(nmds_scores), rownames(meta_dr17k))
#cat("Sample orders match:", sample_order_check, "\n")

# If they don't match, align them:
#nmds_scores$sitelocality <- meta_dr17k[rownames(nmds_scores), "sitelocality"]  
#nmds_scores$fraction <- meta_dr17k[rownames(nmds_scores), "fraction"]


#----------------NMDS Bray-Curtis of 4th Root trans. ----------------
#4th Root proportion transformation NMDS by SITE LOCALITY
```{r}
# Ordinate Bray-Curtis distances on rarefied data
nmdsBray_r17k_4th <- ordinate(d_r17k_4th, method = "NMDS", distance = "bray",
                          k = 2, trymax = 1000)

# look at stress value for plot
print(nmdsBray_r17k_4th)

# plot
oP <- plot_ordination(d_r17k_4th, nmdsBray_r17k_4th)
oP

# Extract NMDS scores
nmds_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(nmdsBray_r17k_4th, display = "sites"))

# Remove original points FIRST (before any flipping)
oP$layers <- oP$layers[-1]

# Add metadata with proper indexing to ensure alignment
nmds_scores$sitelocality <- meta_dr17k[rownames(nmds_scores), "sitelocality"]  
nmds_scores$fraction <- meta_dr17k[rownames(nmds_scores), "fraction"]

# Flip ONLY the extracted scores (not the plot data you already removed)
#nmds_scores$NMDS1 <- -nmds_scores$NMDS1

# Set the correct order for sitelocality
nmds_scores$sitelocality <- factor(nmds_scores$sitelocality, 
                                   levels = c("Stetson_shallow", "EFGB_shallow", "EFGB_deep", 
                                              "Bright_shallow","Bright_deep1", "Bright_deep2",
                                              "McGrail_deep", "Alderdice_shallow", "Alderdice_deep1", 
                                              "Alderdice_deep2","Diaphus_deep1", "Diaphus_deep2"))


# Function to calculate convex hull
find_hull <- function(df) df[chull(df$NMDS1, df$NMDS2), ]

# Calculate convex hulls for each site locality value
hulls <- nmds_scores %>% 
  group_by(sitelocality) %>% 
  do(find_hull(.))

# Add new point layer and other plot modifications
oP <- oP + 
  geom_point(data = nmds_scores, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, fill = sitelocality, shape = fraction), size = 4) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22, 23)) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c12_sites) + 
  labs(title = "18S 4th Root Transformed: Bray NMDS", fill = "Sitelocality", shape = "Fraction") + 
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.box.just = "left",
        legend.margin = margin(t = 10), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 26, hjust = 0.5),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 24),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Site", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, ncol = 2, order = 2,override.aes = list(shape = 21)),
    shape = guide_legend(title = "Fraction", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, ncol=1, order = 1, override.aes = list(fill = "black")))+ 
  geom_polygon(data = hulls, 
               aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = sitelocality, fill = sitelocality), 
               alpha = 0.4,  
               color = NA) +
  annotate('text', x = 1.0, y = -0.9, label = paste('stress =', round(nmdsBray_r17k_4th$stress, 3)), size = 6) # display stress value

# Print the final plot
print(oP)

# Create directoy
# dir.create("./NMDS", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Save plot
ggsave("18S_NMDS_Full_siteloc2.pdf", plot = oP, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./NMDS",
      scale = 1, width = 8.5, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```


# NMDS PLOT Bray-Curtis Dissimilarity of 4th Rooth Transformed by DEPTH
```{r}
oP_depth <- plot_ordination(d_r17k_4th, nmdsBray_r17k_4th)
oP_depth$layers <- oP_depth$layers[-1]
oP_depth$data$NMDS2 <- -oP_depth$data$NMDS2

# Create new scores for depth (with same flipping)
nmds_scores_depth <- as.data.frame(scores(nmdsBray_r17k_4th, display = "sites"))
nmds_scores_depth$NMDS2 <- -nmds_scores_depth$NMDS2
nmds_scores_depth <- nmds_scores_depth[match(rownames(meta_dr17k), rownames(nmds_scores_depth)), ]
nmds_scores_depth$depth <- meta_dr17k$depth  
nmds_scores_depth$fraction <- meta_dr17k$fraction

# Flip
# nmds_scores_depth$NMDS1 <- -nmds_scores_depth$NMDS1

# Calculate hulls for temperature
hulls_depth <- nmds_scores_depth %>% 
  group_by(depth) %>% 
  do(find_hull(.))

# Build temperature plot
oP_depth <- oP_depth + 
  geom_point(data = nmds_scores_depth, aes(fill = depth, shape = fraction), size = 4) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22, 23)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", name = "Mean Temperature", guide = "colorbar", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "18S 4th Root Transformed: Bray-Curtis NMDS", fill = "Depth (m)", shape = "Fraction") + 
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.justification = "left",
        legend.margin = margin(t = 10), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 26),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 24),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(30, "pt"),   
        legend.box.spacing = unit(20, "pt"),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Depth (m)", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, order = 1, barwidth = 20, barheight = 1.5),
         shape = guide_legend(title = "Fraction", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, ncol = 1, order = 2, override.aes = list(fill = "black"))) + 
  geom_polygon(data = hulls_depth, 
               aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = depth, fill = depth), 
               alpha = 0.5,  
               color = NA) +
  annotate('text', x = 1.0, y = -0.9, label = paste('stress =', round(nmdsBray_r17k_4th$stress, 3)), size = 6)

# View Plot
print(oP_depth)

# Save
ggsave("18S_NMDS_4th_DEPTH.pdf", plot = oP_depth, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./NMDS",
      scale = 1, width = 8.5, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

#NMDS 4th Root Transformed - colored by TURBIDITY
colored by standard turbidity rank value
```{r}
# Start from existing plot base (reuse the ordination and flipping)
oP_turb <- plot_ordination(d_r17k_4th, nmdsBray_r17k_4th)  # Use same ordination object
oP_turb$layers <- oP_turb$layers[-1]  # Remove point layer


# Create new scores for turbidity (with same flipping)
nmds_scores_turb <- as.data.frame(scores(nmdsBray_r17k_4th, display = "sites"))
nmds_scores_turb$NMDS2 <- -nmds_scores_turb$NMDS2  # Apply same flip
nmds_scores_turb <- nmds_scores_turb[match(rownames(meta_dr17k), rownames(nmds_scores_turb)), ]
nmds_scores_turb$turbidity_std_rank <- meta_dr17k$turbidity_std_rank  
nmds_scores_turb$fraction <- meta_dr17k$fraction 

# Apply the same flip to match previous plot by site locality
# nmds_scores_turb$NMDS1 <- -nmds_scores_turb$NMDS1

# Calculate hulls for turbidity
hulls_turb <- nmds_scores_turb %>% 
  group_by(turbidity_std_rank) %>% 
  do(find_hull(.))

# Build the plot
oP_turb <- oP_turb + 
  geom_point(data = nmds_scores_turb, aes(fill = turbidity_std_rank, shape = fraction), size = 4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22, 23)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis") +
  labs(title = "18S 4th Root Transformed: Bray NMDS", fill = "Mean Visibility Rank", shape = "Fraction") + 
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.box.just = "left",
        legend.margin = margin(t = 10), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 26, hjust = 0.5),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 24),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(30, "pt"),   
        legend.box.spacing = unit(20, "pt"),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Mean Visibility Rank", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, order = 1, barwidth = 20, barheight = 1.5),
         shape = guide_legend(title = "Fraction", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, ncol = 1, order = 2, override.aes = list(fill = "black"))) + 
  geom_polygon(data = hulls_turb, 
               aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = turbidity_std_rank, fill = turbidity_std_rank), 
               alpha = 0.3,  
               color = NA) +
  annotate('text', x = 1.0, y = -0.7, label = paste('stress =', round(nmdsBray_r17k_4th$stress, 3)), size = 6)

print(oP_turb)

# Save plot
ggsave("18S_NMDS_Full_4th_turb.pdf", plot = oP_turb, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./NMDS",
      scale = 1, width = 8.5, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

# NMDS - PLOT ALL THREE IN ONE
```{r}
#library(patchwork)

# Create modified versions without certain legends
# Site locality plot - keep both legends (this will be our reference)
oP_site <- oP

# Depth plot - remove Fraction legend, keep only Depth
oP_depth_clean <- oP_depth + 
  guides(shape = "none",  # Remove fraction legend
         fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Depth (meters)", 
                              title.position = "top", 
                              title.hjust = 0.5, 
                              order = 1, 
                              barwidth = 20, 
                              barheight = 3))

# Turbidity plot - remove Fraction legend, keep only Mean Visibility Rank  
oP_turb_clean <- oP_turb + 
  guides(shape = "none",  # Remove fraction legend
         fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Mean Visibility Rank", 
                              title.position = "top", 
                              title.hjust = 0.5, 
                              order = 1, 
                              barwidth = 20, 
                              barheight = 3))

# Combine plots horizontally
combined_plot <- oP_site + oP_depth_clean + oP_turb_clean

# Add overall title and panel labels
combined_plot <- combined_plot + 
  plot_annotation(
    title = "18S 4th Root Transformed: Bray-Curtis Dissimilarity NMDS",
    tag_levels = 'A',  # Adds A, B, C labels to each plot
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 28, hjust = 0.5))
  )

# Print the combined plot
print(combined_plot)

# Save the combined plot with appropriate dimensions
ggsave("18S_NMDS_Combined.pdf", 
       plot = combined_plot, 
       device = "pdf", 
       path = "./NMDS",
       scale = 1, 
       width = 24,      # Wide enough for 3 plots side by side
       height = 10,     # Same height as individual plots
       units = "in", 
       dpi = 300)
```


#-------------------------- NMDS P/A JACCARD -------------------------------------

# Create new phyloseq data using presence absence data
presence_absence_rarefied_18S = rarefied p/a data to 17k reads, columns are ASV IDs
```{r}
otu_pa <- otu_table(presence_absence_rarefied_18S, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
samp <-sample_data(meta_dr17k)
cycle18Sdat_PA <- phyloseq(otu_pa, samp)
```

# Calculate NMDS values with Jaccard dissimilarity on presence/absence data
```{r}
# NMDS with Jaccard dissimilarity on presence/absence data
nmdsJaccard_PA <- ordinate(cycle18Sdat_PA, method = "NMDS", distance = "jaccard",
                          k = 2, trymax = 1000)

# Look at stress value for plot
print(nmdsJaccard_PA)

# stress = 0.2094142 
```

# Data checks
```{r}
# Check if phyloseq sample names match metadata rownames
phyloseq_samples <- sample_names(cycle18Sdat_PA)
metadata_samples <- rownames(meta_dr17k)

cat("Phyloseq samples count:", length(phyloseq_samples), "\n")
cat("Metadata samples count:", length(metadata_samples), "\n")
cat("Sample orders match:", identical(phyloseq_samples, metadata_samples), "\n")

# If they don't match, show the first few to diagnose
if(!identical(phyloseq_samples, metadata_samples)) {
  cat("First 10 phyloseq samples:", head(phyloseq_samples, 10), "\n")
  cat("First 10 metadata samples:", head(metadata_samples, 10), "\n")
}

# STEP 2: CALCULATE NMDS VALUES WITH JACCARD DISSIMILARITY
nmdsJaccard_PA <- ordinate(cycle18Sdat_PA, method = "NMDS", distance = "jaccard",
                          k = 2, trymax = 1000)

# Check stress value
cat("NMDS Stress value:", nmdsJaccard_PA$stress, "\n")

# STEP 3: PROPERLY EXTRACT AND ALIGN NMDS SCORES WITH METADATA
# Extract NMDS scores
nmds_scores_PA <- as.data.frame(scores(nmdsJaccard_PA, display = "sites"))

# CRITICAL: Use proper sample name alignment instead of assuming order
# Get the sample names from the NMDS ordination object
nmds_sample_names <- rownames(nmds_scores_PA)

# Add metadata using proper sample name matching
nmds_scores_PA$sitelocality <- meta_dr17k[nmds_sample_names, "sitelocality"]  
nmds_scores_PA$fraction <- meta_dr17k[nmds_sample_names, "fraction"]

# Verify the alignment worked
cat("NMDS scores sample count:", nrow(nmds_scores_PA), "\n")
cat("Metadata successfully added:", all(!is.na(nmds_scores_PA$sitelocality)), "\n")

```


# NMDS PLOT JACCARD Dissimilarity of Presence Absence Data by SITE LOCALITY
```{r}
# Plot
oP_PA <- plot_ordination(cycle18Sdat_PA, nmdsJaccard_PA)
oP_PA$layers <- oP_PA$layers[-1] 




# Extract NMDS scores and add metadata
nmds_scores_PA <- as.data.frame(scores(nmdsJaccard_PA, display = "sites"))
```
