```{r}
nmds_scores_PA$sitelocality <- meta_dr17k$sitelocality  
nmds_scores_PA$fraction <- meta_dr17k$fraction 


# The plot_ordination function should use the same sample order, but let's verify
plot_sample_names <- rownames(oP_PA$data)
cat("Plot data sample alignment check:", identical(plot_sample_names, nmds_sample_names), "\n")

# Set the correct factor order for sitelocality
nmds_scores_PA$sitelocality <- factor(nmds_scores_PA$sitelocality, 
                                     levels = c("Stetson_shallow", "EFGB_shallow", "EFGB_deep", 
                                                "Bright_shallow","Bright_deep1", "Bright_deep2",
                                                "McGrail_deep", "Alderdice_shallow", "Alderdice_deep1", 
                                                "Alderdice_deep2", "Diaphus_deep1", "Diaphus_deep2"))
# Flip NMDS2 axis (vertical flip) for both datasets consistently
nmds_scores_PA$NMDS2 <- -nmds_scores_PA$NMDS2
 # STEP 5: CALCULATE CONVEX HULLS USING PROPERLY ALIGNED DATA
find_hull <- function(df) df[chull(df$NMDS1, df$NMDS2), ]

# Calculate convex hulls for each site locality
hulls_PA <- nmds_scores_PA %>% 
  group_by(sitelocality) %>% 
  do(find_hull(.))

# Build site locality plot
oP_PA <- oP_PA + 
  geom_point(data = nmds_scores_PA, 
             aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, fill = sitelocality, shape = fraction), 
             size = 4) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22, 23)) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c12_sites) + 
  labs(title = "18S Presence/Absence: Jaccard NMDS", 
       fill = "Site", shape = "Fraction") + 
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.box.just = "left",
        legend.margin = margin(t = 10), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 26, hjust = 0.5),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 24),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Site", title.position = "top", 
                            title.hjust = 0, ncol = 2, order = 2, 
                            override.aes = list(shape = 21)),
         shape = guide_legend(title = "Fraction", title.position = "top", 
                             title.hjust = 0, ncol = 1, order = 1, 
                             override.aes = list(fill = "black"))) + 
  # Add convex hulls using properly aligned data
  geom_polygon(data = hulls_PA, 
               aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = sitelocality, fill = sitelocality), 
               alpha = 0.4, color = NA) +
  # Add stress annotation
  annotate('text', x = 0.75, y = -0.6, 
           label = paste('stress =', round(nmdsJaccard_PA$stress, 3)), 
           size = 6)

# Plot
print(oP_PA)

# Save
ggsave("18S_NMDS_Jacc_SITE.pdf", plot = oP_PA, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./NMDS",
      scale = 1, width = 8.5, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```


# NMDS PLOT JACCARD Dissimilarity of Presence Absence Data by MEAN TEMPERATURE
```{r}
oP_temp_PA <- plot_ordination(cycle18Sdat_PA, nmdsJaccard_PA)
oP_temp_PA$layers <- oP_temp_PA$layers[-1]
oP_temp_PA$data$NMDS2 <- -oP_temp_PA$data$NMDS2

# Create new scores for temperature (with same flipping)
nmds_scores_temp_PA <- as.data.frame(scores(nmdsJaccard_PA, display = "sites"))
nmds_scores_temp_PA$NMDS2 <- -nmds_scores_temp_PA$NMDS2
nmds_scores_temp_PA <- nmds_scores_temp_PA[match(rownames(meta_dr17k), rownames(nmds_scores_temp_PA)), ]
nmds_scores_temp_PA$temp_mean <- meta_dr17k$temp_mean  
nmds_scores_temp_PA$fraction <- meta_dr17k$fraction

# Calculate hulls for temperature
hulls_temp_PA <- nmds_scores_temp_PA %>% 
  group_by(temp_mean) %>% 
  do(find_hull(.))

# Build temperature plot
oP_temp_PA <- oP_temp_PA + 
  geom_point(data = nmds_scores_temp_PA, aes(fill = temp_mean, shape = fraction), size = 4) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22, 23)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", name = "Mean Temperature", guide = "colorbar") +
  labs(title = "18S Presence/Absence: Jaccard NMDS", fill = "Mean Temperature", shape = "Fraction") + 
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.justification = "left",
        legend.margin = margin(t = 10), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 26),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 24),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(30, "pt"),   
        legend.box.spacing = unit(20, "pt"),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Mean Temperature", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, order = 1, barwidth = 20, barheight = 1.5),
         shape = guide_legend(title = "Fraction", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, ncol = 1, order = 2, override.aes = list(fill = "black"))) + 
  geom_polygon(data = hulls_temp_PA, 
               aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = temp_mean, fill = temp_mean), 
               alpha = 0.3,  
               color = NA) +
  annotate('text', x = 0.4, y = -0.45, label = paste('stress =', round(nmdsJaccard_PA$stress, 3)), size = 6)

# View Plot
print(oP_temp_PA)

# Save
ggsave("18S_NMDS_Jacc_TEMP.pdf", plot = oP_temp_PA, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./J25",
      scale = 1, width = 8.5, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```


# NMDS PLOT JACCARD Dissimilarity of Presence Absence Data by DEPTH
```{r}
oP_depth_PA <- plot_ordination(cycle18Sdat_PA, nmdsJaccard_PA)
oP_depth_PA$layers <- oP_depth_PA$layers[-1]
oP_depth_PA$data$NMDS2 <- -oP_depth_PA$data$NMDS2

# Create new scores for depth (with same flipping)
nmds_scores_depth_PA <- as.data.frame(scores(nmdsJaccard_PA, display = "sites"))
nmds_scores_depth_PA$NMDS2 <- -nmds_scores_depth_PA$NMDS2
nmds_scores_depth_PA <- nmds_scores_depth_PA[match(rownames(meta_dr17k), rownames(nmds_scores_depth_PA)), ]
nmds_scores_depth_PA$depth <- meta_dr17k$depth  
nmds_scores_depth_PA$fraction <- meta_dr17k$fraction

# Calculate hulls for temperature
hulls_depth_PA <- nmds_scores_depth_PA %>% 
  group_by(depth) %>% 
  do(find_hull(.))

# Build temperature plot
oP_depth_PA <- oP_depth_PA + 
  geom_point(data = nmds_scores_depth_PA, aes(fill = depth, shape = fraction), size = 4) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22, 23)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", name = "Mean Temperature", guide = "colorbar", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "18S Presence/Absence: Jaccard NMDS", fill = "Depth (m)", shape = "Fraction") + 
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.justification = "left",
        legend.margin = margin(t = 10), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 26),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 24),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(30, "pt"),   
        legend.box.spacing = unit(20, "pt"),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Depth (m)", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, order = 1, barwidth = 20, barheight = 1.5),
         shape = guide_legend(title = "Fraction", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, ncol = 1, order = 2, override.aes = list(fill = "black"))) + 
  geom_polygon(data = hulls_depth_PA, 
               aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = depth, fill = depth), 
               alpha = 0.5,  
               color = NA) +
  annotate('text', x = 0.75, y = -0.6, label = paste('stress =', round(nmdsJaccard_PA$stress, 3)), size = 6)

# View Plot
print(oP_depth_PA)

# Save
ggsave("18S_NMDS_Jacc_DEPTH.pdf", plot = oP_depth_PA, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./NMDS",
      scale = 1, width = 8.5, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

# NMDS PLOT JACCARD Dissimilarity of Presence Absence Data by MEAN VISIBILITY RANK
```{r}
oP_turb_PA <- plot_ordination(cycle18Sdat_PA, nmdsJaccard_PA)
oP_turb_PA$layers <- oP_turb_PA$layers[-1]
oP_turb_PA$data$NMDS2 <- -oP_turb_PA$data$NMDS2

# Create new scores for turbidity (with same flipping)
nmds_scores_turb_PA <- as.data.frame(scores(nmdsJaccard_PA, display = "sites"))
nmds_scores_turb_PA$NMDS2 <- -nmds_scores_turb_PA$NMDS2
nmds_scores_turb_PA <- nmds_scores_turb_PA[match(rownames(meta_dr17k), rownames(nmds_scores_turb_PA)), ]
nmds_scores_turb_PA$turbidity_std_rank <- meta_dr17k$turbidity_std_rank  
nmds_scores_turb_PA$fraction <- meta_dr17k$fraction 

# Calculate hulls for turbidity
hulls_turb_PA <- nmds_scores_turb_PA %>% 
  group_by(turbidity_std_rank) %>% 
  do(find_hull(.))

# Build turbidity plot
oP_turb_PA <- oP_turb_PA + 
  geom_point(data = oP_turb_PA$data, aes(fill = turbidity_std_rank, shape = fraction), size = 4) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22, 23)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis") +
  labs(title = "18S Presence/Absence: Jaccard NMDS", fill = "Mean Visibility Rank", shape = "Fraction") + 
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.box.just = "left",
        legend.margin = margin(t = 10), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 26, hjust = 0.5),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 24),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(30, "pt"),   
        legend.box.spacing = unit(20, "pt"),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 24),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Mean Visibility Rank", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, order = 1, barwidth = 20, barheight = 1.5),
         shape = guide_legend(title = "Fraction", title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0, ncol = 1, order = 2, override.aes = list(fill = "black"))) + 
  geom_polygon(data = hulls_turb_PA, 
               aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = turbidity_std_rank, fill = turbidity_std_rank), 
               alpha = 0.3,  
               color = NA) +
  annotate('text', x = 0.75, y = -0.6, label = paste('stress =', round(nmdsJaccard_PA$stress, 3)), size = 6)

# View Plot
print(oP_turb_PA)

# Save
ggsave("18S_NMDS_Jacc_TURB.pdf", plot = oP_turb_PA, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./NMDS",
      scale = 1, width = 8.5, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

# PLOT ALL JACCARD P/A NMDS
```{r}
#library(patchwork)

# Create modified versions without certain legends
oP_site_PA <- oP_PA

# Depth plot - remove Fraction legend, keep only Depth (meters)
oP_depth_clean_PA <- oP_depth_PA + 
  guides(shape = "none",
         fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Depth (meters)", 
                              title.position = "top", 
                              title.hjust = 0.5, 
                              order = 1, 
                              barwidth = 20, 
                              barheight = 3))

# Turbidity plot - remove Fraction legend, keep only Mean Visibility Rank  
oP_turb_clean_PA <- oP_turb_PA + 
  guides(shape = "none",
         fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Mean Visibility Rank", 
                              title.position = "top", 
                              title.hjust = 0.5, 
                              order = 1, 
                              barwidth = 20, 
                              barheight = 3))

# Combine plots horizontally
combined_plot_PA <- oP_site_PA + oP_depth_clean_PA + oP_turb_clean_PA

# Add overall title and panel labels
combined_plot_PA <- combined_plot_PA + 
  plot_annotation(
    title = "18S Presence/Absence: Jaccard Dissimilarity NMDS",
    tag_levels = 'A',
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 28, hjust = 0.5))
  )

# Print the combined plot
print(combined_plot_PA)

# Save the combined plot
ggsave("18S_NMDS_Jaccard_PA_Combined.pdf", 
       plot = combined_plot_PA, 
       device = "pdf", 
       path = "./NMDS",
       scale = 1, 
       width = 24,
       height = 10,
       units = "in", 
       dpi = 300)
```

============================================================
----------------- PERMDISP and PERMANOVAs ------------------
============================================================
There are A LOT of PERMANOVA tests to run because there are 4 datasets to test (sessile, motile, 1000, 500)
Therefore, it will be helpful to create functions to run tests on all of the data subsets and export results.
Conduct PERMANOVAs to seperate environmental from spatial effects using conditional PERMANOVA,
avoiding the common ecological fallacy of attributing spatial autocorrelation to dispersal limitation
when it may reflect unmeasured environmental gradients. 

Variables: Depth, Turbidity, PP_Mean, Lat, Long, Sitelocality
Step 1: Data setup and verification
Organize community data (4th root transformed ASV abundances) by size fraction
Create matched metadata subsets for each fraction
Verify that all required variables (PP_mean, longitude, depth, turbidity, site) are present

Step 2: Assumption testing (PERMDISP)
Test homogeneity of multivariate dispersions for categorical variables
Why this matters: PERMANOVA assumes groups have similar dispersions. Violations mean "significant" results might reflect dispersion differences rather than true centroid differences
What to expect: Some violations are common in ecological data, but flagged results should be interpreted cautiously

Step 3: Run Analyses
MARGINAL Tests (Individual Effects):
PP_mean - Total PP effect (ignoring other variables)
longitude - Total longitude effect (ignoring other variables)
sitelocality - Total site effect (ignoring other variables)
fraction - Total size fraction effect (NEW!, all_samples only)

CONDITIONAL Tests (Explicit Controls):
PP_mean | longitude - PP effect after removing longitude
longitude | PP_mean - Longitude effect after removing PP_mean

SEQUENTIAL Tests (Order Matters):
depth + turbidity_std_rank - Sequential: depth first, then turbidity|depth
depth + turbidity_std_rank + PP_mean - Sequential: depth, turbidity|depth, PP_mean|depth+turbidity
depth + turbidity_std_rank + longitude - Sequential: depth, turbidity|depth, longitude|depth+turbidity
depth + turbidity_std_rank + PP_mean + longitude - Sequential: all variables in order
depth + turbidity_std_rank + sitelocality - Sequential: env first, then site|env
# Set up data variables
```{r set up PERMDISP and PERMANOVA}
# Using 4th Root Transformed Data
# library(vegan)
# library(dplyr)
# library(tidyr)
# library(ggplot2)

# ========== SETUP DATA STRUCTURE USING EXISTING OBJECTS ==========

# Check if sample_meta_clean exists, if not use metadata_ARMS
if (!exists("sample_meta_clean")) {
  sample_meta_clean <- metadata_ARMS
  cat("Using metadata_ARMS as sample_meta_clean\n")
}

# Get sample names for each fraction
samples_100_18S <- rownames(sample_meta_clean)[sample_meta_clean$fraction == "100"]
samples_500_18S <- rownames(sample_meta_clean)[sample_meta_clean$fraction == "500"]
samples_sessile_18S <- rownames(sample_meta_clean)[sample_meta_clean$fraction == "sessile"]
samples_motile_18S <- c(samples_100_18S, samples_500_18S)

# Create corresponding metadata subsets
meta_100 <- sample_meta_clean[samples_100_18S, ]
meta_500 <- sample_meta_clean[samples_500_18S, ]
meta_sessile <- sample_meta_clean[samples_sessile_18S, ]
meta_motile <- sample_meta_clean[samples_motile_18S, ]
meta_all <- sample_meta_clean

# Groups defined earlier
fraction_data_4th <- list(
  "all_samples" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S
  ),
  "sessile" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction == "sessile", ]
  ),
  "motile" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction %in% c("100", "500"), ]
  ),
  "100um" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_100_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction == "100", ]
  ),
  "500um" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_500_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction == "500", ]
  )
)

# Define analysis structure
fraction_data_4th <- list(
  "all_samples" = list(
    community = biotic_percs_4th,
    metadata = meta_all,
    samples = rownames(meta_all)
  ),
  "sessile" = list(
    community = biotic_percs_sessile_4th,
    metadata = meta_sessile,
    samples = samples_sessile_18S
  ),
  "motile" = list(
    community = biotic_percs_motile_4th,
    metadata = meta_motile,
    samples = samples_motile_18S
  ),
  "100um" = list(
    community = biotic_percs_100_4th,
    metadata = meta_100,
    samples = samples_100_18S
  ),
  "500um" = list(
    community = biotic_percs_500_4th,
    metadata = meta_500,
    samples = samples_500_18S
  )
)

# ============= CHECK REQUIRED VARIABLES ================

cat("\nChecking required variables in metadata:\n")
required_vars <- c("depth", "turbidity_std_rank", "latitude", "longitude", "PP_mean", "sitelocality", "fraction")

for (var in required_vars) {
  if (var %in% colnames(sample_meta_clean)) {
    n_non_na <- sum(!is.na(sample_meta_clean[[var]]))
    cat(sprintf("✓ %-20s: %d non-NA values\n", var, n_non_na))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("❌ %-20s: NOT FOUND\n", var))
  }
}

```


# Create PERMDISP and PERMANOVA functions, execute, and export
```{r}
# ============== CORRECTED CONDITIONAL VARIANCE PARTITIONING =================

run_productivity_geography_analysis <- function(fraction_name, fraction_data) {
  
  community_data <- fraction_data$data
  metadata <- fraction_data$metadata
  
  # Clean community data
  community_clean <- community_data[rowSums(community_data) > 0, ]
  
  # Ensure samples match between community and metadata
  community_samples <- colnames(community_clean)
  metadata_samples <- rownames(metadata)
  common_samples <- intersect(community_samples, metadata_samples)
  
  # Subset both to common samples
  community_clean <- community_clean[, common_samples]
  metadata <- metadata[common_samples, ]
  
  cat("Samples in analysis:", ncol(community_clean), "\n")
  
  # Run PERMDISP
  permdisp_results <- test_permdisp_assumptions(fraction_name, community_clean, metadata)
  
  # For PERMANOVA, use community_clean and metadata directly
  run_permanova <- function(formula_str, test_name) {
    tryCatch({
      result <- adonis2(
        as.formula(paste("t(community_clean) ~", formula_str)),
        data = metadata,
        permutations = 999,
        method = "bray",
        by = "terms"
      )
      
      # Convert result to data frame for easier manipulation
      result_df <- as.data.frame(result)
      
      # Calculate model R²
      model_r2 <- sum(result_df[!rownames(result_df) %in% c("Residual", "Total"), "R2"], na.rm = TRUE)
      
      cat(sprintf("  %-35s: R² = %.3f\n", test_name, model_r2))
      
      # Extract detailed statistics for each term (excluding Residual and Total)
      term_rows <- !rownames(result_df) %in% c("Residual", "Total")
      
      if (sum(term_rows) > 0) {
        detailed_stats <- data.frame(
          Test_Name = test_name,
          Term = rownames(result_df)[term_rows],
          Df = result_df[term_rows, "Df"],
          SumOfSqs = result_df[term_rows, "SumOfSqs"],
          R2 = result_df[term_rows, "R2"],
          F_Statistic = result_df[term_rows, "F"],
          P_value = result_df[term_rows, "Pr(>F)"],
          Model_R2 = model_r2,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      } else {
        detailed_stats <- data.frame(
          Test_Name = test_name,
          Term = NA,
          Df = NA,
          SumOfSqs = NA,
          R2 = NA,
          F_Statistic = NA,
          P_value = NA,
          Model_R2 = model_r2,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
      
      return(list(
        result = result, 
        r2 = model_r2,
        detailed_stats = detailed_stats
      ))
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat(sprintf("  %-35s: ERROR - %s\n", test_name, e$message))
      
      # Return error data frame
      error_stats <- data.frame(
        Test_Name = test_name,
        Term = "ERROR",
        Df = NA,
        SumOfSqs = NA,
        R2 = NA,
        F_Statistic = NA,
        P_value = NA,
        Model_R2 = NA,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      
      return(list(
        result = NULL,
        r2 = NA,
        detailed_stats = error_stats
      ))
    })
  }
  
  # Create results list
  results <- list(permdisp = permdisp_results)
  detailed_stats_list <- list()
  
  # ================ CORE ENVIRONMENTAL & SPATIAL EFFECTS =================
  
  # Individual environmental effects
  results$depth_alone <- run_permanova("depth", "Depth Alone")
  detailed_stats_list[["depth_alone"]] <- results$depth_alone$detailed_stats
  
  results$turbidity_alone <- run_permanova("turbidity_std_rank", "Turbidity Alone")
  detailed_stats_list[["turbidity_alone"]] <- results$turbidity_alone$detailed_stats
  
  # Individual geographic effects  
  results$latitude_alone <- run_permanova("latitude", "Latitude Alone")
  detailed_stats_list[["latitude_alone"]] <- results$latitude_alone$detailed_stats
  
  results$longitude_alone <- run_permanova("longitude", "Longitude Alone")
  detailed_stats_list[["longitude_alone"]] <- results$longitude_alone$detailed_stats
  
  # Core environmental model
  results$env_baseline <- run_permanova("depth + turbidity_std_rank", "Environmental Baseline (D+T)")
  detailed_stats_list[["env_baseline"]] <- results$env_baseline$detailed_stats
  
  # Geographic models
  results$geography_combined <- run_permanova("latitude + longitude", "Geographic Combined (Lat+Long)")
  detailed_stats_list[["geography_combined"]] <- results$geography_combined$detailed_stats
  
  # ================= PRODUCTIVITY TESTS ==================
  
  # Test PP_mean alone
  results$pp_alone <- run_permanova("PP_mean", "PP_mean Alone")
  detailed_stats_list[["pp_alone"]] <- results$pp_alone$detailed_stats
  
  # ============== CORRECTED CONDITIONAL TESTS - MODEL COMPARISONS ===========
  
  # CORRECTED: True conditional variance partitioning using model comparisons
  
  # 1. PP_mean | Geography (CORRECTED)
  cat("  Running CORRECTED conditional test: PP_mean | geography\n")
  geography_only_r2 <- results$geography_combined$r2
  pp_geography_result <- run_permanova("latitude + longitude + PP_mean", "Geography + PP_mean")
  pp_geography_r2 <- pp_geography_result$r2
  pp_conditional_r2 <- pp_geography_r2 - geography_only_r2
  
  results$pp_after_geography <- list(
    r2 = pp_conditional_r2,
    detailed_stats = data.frame(
      Test_Name = "PP_mean | All Geography",
      Term = "PP_mean_conditional",
      Df = NA,
      SumOfSqs = NA,
      R2 = pp_conditional_r2,
      F_Statistic = NA,
      P_value = NA,
      Model_R2 = pp_conditional_r2,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  )
  detailed_stats_list[["pp_after_geography"]] <- results$pp_after_geography$detailed_stats
  cat(sprintf("  %-35s: R² = %.3f\n", "PP_mean | All Geography", pp_conditional_r2))
  
  # 2. Geography | PP_mean (CORRECTED)
  cat("  Running CORRECTED conditional test: geography | PP_mean\n")
  pp_only_r2 <- results$pp_alone$r2
  geography_conditional_r2 <- pp_geography_r2 - pp_only_r2
  
  results$geography_after_pp <- list(
    r2 = geography_conditional_r2,
    detailed_stats = data.frame(
      Test_Name = "All Geography | PP_mean",
      Term = "geography_conditional",
      Df = NA,
      SumOfSqs = NA,
      R2 = geography_conditional_r2,
      F_Statistic = NA,
      P_value = NA,
      Model_R2 = geography_conditional_r2,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  )
  detailed_stats_list[["geography_after_pp"]] <- results$geography_after_pp$detailed_stats
  cat(sprintf("  %-35s: R² = %.3f\n", "All Geography | PP_mean", geography_conditional_r2))
  
  # 3. Geography | Environment (CORRECTED)
  cat("  Running CORRECTED conditional test: geography | environment\n")
  env_only_r2 <- results$env_baseline$r2
  env_spatial_result <- run_permanova("depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude", "Environmental + Spatial")
  env_spatial_r2 <- env_spatial_result$r2
  spatial_conditional_r2 <- env_spatial_r2 - env_only_r2
  
  results$geography_after_env <- list(
    r2 = spatial_conditional_r2,
    detailed_stats = data.frame(
      Test_Name = "Geography | Environment", 
      Term = "geography_conditional",
      Df = NA,
      SumOfSqs = NA,
      R2 = spatial_conditional_r2,
      F_Statistic = NA,
      P_value = NA,
      Model_R2 = spatial_conditional_r2,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  )
  detailed_stats_list[["geography_after_env"]] <- results$geography_after_env$detailed_stats
  cat(sprintf("  %-35s: R² = %.3f\n", "Geography | Environment", spatial_conditional_r2))
  
  # Store the environmental + spatial model
  results$env_spatial <- env_spatial_result
  detailed_stats_list[["env_spatial"]] <- results$env_spatial$detailed_stats
  
  # Full model with productivity
  results$full_model <- run_permanova("depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude + PP_mean", "Full Model")
  detailed_stats_list[["full_model"]] <- results$full_model$detailed_stats
  
  # ============ CONTROL ANALYSES ===============
  
  # Site effects (for comparison)
  results$site_alone <- run_permanova("sitelocality", "Site Effects Alone")
  detailed_stats_list[["site_alone"]] <- results$site_alone$detailed_stats
  
  # CORRECTED: Site | All Measured Variables
  cat("  Running CORRECTED conditional test: site | all variables\n")
  full_model_r2 <- results$full_model$r2
  site_all_result <- run_permanova("depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude + PP_mean + sitelocality", "Site | All Measured Variables")
  site_all_r2 <- site_all_result$r2
  site_conditional_r2 <- site_all_r2 - full_model_r2
  
  results$site_after_all <- list(
    r2 = site_conditional_r2,
    detailed_stats = data.frame(
      Test_Name = "Site | All Measured Variables",
      Term = "site_conditional", 
      Df = NA,
      SumOfSqs = NA,
      R2 = site_conditional_r2,
      F_Statistic = NA,
      P_value = NA,
      Model_R2 = site_conditional_r2,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  )
  detailed_stats_list[["site_after_all"]] <- results$site_after_all$detailed_stats
  cat(sprintf("  %-35s: R² = %.3f\n", "Site | All Measured Variables", site_conditional_r2))
  
  # Fraction effects (only for all_samples dataset)
  if (fraction_name == "all_samples") {
    results$fraction_alone <- run_permanova("fraction", "Fraction Effects Alone")
    detailed_stats_list[["fraction_alone"]] <- results$fraction_alone$detailed_stats
  }
  
  # Environmental + site (standard model)
  results$env_site <- run_permanova("depth + turbidity_std_rank + sitelocality", "Environmental + Site")
  detailed_stats_list[["env_site"]] <- results$env_site$detailed_stats
  
  # Combine all detailed statistics for this fraction
  all_detailed_stats <- do.call(rbind, detailed_stats_list)
  all_detailed_stats$Fraction <- fraction_name
  
  # Add this to results
  results$all_detailed_stats <- all_detailed_stats
  
  return(results)
}

# ============= RUN ANALYSES FOR ALL FRACTIONS ==============

cat("\n\nRUNNING ANALYSES FOR ALL FRACTIONS\n")
cat("===================================\n")

all_results <- list()
all_permdisp_results <- list()
all_detailed_statistics <- list()

for (fraction_name in names(fraction_data_4th)) {
  result <- run_productivity_geography_analysis(fraction_name, fraction_data_4th[[fraction_name]])
  if (!is.null(result)) {
    all_results[[fraction_name]] <- result
    if (!is.null(result$permdisp)) {
      all_permdisp_results[[fraction_name]] <- result$permdisp
    }
    if (!is.null(result$all_detailed_stats)) {
      all_detailed_statistics[[fraction_name]] <- result$all_detailed_stats
    }
  }
}

# =============== COMPILE RESULTS ================

cat("\n\nCOMPILING RESULTS\n")
cat("=================\n")

# Create PERMANOVA summary table
compile_permanova_summary <- function() {
  summary_df <- data.frame(
    Fraction = character(),
    Analysis = character(),
    Test_Description = character(),
    R2 = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  test_descriptions <- list(
    "depth_alone" = "Depth Effects Alone",
    "turbidity_alone" = "Turbidity Effects Alone", 
    "latitude_alone" = "Latitude Effects Alone (Shelf-Deep Gradient)",
    "longitude_alone" = "Longitude Effects Alone (East-West Gradient)",
    "env_baseline" = "Environmental Baseline (Depth + Turbidity)",
    "geography_combined" = "Geographic Combined (Latitude + Longitude)",
    "pp_alone" = "Primary Productivity Alone", 
    "pp_after_geography" = "PP_mean | All Geography",
    "geography_after_pp" = "All Geography | PP_mean",
    "geography_after_env" = "Geography | Environment",
    "env_spatial" = "Environmental + Spatial",
    "full_model" = "Full Model (Env + Spatial + PP)",
    "site_alone" = "Site Effects Alone",
    "fraction_alone" = "Fraction Effects Alone",
    "env_site" = "Environmental + Site"
  )
  
  for (fraction_name in names(all_results)) {
    fraction_results <- all_results[[fraction_name]]
    
    for (analysis_name in names(fraction_results)) {
      if (analysis_name != "permdisp" && 
          analysis_name != "all_detailed_stats" && 
          !is.null(fraction_results[[analysis_name]])) {
        
        r2_value <- fraction_results[[analysis_name]]$r2
        
        if (!is.null(r2_value) && !is.na(r2_value)) {
          description <- ifelse(analysis_name %in% names(test_descriptions), 
                               test_descriptions[[analysis_name]], 
                               analysis_name)
          
          summary_df <- rbind(summary_df, data.frame(
            Fraction = fraction_name,
            Analysis = analysis_name,
            Test_Description = description,
            R2 = r2_value,
            stringsAsFactors = FALSE
          ))
        } else {
          cat("Warning: Missing R2 value for", fraction_name, "-", analysis_name, "\n")
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(summary_df)
}

results_summary <- compile_permanova_summary()

# Compile comprehensive detailed statistics
if (length(all_detailed_statistics) > 0) {
  comprehensive_stats <- do.call(rbind, all_detailed_statistics)
  
  if (!is.null(comprehensive_stats) && nrow(comprehensive_stats) > 0) {
    # Reorder columns for better readability
    comprehensive_stats <- comprehensive_stats[, c("Fraction", "Test_Name", "Term", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F_Statistic", "P_value", "Model_R2")]
    
    # Round numeric columns for readability
    comprehensive_stats$SumOfSqs <- round(comprehensive_stats$SumOfSqs, 6)
    comprehensive_stats$R2 <- round(comprehensive_stats$R2, 6)
    comprehensive_stats$F_Statistic <- round(comprehensive_stats$F_Statistic, 4)
    comprehensive_stats$P_value <- round(comprehensive_stats$P_value, 6)
    comprehensive_stats$Model_R2 <- round(comprehensive_stats$Model_R2, 6)
    
    # Create significance indicators
    comprehensive_stats$Significance <- ifelse(
      is.na(comprehensive_stats$P_value), "",
      ifelse(comprehensive_stats$P_value < 0.001, "***",
      ifelse(comprehensive_stats$P_value < 0.01, "**",
      ifelse(comprehensive_stats$P_value < 0.05, "*",
      ifelse(comprehensive_stats$P_value < 0.1, ".", ""))))
    )
    
    cat("✓ Compiled detailed statistics for", nrow(comprehensive_stats), "tests\n")
  }
} else {
  comprehensive_stats <- NULL
  cat("No detailed statistics to compile\n")
}

# Create PERMDISP summary table
compile_permdisp_summary <- function() {
  permdisp_df <- data.frame(
    Fraction = character(),
    Variable = character(),
    F_value = numeric(),
    P_value = numeric(),
    Assumption_Met = logical(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  for (fraction_name in names(all_permdisp_results)) {
    permdisp_data <- all_permdisp_results[[fraction_name]]
    
    for (var_name in names(permdisp_data)) {
      if (!is.null(permdisp_data[[var_name]])) {
        permdisp_df <- rbind(permdisp_df, data.frame(
          Fraction = fraction_name,
          Variable = var_name,
          F_value = permdisp_data[[var_name]]$F_value,
          P_value = permdisp_data[[var_name]]$p_value,
          Assumption_Met = permdisp_data[[var_name]]$assumption_met,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(permdisp_df)
}

permdisp_summary <- compile_permdisp_summary()

# ============== SAVE RESULTS =================

# Create  directory
_dir <- "./PERMANOVA_18S"
if (!dir.exists(_dir)) {
  dir.create(_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  cat("✓ Created  directory:", _dir, "\n")
}

# Save your existing results
write.csv(results_summary, file.path(_dir, "PERMANOVA_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

if (nrow(permdisp_summary) > 0) {
  write.csv(permdisp_summary, file.path(_dir, "PERMDISP_assumption_testing.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}

# Save detailed statistics
if (!is.null(comprehensive_stats) && nrow(comprehensive_stats) > 0) {
  # Save the comprehensive detailed statistics
  write.csv(comprehensive_stats, 
           file.path(output_dir, "PERMANOVA_comprehensive_detailed_statistics.csv"), 
           row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Create simplified version for spreadsheet using base R
  simplified_stats <- comprehensive_stats[, c("Fraction", "Test_Name", "Term", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F_Statistic", "P_value", "Significance")]
  
  # Rename columns using base R
  colnames(simplified_stats) <- c("Size_Fraction", "Analysis", "Variable", "df", "SS", "R2", "F.Statistic", "p.value", "Significance")
  
  write.csv(simplified_stats, 
           file.path(output_dir, "PERMANOVA_simplified_for_spreadsheet.csv"), 
           row.names = FALSE)
  
  cat("✓ Saved detailed PERMANOVA statistics\n")
  cat("✓ Use 'PERMANOVA_simplified_for_spreadsheet.csv' for copy-paste to your spreadsheet\n")
} else {
  cat("No detailed statistics to save\n")
}
```


```{r}
# Print results in the order they were run
#"depth_alone", "turbidity_alone", "latitude_alone", "longitude_alone","env_baseline", "geography_combined", "pp_alone"
#"pp_after_geography", "geography_after_pp","geography_after_env"
test_order <- c("geography_after_env", "env_spatial", "full_model", "site_alone", "fraction_alone","env_site", "site_after_all")

for (fraction_name in names(all_results)) {
  cat("\n", rep("=", 80), sep="")
  cat("\nFRACTION:", toupper(fraction_name))
  cat("\n", rep("=", 80), sep="", "\n")
  
  for (test_name in test_order) {
    if (test_name %in% names(all_results[[fraction_name]])) {
      cat("\n", test_name, ":")
      cat("\n", rep("-", 40), sep="", "\n")
      print(all_results[[fraction_name]][[test_name]]$result)
      cat("\n")
    }
  }
}
```

#Testing for differences between coral and backgournd ARMS within Bright, Alderdice, and Diaphus deep samples
```{r coral v background}
# ================== CREATE DEEP DEPLOYMENT SUBSET ==================

create_deep_deployment_subset <- function(community_data, metadata) {
  
  cat("Creating deep deployment coral vs background subset...\n")
  
  # Define deep deployment sitelocalities
  deep_sites <- c("Bright_deep1", "Bright_deep2", "Alderdice_deep1", "Alderdice_deep2", 
                  "Diaphus_deep1", "Diaphus_deep2")
  
  # Filter metadata to deep sites only
  deep_metadata <- metadata[metadata$sitelocality %in% deep_sites, ]
  
  # Filter to sessile and motile fractions only
  deep_metadata <- deep_metadata[deep_metadata$fraction %in% c("sessile", "100", "500"), ]
  
  # Get corresponding samples
  deep_samples <- rownames(deep_metadata)
  
  # Filter community data
  deep_community <- community_data[, deep_samples]
  
  # Remove ASVs with zero reads in this subset
  deep_community <- deep_community[rowSums(deep_community) > 0, ]
  
  # Create coral vs background variable
  deep_metadata$habitat_type <- ifelse(
    grepl("deep1$", deep_metadata$sitelocality), "coral", "background"
  )
  
  # Create broader site variable (without deep1/deep2 distinction)
  deep_metadata$site_group <- gsub("_deep[12]$", "", deep_metadata$sitelocality)
  
  cat("Subset created:\n")
  cat("  Total samples:", nrow(deep_metadata), "\n")
  cat("  Coral sites:", sum(deep_metadata$habitat_type == "coral"), "\n")
  cat("  Background sites:", sum(deep_metadata$habitat_type == "background"), "\n")
  cat("  Site groups:", paste(unique(deep_metadata$site_group), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("  Fractions:", paste(unique(deep_metadata$fraction), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Verify pairing
  cat("\nSite pairing verification:\n")
  pairing_table <- table(deep_metadata$site_group, deep_metadata$habitat_type)
  print(pairing_table)
  
  return(list(
    community = deep_community,
    metadata = deep_metadata
  ))
}

# ================== RUN CORAL VS BACKGROUND PERMANOVA ==================

test_coral_background_effects <- function(community_data, metadata, include_sitelocality = FALSE) {
  
  cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
  cat("TESTING CORAL vs BACKGROUND HABITAT EFFECTS\n")
  cat("Include sitelocality:", include_sitelocality, "\n")
  cat(paste(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
  
  # Create distance matrix
  dist_matrix <- vegdist(t(community_data), method = "bray")
  
  results <- list()
  
  # Model 1: Test habitat type alone
  cat("\n1. HABITAT TYPE ALONE:\n")
  habitat_alone <- adonis2(
    dist_matrix ~ habitat_type,
    data = metadata,
    permutations = 999,
    method = "bray"
  )
  
  habitat_r2 <- habitat_alone$R2[1]
  cat(sprintf("   Habitat type (coral vs background): R² = %.3f, F = %.2f, p = %.3f\n", 
              habitat_r2, habitat_alone$F[1], habitat_alone$`Pr(>F)`[1]))
  
  results$habitat_alone <- list(
    result = habitat_alone,
    r2 = habitat_r2
  )
  
  # Model 2: Test environmental variables alone
  cat("\n2. ENVIRONMENTAL VARIABLES:\n")
  env_model <- adonis2(
    dist_matrix ~ depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude,
    data = metadata,
    permutations = 999,
    method = "bray",
    by = "terms"
  )
  
  env_r2 <- sum(env_model$R2[1:4])  # Sum R² for all environmental terms
  cat(sprintf("   Environmental (depth + turbidity + lat + long): R² = %.3f\n", env_r2))
  
  results$environmental <- list(
    result = env_model,
    r2 = env_r2
  )
  
  # Model 3: Test habitat after environmental variables
  cat("\n3. HABITAT TYPE AFTER ENVIRONMENTAL VARIABLES:\n")
  if (include_sitelocality) {
    # Conservative approach: include sitelocality
    habitat_after_env <- adonis2(
      dist_matrix ~ depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude + sitelocality + habitat_type,
      data = metadata,
      permutations = 999,
      method = "bray",
      by = "terms"
    )
    
    # habitat_type is the last term
    habitat_conditional_r2 <- habitat_after_env$R2[nrow(habitat_after_env) - 1]  # Exclude residual row
    
    cat(sprintf("   Habitat | (Environment + Site): R² = %.3f, F = %.2f, p = %.3f\n", 
                habitat_conditional_r2, 
                habitat_after_env$F[nrow(habitat_after_env) - 1],
                habitat_after_env$`Pr(>F)`[nrow(habitat_after_env) - 1]))
    
  } else {
    # Direct approach: test habitat after environment only
    habitat_after_env <- adonis2(
      dist_matrix ~ depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude + habitat_type,
      data = metadata,
      permutations = 999,
      method = "bray",
      by = "terms"
    )
    
    # habitat_type is the 5th term
    habitat_conditional_r2 <- habitat_after_env$R2[5]
    
    cat(sprintf("   Habitat | Environment: R² = %.3f, F = %.2f, p = %.3f\n", 
                habitat_conditional_r2, 
                habitat_after_env$F[5],
                habitat_after_env$`Pr(>F)`[5]))
  }
  
  results$habitat_conditional <- list(
    result = habitat_after_env,
    r2 = habitat_conditional_r2
  )
  
  # Model 4: Full model for context
  cat("\n4. FULL MODEL:\n")
  if (include_sitelocality) {
    full_formula <- "depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude + sitelocality + habitat_type"
  } else {
    full_formula <- "depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude + habitat_type"
  }
  
  full_model <- adonis2(
    as.formula(paste("dist_matrix ~", full_formula)),
    data = metadata,
    permutations = 999,
    method = "bray"
  )
  
  full_r2 <- sum(full_model$R2[-nrow(full_model)])  # Exclude residual
  cat(sprintf("   Full model: R² = %.3f\n", full_r2))
  
  results$full_model <- list(
    result = full_model,
    r2 = full_r2
  )
  
  # Model 5: Test fraction effects (since we have sessile + motile)
  cat("\n5. FRACTION EFFECTS:\n")
  fraction_model <- adonis2(
    dist_matrix ~ fraction,
    data = metadata,
    permutations = 999,
    method = "bray"
  )
  
  fraction_r2 <- fraction_model$R2[1]
  cat(sprintf("   Fraction effects: R² = %.3f, F = %.2f, p = %.3f\n", 
              fraction_r2, fraction_model$F[1], fraction_model$`Pr(>F)`[1]))
  
  results$fraction <- list(
    result = fraction_model,
    r2 = fraction_r2
  )
  
  return(results)
}

# ================== RUN THE ANALYSIS ==================

# Create deep deployment subset
deep_data <- create_deep_deployment_subset(biotic_percs_18S_4th, metadata_18S)

# Test both approaches
cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("APPROACH 1: DIRECT TEST (without sitelocality covariate)\n")
cat("Question: Does coral vs background habitat explain site differences?\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

results_direct <- test_coral_background_effects(
  deep_data$community, 
  deep_data$metadata, 
  include_sitelocality = FALSE
)

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("APPROACH 2: CONSERVATIVE TEST (with sitelocality covariate)\n") 
cat("Question: Is there coral vs background effect beyond all site-specific variation?\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

results_conservative <- test_coral_background_effects(
  deep_data$community, 
  deep_data$metadata, 
  include_sitelocality = TRUE
)

# ================== INTERPRETATION SUMMARY ==================

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("INTERPRETATION SUMMARY\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")

habitat_direct_r2 <- results_direct$habitat_conditional$r2
habitat_conservative_r2 <- results_conservative$habitat_conditional$r2

cat("CORAL vs BACKGROUND EFFECTS:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Direct approach (Habitat | Environment): R² = %.3f\n", habitat_direct_r2))
cat(sprintf("  Conservative approach (Habitat | Env + Site): R² = %.3f\n", habitat_conservative_r2))

cat("\nINTERPRETATION:\n")
if (habitat_direct_r2 > 0.05 && habitat_conservative_r2 < 0.02) {
  cat("  → Site differences are largely explained by coral vs background habitat\n")
  cat("  → Coral and background communities are systematically different\n")
} else if (habitat_direct_r2 < 0.02) {
  cat("  → Site differences are NOT explained by coral vs background habitat\n") 
  cat("  → Local unmeasured conditions or stochastic processes dominate\n")
} else if (habitat_conservative_r2 > 0.05) {
  cat("  → Strong coral vs background effect beyond all site-specific variation\n")
  cat("  → Habitat type is a fundamental structuring factor\n")
} else {
  cat("  → Mixed evidence - habitat effects present but site-specific factors also important\n")
}

# ================== SAVE RESULTS ==================

# Save the subset for future use
write.csv(deep_data$metadata, "deep_deployment_metadata.csv", row.names = TRUE)

cat("\nDeep deployment subset metadata saved to: deep_deployment_metadata.csv\n")
cat("Subset includes", nrow(deep_data$metadata), "samples from 6 deep deployment sites\n")
cat("Testing coral vs background habitat effects on", nrow(deep_data$community), "ASVs\n")
```


# ------------ Export PERMANOVA Results Tables ---------------
```{r}
#library(dplyr)
#library(tidyr)
#library(openxlsx)

# =========== Function 1: Individual Effects Model ===========

create_individual_effects_table <- function(data) {
  
  # Define individual effect tests
  individual_tests <- c("Depth Alone", "Turbidity Alone", "Latitude Alone", 
                       "Longitude Alone", "PP_mean Alone")
  
  # Filter and reshape data
  individual_data <- data %>%
    dplyr::filter(Test_Name %in% individual_tests) %>%
    dplyr::select(Fraction, Test_Name, Term, Df, SumOfSqs, R2, F_Statistic, P_value) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      # Clean up test names for display
      Test_Display = dplyr::case_when(
        Test_Name == "Depth Alone" ~ "Depth",
        Test_Name == "Turbidity Alone" ~ "Turbidity",
        Test_Name == "Latitude Alone" ~ "Latitude", 
        Test_Name == "Longitude Alone" ~ "Longitude",
        Test_Name == "PP_mean Alone" ~ "PP_mean",
        TRUE ~ Test_Name
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(factor(Test_Display, levels = c("Depth", "Turbidity", "Latitude", "Longitude", "PP_mean")))
  
  # Create wide format
  individual_wide <- individual_data %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = Fraction,
      values_from = c(Df, SumOfSqs, R2, F_Statistic, P_value),
      names_sep = "_"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(Test_Display, Term, everything())
  
  return(individual_wide)
}

# =========== Function 2: Model Comparison Table ============

create_model_comparison_table <- function(data) {
  
  # Updated model_tests to include ALL model tests
  model_tests <- c("Environmental Baseline (D+T)", "Geographic Combined (Lat+Long)",
                  "Environmental + Spatial", "Full Model", "Site Effects Alone",
                  "Environmental + Site", "Fraction Effects Alone")
  
  # Get unique tests per fraction (removing individual terms)
  model_data <- data %>%
    dplyr::filter(Test_Name %in% model_tests) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Fraction, Test_Name) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      Model_R2 = dplyr::first(Model_R2),
      Total_Df = sum(Df, na.rm = TRUE),
      Total_SS = sum(SumOfSqs, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = 'drop'
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Test_Display = dplyr::case_when(
        Test_Name == "Environmental Baseline (D+T)" ~ "Environmental (D+T)",
        Test_Name == "Geographic Combined (Lat+Long)" ~ "Geographic (Lat+Long)",
        Test_Name == "Environmental + Spatial" ~ "Environmental + Spatial",
        Test_Name == "Full Model" ~ "Full Model",
        Test_Name == "Site Effects Alone" ~ "Site Effects",
        Test_Name == "Environmental + Site" ~ "Environmental + Site",
        Test_Name == "Fraction Effects Alone" ~ "Fraction Effects",
        TRUE ~ Test_Name
      )
    )

  # Create wide format
  model_wide <- model_data %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = Fraction,
      values_from = c(Total_Df, Total_SS, Model_R2),
      names_sep = "_"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(factor(Test_Display, levels = c("Environmental (D+T)", "Geographic (Lat+Long)", 
                                           "Environmental + Spatial", "Full Model", "Site Effects",
                                           "Environmental + Site", "Fraction Effects")))
  
  return(model_wide)
}

# ============ Function 3: Conditional Variance Partitioning Table ============

create_conditional_table <- function(data) {
  
  # Filter conditional tests
  conditional_tests <- c("PP_mean | All Geography", "All Geography | PP_mean",
                        "Geography | Environment", "Site | All Measured Variables")
  
  conditional_data <- data %>%
    dplyr::filter(Test_Name %in% conditional_tests) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Fraction, Test_Name) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(Conditional_R2 = dplyr::first(Model_R2), .groups = 'drop') %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Test_Display = dplyr::case_when(
        Test_Name == "PP_mean | All Geography" ~ "PP_mean | Geography",
        Test_Name == "All Geography | PP_mean" ~ "Geography | PP_mean",
        Test_Name == "Geography | Environment" ~ "Geography | Environment", 
        Test_Name == "Site | All Measured Variables" ~ "Site | All Variables",
        TRUE ~ Test_Name
      )
    )
  
  # Create wide format
  conditional_wide <- conditional_data %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = Fraction,
      values_from = Conditional_R2,
      names_prefix = "R2_"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(factor(Test_Display, levels = c("Geography | Environment", "PP_mean | Geography",
                                           "Geography | PP_mean", "Site | All Variables")))
  
  return(conditional_wide)
}

# ============= Function 4: Detailed Terms Table ===========

create_detailed_term_table <- function(data) {
  
  # Select main effect tests and organize by term
  main_tests <- c("Environmental Baseline (D+T)", "Geographic Combined (Lat+Long)",
                 "Environmental + Spatial", "Full Model", "Site Effects Alone",
                 "Environmental + Site", "Fraction Effects Alone")
  
  detailed_data <- data %>%
    dplyr::filter(Test_Name %in% main_tests, !is.na(Term), Term != "ERROR") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Test_Term = paste(Test_Name, Term, sep = " - "),
      Statistic_Set = paste(Df, round(SumOfSqs, 4), round(R2, 4), 
                           round(F_Statistic, 2), round(P_value, 4), sep = " | ")
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(Fraction, Test_Name, Term, Statistic_Set) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = Fraction,
      values_from = Statistic_Set
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(Test_Name, Term)
  
  return(detailed_data)
}

# ============ Function 5: PERMDISP Table ============

create_permdisp_table <- function(permdisp_data) {
  
  # Convert permdisp results to data frame
  permdisp_df <- data.frame()
  
  for (fraction_name in names(permdisp_data)) {
    fraction_permdisp <- permdisp_data[[fraction_name]]
    
    for (var_name in names(fraction_permdisp)) {
      if (!is.null(fraction_permdisp[[var_name]])) {
        permdisp_df <- rbind(permdisp_df, data.frame(
          Fraction = fraction_name,
          Variable = var_name,
          F_value = fraction_permdisp[[var_name]]$F_value,
          P_value = fraction_permdisp[[var_name]]$p_value,
          Assumption_Met = ifelse(fraction_permdisp[[var_name]]$assumption_met, "PASSED", "VIOLATED"),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(permdisp_df)
}

# =========== Function 6: Export to Excel ============

export_supplementary_tables <- function(table_list, filename = "PERMANOVA_Supplementary_Tables.xlsx", output_dir = "./") {
  
  # Create output directory if it doesn't exist
  if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
    dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    cat("✓ Created output directory:", output_dir, "\n")
  }
  
  # Full file path
  full_path <- file.path(output_dir, filename)
  
  # Create workbook
  wb <- createWorkbook()
  
  # Define styles
  header_style <- createStyle(textDecoration = "bold", halign = "center")
  number_style <- createStyle(numFmt = "0.000", halign = "center")
  
  # Add each table as a worksheet
  sheet_names <- c("Individual_Effects", "Model_Comparison", "Conditional_Partitioning", 
                   "Detailed_Terms", "PERMDISP_Assumptions")
  
  for (i in 1:length(table_list)) {
    
    # Use sheet name based on position
    sheet_name <- sheet_names[i]
    
    # Add worksheet
    addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
    
    # Write data
    writeData(wb, sheet_name, table_list[[i]], startRow = 1, startCol = 1)
    
    # Apply header formatting
    addStyle(wb, sheet_name, header_style, rows = 1, cols = 1:ncol(table_list[[i]]))
    
    # Apply number formatting to numeric columns
    numeric_cols <- which(sapply(table_list[[i]], is.numeric))
    if (length(numeric_cols) > 0) {
      addStyle(wb, sheet_name, number_style, rows = 2:(nrow(table_list[[i]]) + 1), 
               cols = numeric_cols, gridExpand = TRUE)
    }
    
    # Auto-adjust column widths
    setColWidths(wb, sheet_name, cols = 1:ncol(table_list[[i]]), widths = "auto")
  }
  
  # Save workbook
  saveWorkbook(wb, full_path, overwrite = TRUE)
  cat("✓ Exported supplementary tables to:", full_path, "\n")
}

# ========== Create and Export All Tables ===========

# 1. Create individual tables
individual_table <- create_individual_effects_table(comprehensive_stats)
model_table <- create_model_comparison_table(comprehensive_stats)
conditional_table <- create_conditional_table(comprehensive_stats)
detailed_table <- create_detailed_term_table(comprehensive_stats)
permdisp_table <- create_permdisp_table(all_permdisp_results)

# 2. Combine into list
supp_tables <- list(
  individual_effects = individual_table,
  model_comparison = model_table,
  conditional_partitioning = conditional_table,
  detailed_terms = detailed_table,
  permdisp_assumptions = permdisp_table
)

# 3. Export to Excel
export_supplementary_tables(supp_tables, "PERMANOVA_Supplementary_Tables.xlsx", output_dir = "./PERMANOVA_18S")

# 4. Export individual CSV files
write.csv(supp_tables$individual_effects, "./PERMANOVA_18S/Supp_Table_S1_Individual_Effects.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(supp_tables$model_comparison, "./PERMANOVA_18S/Supp_Table_S2_Model_Comparison.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(supp_tables$conditional_partitioning, "./PERMANOVA_18S/Supp_Table_S3_Conditional_Partitioning.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(supp_tables$detailed_terms, "./PERMANOVA_18S/Supp_Table_S4_Detailed_Terms.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(supp_tables$permdisp_assumptions, "./PERMANOVA_18S/Supp_Table_S5_PERMDISP_Assumptions.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 5. Preview what was created
cat("Tables created:\n")
print(names(supp_tables))
cat("\nFiles exported to ./PERMANOVA_18S/\n")
```


===============================================================================
------------------ PCoAs w/ 4th Root Transformed Rel. abund. -----------------
===============================================================================

# Convert raw abundance data (biotic) to relative abundance table (biotic_percs) for bray-curtis distance PCoAs ONLY REMOVE?
```{r}
# Convert to relative abundance (percentages)
biotic_percs_18S <- apply(biotic_18S, 2, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Verify it worked (columns should sum to 1)
colSums(biotic_percs_18S)[1:5]  # Check first 5 samples

```


# Create new directory 
```{r}
dir.create("./PCoA_18S", showWarnings = FALSE)
```


# PCoA using Bray-Curtis dissimilarity of 4th root transformation relative abundance data
All samples with ggplot2 colored by fraction
```{r}
# Data preparation
b <- biotic_percs_18S_4th
#b <- b[rowSums(b) > 0, ] # Remove taxa that are absent in all samples
c <- metadata_18S

# Compute Bray-Curtis distance matrix
distance_matrix <- vegdist(b, method = "bray")

# Perform PCoA
pcoa_result <- cmdscale(distance_matrix, eig = TRUE, k = 2)
pcoa_points <- as.data.frame(pcoa_result$points)
colnames(pcoa_points) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")
pcoa_points$fraction <- c$fraction

# Calculate the percentage of variation explained by each axis
eig_values <- pcoa_result$eig
var_explained <- eig_values / sum(eig_values) * 100

# Define color palette for the three fractions
c3 <- c("sessile" = "#3B8C6E","500" = "#F68C30","100" = "#FEBC2C")

# Function to calculate convex hull
find_hull <- function(df) df[chull(df$PCoA1, df$PCoA2), ]

# Calculate convex hulls for each fraction
hulls_pcoa <- pcoa_points %>% 
  group_by(fraction) %>% 
  do(find_hull(.))

# Plot PCoA using ggplot2 and color by fraction
pcoa_plot <- ggplot(pcoa_points, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = fraction)) +
  # Add hulls first (so they appear behind points)
  geom_polygon(data = hulls_pcoa, 
               aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, group = fraction, fill = fraction), 
               alpha = 0.2, 
               color = NA) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c3) +  # Add fill scale for hulls
  labs(title = "All Samples PCoA by Bray-Curtis Dissimilarity- Size Fraction",
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", round(var_explained[1], 2), "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", round(var_explained[2], 2), "%)"),
       color = "Fraction") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right", 
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 14, color = "black"),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1)) +
  guides(fill = "none")  # Hide fill legend (since it's same as color)

print(pcoa_plot)

# Save Plot
ggsave("18S_PCoA_fraction_BRAY.pdf", plot = last_plot(), device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA",
      scale = 0.7, width = 10, height = 8.5, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

# Plot All PCoAs: Bry-Curtis Dissimilarity based on 4th Root Transformed Proportions
```{r}
# 13-PLOT FIGURE: Bray-Curtis PCoA on 4th Root Transformed Relative Abundance Data
#library(patchwork)
#library(dplyr)

# Define color palettes (same as NMDS plots)
# c3_fractions <- c("sessile" = "#3B8C6E","500" = "#F68C30","100" = "#FEBC2C")
# c12_sites <- c("Alderdice_deep1" = "#0E4077", "Alderdice_deep2" = "#0E4077", "Alderdice_shallow" = "#9DC9D3", 
#               "Bright_deep1" = "#3B8C6E", "Bright_deep2" = "#3B8C6E", "Bright_shallow" = "#B0DBBB", 
#               "Diaphus_deep1" = "#996599", "Diaphus_deep2" = "#996599", "EFGB_deep" = "#F68C30", 
#               "EFGB_shallow" = "#FEBC2C", "McGrail_deep" = "#015951", "Stetson_shallow" = "#FF595E")

#  Function to create PCoA plot
create_pcoa_plot <- function(data_matrix, metadata, color_var, color_palette, title_text, legend_title) {
  # Compute distance matrix and PCoA
  distance_matrix <- vegdist(data_matrix, method = "bray")
  pcoa_result <- cmdscale(distance_matrix, eig = TRUE, k = 2)
  pcoa_points <- as.data.frame(pcoa_result$points)
  colnames(pcoa_points) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")
  
  # Add metadata
  pcoa_points[[color_var]] <- metadata[[color_var]]
  
  # Calculate variance explained
  eig_values <- pcoa_result$eig[pcoa_result$eig > 0]  # Only positive eigenvalues
  var_explained <- eig_values / sum(eig_values) * 100
  
  # Create hulls if categorical variable
  # Create plots without hulls for categorical variables
  if(color_var %in% c("fraction", "sitelocality")) {
    plot <- ggplot(pcoa_points, aes_string(x = "PCoA1", y = "PCoA2", color = color_var)) +
      geom_point(size = 4) +
      scale_color_manual(values = color_palette)
  } else {
    # Continuous variable (depth, turbidity)
    plot <- ggplot(pcoa_points, aes_string(x = "PCoA1", y = "PCoA2", color = color_var)) +
      geom_point(size = 4) +
      scale_color_viridis_c(option = if(color_var == "depth") "mako" else "cividis")
  }
    
  # Add common elements
  plot <- plot +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(title = title_text,
         x = paste0("PCoA1 (", round(var_explained[1], 1), "%)"),
         y = paste0("PCoA2 (", round(var_explained[2], 1), "%)"),
         color = legend_title) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
          panel.grid = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
          legend.position = "bottom")
  
  return(plot)
}

# ROW 1: All samples - use the main 4th root matrix
plot1_all <- create_pcoa_plot(biotic_percs_18S_4th, meta_consistent, "fraction", c3_fractions, 
                              "All Samples", "Fraction")

# ROW 2: Sessile fraction
plot2_sessile_site <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th), meta_sessile, "sitelocality", c12_sites, 
                                       "Sessile - Site", "Site")
plot2_sessile_depth <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th), meta_sessile, "depth", NULL, 
                                       "Sessile - Depth", "Depth (meters)")
plot2_sessile_turb <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th), meta_sessile, "turbidity_std_rank", NULL, 
                                       "Sessile - Turbidity", "Turbidity Rank")

# ROW 3: Motile fraction  
plot3_motile_site <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th), meta_motile, "sitelocality", c12_sites, 
                                      "Motile - Site", "Site")
plot3_motile_depth <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th), meta_motile, "depth", NULL, 
                                      "Motile - Depth", "Depth (meters)")
plot3_motile_turb <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th), meta_motile, "turbidity_std_rank", NULL, 
                                      "Motile - Turbidity", "Turbidity Rank")

# ROW 4: 100 micron fraction
plot4_100_site <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th), meta_100, "sitelocality", c12_sites, 
                                   "100μm - Site", "Site")
plot4_100_depth <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th), meta_100, "depth", NULL, 
                                   "100μm - Depth", "Depth (meters)")
plot4_100_turb <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th), meta_100, "turbidity_std_rank", NULL, 
                                   "100μm - Turbidity", "Turbidity Rank")

# ROW 5: 500 micron fraction
plot5_500_site <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_500_4th), meta_500, "sitelocality", c12_sites, 
                                   "500μm - Site", "Site")
plot5_500_depth <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_500_4th), meta_500, "depth", NULL, 
                                   "500μm - Depth", "Depth (meters)")
plot5_500_turb <- create_pcoa_plot(t(biotic_percs_18S_500_4th), meta_500, "turbidity_std_rank", NULL, 
                                   "500μm - Turbidity", "Turbidity Rank")

# Remove legends from plots you don't want them on
plot3_motile_site <- plot3_motile_site + theme(legend.position = "none")
plot3_motile_depth <- plot3_motile_depth + theme(legend.position = "none")
plot3_motile_turb <- plot3_motile_turb + theme(legend.position = "none")

plot4_100_site <- plot4_100_site + theme(legend.position = "none")
plot4_100_depth <- plot4_100_depth + theme(legend.position = "none")
plot4_100_turb <- plot4_100_turb + theme(legend.position = "none")

plot5_500_site <- plot5_500_site + theme(legend.position = "none")
plot5_500_depth <- plot5_500_depth + theme(legend.position = "none")
plot5_500_turb <- plot5_500_turb + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Combine all plots using patchwork
combined_13_plots <- (plot1_all + plot_spacer() + plot_spacer()) /
                     (plot2_sessile_site + plot2_sessile_depth + plot2_sessile_turb) /
                     (plot3_motile_site + plot3_motile_depth + plot3_motile_turb) /
                     (plot4_100_site + plot4_100_depth + plot4_100_turb) /
                     (plot5_500_site + plot5_500_depth + plot5_500_turb)



# Keep legends on: plot1_all, plot2_sessile_site, plot2_sessile_depth, plot2_sessile_turb

# Add overall title and labels - UPDATED TITLE
combined_13_plots <- combined_13_plots + 
  plot_annotation(
    title = "PCoA Analysis: Bray-Curtis Dissimilarity on 4th Root Transformed Relative Abundance Data",
    tag_levels = list(c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G", "H", "I", "J", "K", "L", "M")),
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
  )


# Print the combined plot
print(combined_13_plots)

# Save the combined plot - UPDATED FILENAME
ggsave("18S_PCoA_BrayCurtis_4thRoot_13plots_Combined.pdf", 
       plot = combined_13_plots, 
       device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA",
       scale = 1, 
       width = 14,      # Wide enough for 3 plots per row
       height = 20,     # Tall enough for 5 rows
       units = "in", 
       dpi = 300)
```

#------NEW FINAL PCoA PLOTS -------

```{r plot PCoA All Samples}
setwd("path/to/folder")
# ---------------------- CREATE PCoA PLOTS (3-PANEL APPROACH) -----------------
# Compute distance matrix and PCoA for plotting
distance_matrix_plot <- vegdist(biotic_percs_18S_4th, method = "bray")
pcoa_result_plot <- cmdscale(distance_matrix_plot, eig = TRUE, k = 2)
pcoa_scores <- as.data.frame(pcoa_result_plot$points)
colnames(pcoa_scores) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")

# Add metadata to scores
pcoa_scores$sitelocality <- meta_consistent$sitelocality
pcoa_scores$depth <- meta_consistent$depth
pcoa_scores$turbidity_std_rank <- meta_consistent$turbidity_std_rank

# Calculate variance explained
eig_values_plot <- pcoa_result_plot$eig[pcoa_result_plot$eig > 0]
var_explained_plot <- eig_values_plot / sum(eig_values_plot) * 100
axis1_var_pcoa <- round(var_explained_plot[1], 1)
axis2_var_pcoa <- round(var_explained_plot[2], 1)

# Set factor levels for sitelocality if needed
# pcoa_scores$sitelocality <- factor(pcoa_scores$sitelocality, levels = ordered_sites)

# ---------------------- PANEL A: PCoA BY SITE ---------------------
p_pcoa_site <- ggplot(pcoa_scores, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
  # Add reference lines
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Add points with styling matching eDNA plots
  geom_point(aes(fill = sitelocality), size = 8, shape = 21,alpha = 0.9) + 
  
  # Use site color scheme
  scale_fill_manual(values = c12_sites) +
  
  # Labels and theme
  labs(title = "18S: PCoA Bray-Curtis by Site (4th Root)", 
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", axis1_var_pcoa, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", axis2_var_pcoa, "%)"),
       fill = "Site") +
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16)) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Site", ncol = 3, override.aes = list(size = 4)))

print(p_pcoa_site)

# ---------------------- PANEL B: PCoA BY DEPTH ----------------------
p_pcoa_depth <- ggplot(pcoa_scores, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
  # Add reference lines
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Add points colored by depth
  geom_point(aes(fill = depth), size = 8, shape = 21, alpha = 0.9) + 
  
  # Depth color scale (matching eDNA)
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", name = "Depth (meters)", direction = -1) +
  
  # Labels and theme
  labs(title = "18S: PCoA by Depth (4th Root)", 
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", axis1_var_pcoa, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", axis2_var_pcoa, "%)")) +
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16)) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Depth (meters)", 
                               title.position = "top", 
                               barwidth = 15, barheight = 1))

print(p_pcoa_depth)

# ---------------------- PANEL C: PCoA BY TURBIDITY ----------------------
p_pcoa_turbidity <- ggplot(pcoa_scores, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
  # Add reference lines
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Add points colored by turbidity
  geom_point(aes(fill = turbidity_std_rank), size = 8, shape = 21, alpha = 0.9) + 
  
  # Turbidity color scale (matching eDNA)
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis", name = "Turbidity Rank") +
  
  # Labels and theme
  labs(title = "18S: PCoA by Turbidity (4th Root)", 
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", axis1_var_pcoa, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", axis2_var_pcoa, "%)")) +
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16)) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Turbidity Rank", 
                               title.position = "top", 
                               barwidth = 15, barheight = 1))

print(p_pcoa_turbidity)

# ---------------------- SAVE INDIVIDUAL PLOTS -----------------
# Save individual plots for later combination in Adobe Illustrator
ggsave("18S_PCoA_Site_ALL.pdf", plot = p_pcoa_site, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

ggsave("18S_PCoA_Depth_ALL.pdf", plot = p_pcoa_depth, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

ggsave("18S_PCoA_Turbidity_ALL.pdf", plot = p_pcoa_turbidity, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

# Optional: Create and save a combined plot for reference
# Remove individual titles for combined plot
p_pcoa_site_panel <- p_pcoa_site + labs(title = NULL)
p_pcoa_depth_panel <- p_pcoa_depth + labs(title = NULL)
p_pcoa_turbidity_panel <- p_pcoa_turbidity + labs(title = NULL)

# Three-panel combination
combined_pcoa_3panel <- p_pcoa_site_panel | p_pcoa_depth_panel | p_pcoa_turbidity_panel

# Add overall title and panel labels
combined_pcoa_3panel <- combined_pcoa_3panel +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "18S-ARMS All Samples: PCoA Bray-Curtis (4th Root Transformed)",
    tag_levels = "A",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5))
  )

# Save combined plot
ggsave("18S_PCoA_Combined_ALL.pdf", plot = combined_pcoa_3panel, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 14, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

# Plot PCoA Motile and Sessile Fractions
```{r}
# ============= SESSILE FRACTION PCoA PLOTS =============
# Make sure the data is properly oriented (samples as rows)
if(ncol(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th) == nrow(meta_sessile)) {
  # Data needs to be transposed (samples should be rows)
  b_sessile <- t(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th)
} else if(nrow(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th) == nrow(meta_sessile)) {
  # Data is already correctly oriented
  b_sessile <- biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th
}

# Compute Bray-Curtis distance matrix
distance_matrix_sessile <- vegdist(b_sessile, method = "bray")

# Perform PCoA
pcoa_result_sessile <- cmdscale(distance_matrix_sessile, eig = TRUE, k = 2)
pcoa_points_sessile <- as.data.frame(pcoa_result_sessile$points)
colnames(pcoa_points_sessile) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")

# Add metadata
pcoa_points_sessile$sitelocality <- meta_sessile$sitelocality
pcoa_points_sessile$depth <- meta_sessile$depth
pcoa_points_sessile$turbidity_std_rank <- meta_sessile$turbidity_std_rank

# Calculate the percentage of variation explained by each axis
eig_values_sessile <- pcoa_result_sessile$eig[pcoa_result_sessile$eig > 0]
var_explained_sessile <- eig_values_sessile / sum(eig_values_sessile) * 100
axis1_var_sessile <- round(var_explained_sessile[1], 1)
axis2_var_sessile <- round(var_explained_sessile[2], 1)

# ---------------------- SESSILE: PCoA BY SITE ---------------------
p_sessile_site <- ggplot(pcoa_points_sessile, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = sitelocality), size = 8, shape = 21, alpha = 0.9) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c12_sites) +
  labs(title = "Sessile: PCoA Bray-Curtis by Site (4th Root)", 
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", axis1_var_sessile, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", axis2_var_sessile, "%)"),
       fill = "Site") +
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16)) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Site", ncol = 3, override.aes = list(size = 4)))

# ---------------------- SESSILE: PCoA BY DEPTH ----------------------
p_sessile_depth <- ggplot(pcoa_points_sessile, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = depth), size = 8, shape = 21, alpha = 0.9) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", name = "Depth (meters)", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Sessile: PCoA by Depth (4th Root)", 
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", axis1_var_sessile, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", axis2_var_sessile, "%)")) +
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16)) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Depth (meters)", 
                             title.position = "top", 
                             barwidth = 15, barheight = 1))

# ---------------------- SESSILE: PCoA BY TURBIDITY ----------------------
p_sessile_turbidity <- ggplot(pcoa_points_sessile, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = turbidity_std_rank), size = 8, shape = 21, alpha = 0.9) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis", name = "Turbidity Rank") +
  labs(title = "Sessile: PCoA by Turbidity (4th Root)", 
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", axis1_var_sessile, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", axis2_var_sessile, "%)")) +
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16)) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Turbidity Rank", 
                             title.position = "top", 
                             barwidth = 15, barheight = 1))

# Save sessile plots
ggsave("18S_Sessile_PCoA_Site.pdf", plot = p_sessile_site, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

ggsave("18S_Sessile_PCoA_Depth.pdf", plot = p_sessile_depth, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

ggsave("18S_Sessile_PCoA_Turbidity.pdf", plot = p_sessile_turbidity, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)


# ============= MOTILE FRACTION PCoA PLOTS =============
# Make sure the data is properly oriented (samples as rows)
if(ncol(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th) == nrow(meta_motile)) {
  # Data needs to be transposed (samples should be rows)
  b_motile <- t(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th)
} else if(nrow(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th) == nrow(meta_motile)) {
  # Data is already correctly oriented
  b_motile <- biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th
}

# Compute Bray-Curtis distance matrix
distance_matrix_motile <- vegdist(b_motile, method = "bray")

# Perform PCoA
pcoa_result_motile <- cmdscale(distance_matrix_motile, eig = TRUE, k = 2)
pcoa_points_motile <- as.data.frame(pcoa_result_motile$points)
colnames(pcoa_points_motile) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")

# Add metadata
pcoa_points_motile$sitelocality <- meta_motile$sitelocality
pcoa_points_motile$depth <- meta_motile$depth
pcoa_points_motile$turbidity_std_rank <- meta_motile$turbidity_std_rank

# Calculate the percentage of variation explained by each axis
eig_values_motile <- pcoa_result_motile$eig[pcoa_result_motile$eig > 0]
var_explained_motile <- eig_values_motile / sum(eig_values_motile) * 100
axis1_var_motile <- round(var_explained_motile[1], 1)
axis2_var_motile <- round(var_explained_motile[2], 1)

# ---------------------- MOTILE: PCoA BY SITE ---------------------
p_motile_site <- ggplot(pcoa_points_motile, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = sitelocality), size = 8, shape = 21, alpha = 0.9) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c12_sites) +
  labs(title = "Motile: PCoA Bray-Curtis by Site (4th Root)", 
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", axis1_var_motile, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", axis2_var_motile, "%)"),
       fill = "Site") +
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16)) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Site", ncol = 3, override.aes = list(size = 4)))

# ---------------------- MOTILE: PCoA BY DEPTH ----------------------
p_motile_depth <- ggplot(pcoa_points_motile, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = depth), size = 8, shape = 21, alpha = 0.9) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", name = "Depth (meters)", direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "Motile: PCoA by Depth (4th Root)", 
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", axis1_var_motile, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", axis2_var_motile, "%)")) +
  theme_light() +
```
