```{r}
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16)) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Depth (meters)", 
                             title.position = "top", 
                             barwidth = 15, barheight = 1))

# ---------------------- MOTILE: PCoA BY TURBIDITY ----------------------
p_motile_turbidity <- ggplot(pcoa_points_motile, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = turbidity_std_rank), size = 8, shape = 21, alpha = 0.9) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis", name = "Turbidity Rank") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  labs(title = "Motile: PCoA by Turbidity (4th Root)", 
       x = paste0("PCoA1 (", axis1_var_motile, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA2 (", axis2_var_motile, "%)")) +
  theme_light() + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),  
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 20),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16)) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Turbidity Rank", 
                             title.position = "top", 
                             barwidth = 15, barheight = 1))

# Save motile plots
ggsave("18S_Motile_PCoA_Site.pdf", plot = p_motile_site, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

ggsave("18S_Motile_PCoA_Depth.pdf", plot = p_motile_depth, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

ggsave("18S_Motile_PCoA_Turbidity.pdf", plot = p_motile_turbidity, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 8, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

# Optional: Create combined plots for reference
library(patchwork)

# Sessile combined plot
p_sessile_site_panel <- p_sessile_site + labs(title = NULL)
p_sessile_depth_panel <- p_sessile_depth + labs(title = NULL)
p_sessile_turbidity_panel <- p_sessile_turbidity + labs(title = NULL)

combined_sessile_3panel <- p_sessile_site_panel | p_sessile_depth_panel | p_sessile_turbidity_panel
combined_sessile_3panel <- combined_sessile_3panel +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "18S Sessile Communities: PCoA Bray-Curtis (4th Root)",
    tag_levels = "A",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5))
  )

ggsave("18S_Sessile_PCoA_Combined.pdf", plot = combined_sessile_3panel, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 14, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)

# Motile combined plot
p_motile_site_panel <- p_motile_site + labs(title = NULL)
p_motile_depth_panel <- p_motile_depth + labs(title = NULL)
p_motile_turbidity_panel <- p_motile_turbidity + labs(title = NULL)

combined_motile_3panel <- p_motile_site_panel | p_motile_depth_panel | p_motile_turbidity_panel
combined_motile_3panel <- combined_motile_3panel +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "18S Motile Communities: PCoA Bray-Curtis (4th Root)",
    tag_levels = "A",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.5))
  )

ggsave("18S_Motile_PCoA_Combined.pdf", plot = combined_motile_3panel, device = "pdf", 
       path = "./PCoA_18S", scale = 1, width = 14, height = 5.9, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```


# ----------------- PCoA ENVIRONMENTAL CORRELATION ANALYSIS -----------------
# Check orientation of data
```{r}
#Check orientation - need samples in rows
dim(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th)
dim(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th)
dim(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th)
dim(biotic_percs_18S_500_4th)

# Fix the orientation of all subset matrices before running analysis
biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th <- t(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th)
biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th <- t(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th) 
biotic_percs_18S_100_4th <- t(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th)
biotic_percs_18S_500_4th <- t(biotic_percs_18S_500_4th)

# Verify they're now samples as rows
cat("Sessile dimensions:", dim(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th), "\n")
cat("Sample names in rows?", any(rownames(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th) %in% rownames(meta_sessile)), "\n")
cat("Sample names in rows?", any(rownames(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th) %in% rownames(meta_motile)), "\n")
cat("Sample names in rows?", any(rownames(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th) %in% rownames(meta_100)), "\n")
cat("Sample names in rows?", any(rownames(biotic_percs_18S_500_4th) %in% rownames(meta_500)), "\n")
```


# Testing only: depth, turbidity_std_rank, latitude, longitude
```{r}
# Function to perform environmental correlation analysis
perform_envfit_analysis <- function(data_matrix, metadata, subset_name, output_dir) {
  
  cat("\n=== PCoA ENVIRONMENTAL CORRELATION ANALYSIS FOR", subset_name, "===\n")
  cat("Testing variables: depth, turbidity_std_rank, latitude, longitude\n")
  
  # Compute distance matrix and PCoA
  distance_matrix <- vegdist(data_matrix, method = "bray")
  pcoa_result <- cmdscale(distance_matrix, eig = TRUE, k = min(4, nrow(t(data_matrix))-1))
  
  # Extract PCoA scores (first 4 axes for comprehensive analysis)
  n_axes <- min(4, ncol(pcoa_result$points))
  pcoa_scores_all <- as.data.frame(pcoa_result$points[, 1:n_axes])
  colnames(pcoa_scores_all) <- paste0("PCoA", 1:n_axes)
  
  # Calculate variance explained for all axes
  eig_values <- pcoa_result$eig[pcoa_result$eig > 0]  # Only positive eigenvalues
  pcoa_var_all <- round(eig_values / sum(eig_values) * 100, 2)
  names(pcoa_var_all) <- paste0("PCoA", 1:length(pcoa_var_all))
  
  for(i in 1:min(n_axes, length(pcoa_var_all))) {
    cat(paste0("PCoA", i, ": ", pcoa_var_all[i], "%\n"))
  }
  cat("Cumulative (first", n_axes, "axes):", sum(pcoa_var_all[1:n_axes]), "%\n\n")
  
  # Prepare environmental variables for fitting - ONLY SPECIFIED VARIABLES
  env_vars_for_fit <- metadata %>%
    dplyr::select(any_of(c("depth", "turbidity_std_rank", "latitude", "longitude")))
  
  # Convert all variables to numeric 
  env_vars_for_fit <- env_vars_for_fit %>%
    dplyr::mutate_all(~ as.numeric(.))
  
  # Remove any columns with all NAs or constant values, and keep only numeric columns for correlation analysis
  numeric_env_vars <- env_vars_for_fit %>%
    dplyr::select_if(~ is.numeric(.) && !all(is.na(.)) && length(unique(na.omit(.))) > 1)
  
  # Keep all variables for envfit
  env_vars_for_fit <- env_vars_for_fit %>%
    dplyr::select_if(~ !all(is.na(.)) && length(unique(na.omit(.))) > 1)
  
  if(ncol(env_vars_for_fit) == 0) {
    cat("⚠️ No suitable environmental variables found for", subset_name, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  if(ncol(numeric_env_vars) == 0) {
    cat("⚠️ No numeric environmental variables found for correlation analysis in", subset_name, "\n")
  }
  
  # Test environmental correlations with PCoA axes
  cat("Environmental fitting to PCoA axes...\n")
  envfit_result <- envfit(pcoa_scores_all[, 1:min(2, n_axes)], env_vars_for_fit, permutations = 999)
  print(envfit_result)
  
  # Extract results for reporting
  if(length(envfit_result$vectors$arrows) > 0) {
    envfit_results <- data.frame(
      Subset = subset_name,
      Variable = rownames(envfit_result$vectors$arrows),
      PCoA1_correlation = envfit_result$vectors$arrows[, 1],
      PCoA2_correlation = if(ncol(envfit_result$vectors$arrows) > 1) envfit_result$vectors$arrows[, 2] else NA,
      R_squared = envfit_result$vectors$r,
      p_value = envfit_result$vectors$pvals,
      Significance = ifelse(envfit_result$vectors$pvals < 0.001, "***",
                           ifelse(envfit_result$vectors$pvals < 0.01, "**",
                           ifelse(envfit_result$vectors$pvals < 0.05, "*", ""))),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    print(envfit_results)
  } else {
    envfit_results <- data.frame()
  }
  
  # ===================== INDIVIDUAL VARIABLE CORRELATIONS WITH ALL PCoA AXES =====================
  
  # Calculate correlations between each NUMERIC environmental variable and PCoA axes
  individual_correlations <- data.frame()
  
  if(ncol(numeric_env_vars) > 0) {
    for(var_name in colnames(numeric_env_vars)) {
      env_var <- numeric_env_vars[[var_name]]
      
      for(axis in 1:n_axes) {
        pcoa_axis <- pcoa_scores_all[[paste0("PCoA", axis)]]
        
        # Calculate correlation (remove NA values)
        if(sum(!is.na(env_var) & !is.na(pcoa_axis)) > 3) {  # Need at least 4 pairs
          # Suppress the ties warning since it's not problematic
          cor_result <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(env_var, pcoa_axis, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"))
          
          individual_correlations <- rbind(individual_correlations, data.frame(
            Subset = subset_name,
            Environmental_Variable = var_name,
            PCoA_Axis = paste0("PCoA", axis),
            Correlation = cor_result$estimate,
            p_value = cor_result$p.value,
            Significance = dplyr::case_when(
              cor_result$p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
              cor_result$p.value < 0.01 ~ "**", 
              cor_result$p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
              cor_result$p.value < 0.1 ~ ".",
              TRUE ~ ""
            ),
            stringsAsFactors = FALSE
          ))
        }
      }
    }
  } else {
    cat("⚠️ No numeric variables available for individual correlation analysis\n")
  }
  
  # Create wide format for easier interpretation
  if(nrow(individual_correlations) > 0) {
    individual_correlations_wide <- individual_correlations %>%
      dplyr::select(-p_value, -Significance) %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PCoA_Axis, values_from = Correlation) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(desc(abs(PCoA1)))
    print(individual_correlations_wide)
  }
  
  
  #===================== SAVE RESULTS =====================
  cat("\nSaving PCoA results for", subset_name, "...\n")
  
  # Export envfit results
  if(nrow(envfit_results) > 0) {
    write.csv(envfit_results, 
              file.path(output_dir, paste0("Metabarcoding_PCoA_envfit_results_", subset_name, "_4thRoot.csv")), 
              row.names = FALSE)
  }
  
  # Export individual correlations
  if(nrow(individual_correlations) > 0) {
    write.csv(individual_correlations, 
              file.path(output_dir, paste0("Metabarcoding_PCoA_individual_correlations_", subset_name, "_4thRoot.csv")), 
              row.names = FALSE)
  }
  
  # Export PCoA variance explained
  pcoa_variance <- data.frame(
    Subset = subset_name,
    Axis = paste0("PCoA", 1:n_axes),
    Variance_Explained = pcoa_var_all[1:n_axes],
    Cumulative_Variance = cumsum(pcoa_var_all[1:n_axes])
  )
  write.csv(pcoa_variance, 
            file.path(output_dir, paste0("Metabarcoding_PCoA_variance_explained_", subset_name, "_4thRoot.csv")), 
            row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Export PCoA scores with metadata
  pcoa_scores_export <- pcoa_scores_all
  pcoa_scores_export$sample_id <- rownames(pcoa_scores_export)
  write.csv(pcoa_scores_export, 
            file.path(output_dir, paste0("Metabarcoding_PCoA_scores_", subset_name, "_4thRoot.csv")), 
            row.names = TRUE)
  
  cat("✓ Results saved for", subset_name, "\n")
  
  # Return results for compilation
  return(list(
    envfit_results = envfit_results,
    individual_correlations = individual_correlations,
    pcoa_variance = pcoa_variance,
    pcoa_scores = pcoa_scores_export
  ))
}

#===================== MAIN ANALYSIS: RUN FOR ALL SUBSETS =====================

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "./PCoA/Environmental_Correlations"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

# Initialize compiled results
all_envfit_results <- data.frame()
all_individual_correlations <- data.frame()
all_pcoa_variance <- data.frame()

# Define data subsets and their corresponding metadata
data_subsets <- list(
  "All_Samples" = list(data = biotic_percs_18S_4th, metadata = metadata_18S),
  "Sessile" = list(data = biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th, metadata = meta_sessile),
  "Motile" = list(data = biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th, metadata = meta_motile),
  "100um" = list(data = biotic_percs_18S_100_4th, metadata = meta_100),
  "500um" = list(data = biotic_percs_18S_500_4th, metadata = meta_500)
)

# Run analysis for each subset
for(subset_name in names(data_subsets)) {
  cat("\n" , rep("=", 80), "\n")
  cat("PROCESSING:", subset_name, "\n")
  cat(rep("=", 80), "\n")
  
  subset_data <- data_subsets[[subset_name]]$data
  subset_metadata <- data_subsets[[subset_name]]$metadata
  
  # Check if data exists and has sufficient samples
  if(exists(deparse(substitute(subset_data))) && ncol(subset_data) > 3) {
    results <- perform_envfit_analysis(subset_data, subset_metadata, subset_name, output_dir)
    
    if(!is.null(results)) {
      # Compile results
      if(nrow(results$envfit_results) > 0) {
        all_envfit_results <- rbind(all_envfit_results, results$envfit_results)
      }
      if(nrow(results$individual_correlations) > 0) {
        all_individual_correlations <- rbind(all_individual_correlations, results$individual_correlations)
      }
      all_pcoa_variance <- rbind(all_pcoa_variance, results$pcoa_variance)
    }
  } else {
    cat("⚠️ Skipping", subset_name, "- insufficient data\n")
  }
}

#===================== SAVE COMPILED RESULTS =====================
cat("\n=== SAVING COMPILED RESULTS ===\n")

# Save compiled envfit results
if(nrow(all_envfit_results) > 0) {
  write.csv(all_envfit_results, 
            file.path(output_dir, "Metabarcoding_PCoA_envfit_results_ALL_SUBSETS_4thRoot.csv"), 
            row.names = FALSE)
  cat("✓ Compiled envfit results saved\n")
}

# Save compiled individual correlations
if(nrow(all_individual_correlations) > 0) {
  write.csv(all_individual_correlations, 
            file.path(output_dir, "Metabarcoding_PCoA_individual_correlations_ALL_SUBSETS_4thRoot.csv"), 
            row.names = FALSE)
  cat("✓ Compiled individual correlations saved\n")
}

# Save compiled variance explained
write.csv(all_pcoa_variance, 
          file.path(output_dir, "Metabarcoding_PCoA_variance_explained_ALL_SUBSETS_4thRoot.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Compiled variance explained saved\n")

#===================== SUMMARY REPORT =====================
cat("✓ Analysis completed for", length(names(data_subsets)), "data subsets\n")
cat("✓ Results saved to:", output_dir, "\n\n")

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
for(subset_name in names(data_subsets)) {
  cat("Individual subset files for", subset_name, ":\n")
  cat("  - Metabarcoding_PCoA_envfit_results_", subset_name, "_4thRoot.csv\n")
  cat("  - Metabarcoding_PCoA_individual_correlations_", subset_name, "_4thRoot.csv\n")
  cat("  - Metabarcoding_PCoA_variance_explained_", subset_name, "_4thRoot.csv\n")
  cat("  - Metabarcoding_PCoA_scores_", subset_name, "_4thRoot.csv\n\n")
}

cat("Compiled files:\n")
cat("  - Metabarcoding_PCoA_envfit_results_ALL_SUBSETS_4thRoot.csv\n")
cat("  - Metabarcoding_PCoA_individual_correlations_ALL_SUBSETS_4thRoot.csv\n")
cat("  - Metabarcoding_PCoA_variance_explained_ALL_SUBSETS_4thRoot.csv\n\n")

# Display summary of significant correlations
if(nrow(all_envfit_results) > 0) {
  sig_correlations <- all_envfit_results %>%
    dplyr::filter(p_value < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(Subset, p_value)
  
  if(nrow(sig_correlations) > 0) {
    cat("SIGNIFICANT ENVIRONMENTAL CORRELATIONS (p < 0.05):\n")
    for(i in 1:nrow(sig_correlations)) {
      cat(paste0("  ", sig_correlations$Subset[i], " - ", sig_correlations$Variable[i], 
                 ": R² = ", round(sig_correlations$R_squared[i], 3), 
                 ", p = ", round(sig_correlations$p_value[i], 4), 
                 " ", sig_correlations$Significance[i], "\n"))
    }
  } else {
    cat("⚠️ No significant environmental correlations found across all subsets\n")
  }
}

cat("\n=== ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")
```

#metadata fixes
```{r}
# ================== DIAGNOSE AND FIX metadata_18S IN PLACE ==================

# First, let's see what we're working with
cat("=== CURRENT STATE ===\n")
cat("Community data dimensions:", dim(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th), "\n")
cat("Metadata dimensions:", dim(metadata_18S), "\n")

# Check current sample names
community_samples <- colnames(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th)
metadata_samples <- rownames(metadata_18S)

cat("First 5 community samples:", paste(head(community_samples, 5), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("First 5 metadata samples:", paste(head(metadata_samples, 5), collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Check for any current matches
current_matches <- intersect(community_samples, metadata_samples)
cat("Current matches:", length(current_matches), "\n")

# Now we need to update all your fraction-specific datasets too
cat("\n=== UPDATING FRACTION DATA ===\n")

# Update each fraction dataset
fraction_data_4th[["all_samples"]]$data <- biotic_percs_18S_4th

# For the subset fractions, we need to transpose them too if they exist
if (exists("biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th")) {
  biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th <- t(biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th)
  fraction_data_4th[["sessile"]]$data <- biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th
  cat("Updated sessile fraction\n")
}

if (exists("biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th")) {
  biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th <- t(biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th)
  fraction_data_4th[["motile"]]$data <- biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th
  cat("Updated motile fraction\n")
}

if (exists("biotic_percs_18S_100_4th")) {
  biotic_percs_18S_100_4th <- t(biotic_percs_18S_100_4th)
  fraction_data_4th[["100um"]]$data <- biotic_percs_18S_100_4th
  cat("Updated 100um fraction\n")
}

if (exists("biotic_percs_18S_500_4th")) {
  biotic_percs_18S_500_4th <- t(biotic_percs_18S_500_4th)
  fraction_data_4th[["500um"]]$data <- biotic_percs_18S_500_4th
  cat("Updated 500um fraction\n")
}

```

===================================================================
 ----------------- dbRDA WITH 4th ROOT TRANSF. DATA --------------
===================================================================

distance-based RDA ANALYSES - ENVIRONMENTAL vs SPATIAL DRIVERS
Based on PERMANOVA results showing marginal environmental > spatial effects
dbRDA used to constrain ordination by environmental variables, allowing the visualization of how much 
of the community variation can be directly attributed to measured environmental factors.
Moves beyond "communities differ between sites" to 
"communities differ predictably along depth and turbidity gradients"

dbRDA Tests:
1. depth alone - Primary light attenuation layer driver
2. turbidity_std_rank alone - Secondary environmental driver
3. environmental (depth + turbidity_std_ran) - Combined environmental model 

Spatial Comparison:
4. latitude + longitude - Spatial model for comparison
5. depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude - to see how environmental and spatial vectors relate

Mechanistic Insights:
6. turbidity_std_rank | depth - Turbidity beyond depth
Tests whether turbidity has independent effects beyond depth
7. latitude + longitude | depth + turbidity_std_rank - Geography beyond environment (key test)
Tests whether spatial patterns exist beyond environmental gradients (the key question for dispersal limitation vs environmental filtering)

8. site only - tests site effects

bi-plots produced: Environmental Models: Depth + turbidity vectors 
Spatial Models: Latitude + longitude vectors 
Combined Models: All four vectors together 

Output Interpretation:
Arrows: Direction and strength of environmental gradients
Sample positions: Where communities fall along those gradients
Arrow length: Relative importance of each environmental variable
R² values: Proportion of total community variation explained by constraints

```{r dbRDA setup}
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(openxlsx)
library(conflicted)

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "./dbRDA_18S"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

# ================== SETUP DATA STRUCTURE ==================

fraction_data_4th <- list(
  "all_samples" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S
  ),
  "sessile" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction == "sessile", ]
  ),
  "motile" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction %in% c("100", "500"), ]
  ),
  "100um" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_100_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction == "100", ]
  ),
  "500um" = list(
    data = biotic_percs_18S_500_4th,
    metadata = metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction == "500", ]
  )
)
```

# Create main dbRDA functions
```{r create maine dbRDA functions}
# ================== MAIN dbRDA FUNCTION ==================

run_dbrda <- function(community_data, metadata, fraction_name) {
  
  cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 50), collapse = ""), "\n")
  cat("ANALYZING:", fraction_name, "\n")
  cat(paste(rep("=", 50), collapse = ""), "\n")
  
  # Sample matching
  b <- community_data[rowSums(community_data) > 0, ]
  community_samples <- colnames(b)
  metadata_samples <- rownames(metadata)
  common_samples <- intersect(community_samples, metadata_samples)
  
  b_matched <- b[, common_samples]
  metadata_matched <- metadata[common_samples, ]
  
  # Create Bray-Curtis dissimilarity matrix
  distance_matrix <- vegdist(t(b_matched), method = "bray")
  
  # Ensure metadata order matches distance matrix
  dist_samples <- rownames(as.matrix(distance_matrix))
  metadata_matched <- metadata_matched[dist_samples, ]
  
  cat("Samples in analysis:", length(common_samples), "\n")
  
  # Check for required variables
  required_vars <- c("depth", "turbidity_std_rank", "latitude", "longitude", "sitelocality")
  missing_vars <- required_vars[!required_vars %in% colnames(metadata_matched)]
  if (length(missing_vars) > 0) {
    cat("Missing variables:", paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  analyses <- list()
  
  # ================== Core Environmental Tests ==================
  
  analyses[["depth_alone"]] <- dbrda(distance_matrix ~ depth, data = metadata_matched)
  analyses[["turbidity_alone"]] <- dbrda(distance_matrix ~ turbidity_std_rank, data = metadata_matched)
  analyses[["environmental"]] <- dbrda(distance_matrix ~ depth + turbidity_std_rank, data = metadata_matched)

  # ================== Spatial Comparison Tests ==================
 
  analyses[["spatial"]] <- dbrda(distance_matrix ~ latitude + longitude, data = metadata_matched)
  analyses[["env_spatial_combined"]] <- dbrda(distance_matrix ~ depth + turbidity_std_rank + latitude + longitude, data = metadata_matched)

  # ================== Conditional Tests ==================
 
  analyses[["turbidity_after_depth"]] <- dbrda(distance_matrix ~ turbidity_std_rank + Condition(depth), data = metadata_matched)
  analyses[["spatial_after_env"]] <- dbrda(distance_matrix ~ latitude + longitude + Condition(depth + turbidity_std_rank), data = metadata_matched)
  
  # ================== Control Tests ==================
  
  analyses[["site_only"]] <- dbrda(distance_matrix ~ sitelocality, data = metadata_matched)
  
  # ================== Extract Basic Results ==================
 
  results <- data.frame(
    Fraction = fraction_name,
    Analysis = names(analyses),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Store ANOVA by terms tables for key models
  detailed_anova_tables <- list()
  
  # Extract statistics for each analysis
  for (i in 1:length(analyses)) {
    model <- analyses[[i]]
    analysis_name <- names(analyses)[i]
    
    # Test significance
    test_result <- anova(model, permutations = 999)
    
    # Get R²
    r2_result <- RsquareAdj(model)
    
    # Store basic statistics INCLUDING df
    results$R2[i] <- round(r2_result$r.squared, 4)
    results$Adj_R2[i] <- round(r2_result$adj.r.squared, 4)
    results$F_statistic[i] <- round(test_result$F[1], 2)
    results$p_value[i] <- test_result$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    results$Model_df[i] <- test_result$Df[1]
    results$Residual_df[i] <- test_result$Df[2]
    
    # Extract and store ANOVA by terms for key models
    if (analysis_name %in% c("environmental", "spatial", "env_spatial_combined")) {
      
      if (length(attr(model$terms, "term.labels")) > 1) {
        anova_by_terms <- anova(model, by = "terms", permutations = 999)
        detailed_anova_tables[[paste0(fraction_name, "_", analysis_name)]] <- anova_by_terms
        
        cat(sprintf("\n--- DETAILED ANOVA FOR %s ---\n", toupper(analysis_name)))
        print(anova_by_terms)
      }
    }
    
    cat(sprintf("  %-25s: R² = %.3f, F = %.2f, p = %.3f\n", 
                names(analyses)[i], r2_result$r.squared, test_result$F[1], test_result$`Pr(>F)`[1]))
  }
  
  # Format p-values
  results$p_formatted <- ifelse(results$p_value < 0.001, "< 0.001", 
                               sprintf("%.3f", results$p_value))
  
  return(list(
    results = results, 
    models = analyses,
    anova_tables = detailed_anova_tables
  ))
}

# ================== RUN ANALYSES ==================

all_results_dbrda <- list()
all_models_dbrda <- list()
all_anova_tables_dbrda <- list()

cat("Starting 18S dbRDA analysis...\n")

for (fraction_name in names(fraction_data_4th)) {
  result <- run_dbrda(
    community_data = fraction_data_4th[[fraction_name]]$data,
    metadata = fraction_data_4th[[fraction_name]]$metadata,
    fraction_name = fraction_name
  )
  
  if (!is.null(result)) {
    all_results_dbrda[[fraction_name]] <- result$results
    all_models_dbrda[[fraction_name]] <- result$models
    all_anova_tables_dbrda <- c(all_anova_tables_dbrda, result$anova_tables)
  }
}

# Combine all results
results_full <- do.call(rbind, all_results_dbrda)

# ================== Panel_A: VARIANCE PARTITIONING ==================
# Attach commonly used dplyr functions explicitly
filter <- dplyr::filter
select <- dplyr::select
mutate <- dplyr::mutate
summarise <- dplyr::summarise
arrange <- dplyr::arrange
group_by <- dplyr::group_by


cat("\n=== CREATING Panel_A: VARIANCE PARTITIONING ===\n")

Panel_A <- results_full %>%
  filter(Analysis %in% c("depth_alone", "turbidity_alone", 
                         "environmental", "spatial", 
                         "env_spatial_combined", "site_only")) %>%
  mutate(
    Analysis_Clean = case_when(
      Analysis == "depth_alone" ~ "Depth alone",
      Analysis == "turbidity_alone" ~ "Turbidity alone",
      Analysis == "environmental" ~ "Environmental (Depth + Turbidity)",
      Analysis == "spatial" ~ "Spatial (Latitude + Longitude)",
      Analysis == "env_spatial_combined" ~ "Environmental + Spatial combined",
      Analysis == "site_only" ~ "Site identity",
      TRUE ~ Analysis
    )
  ) %>%
  select(Fraction, Analysis = Analysis_Clean, R2, Adj_R2, 
         F_statistic, p_value, Model_df, Residual_df, p_formatted) %>%
  arrange(Fraction, 
          factor(Analysis, levels = c("Depth alone", "Turbidity alone",
                                      "Environmental (Depth + Turbidity)",
                                      "Spatial (Latitude + Longitude)",
                                      "Environmental + Spatial combined",
                                      "Site identity")))

print(Panel_A)
write.csv(Panel_A, file.path(output_dir, "Panel_A_Variance_Partitioning.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Panel_A saved\n")

# ================== Panel_B: MARGINAL vs CONDITIONAL EFFECTS ==================

cat("\n=== CREATING Panel_B: MARGINAL & CONDITIONAL EFFECTS ===\n")

marginal_effects <- results_full %>%
  filter(Analysis %in% c("depth_alone", "turbidity_alone", "environmental", "spatial")) %>%
  mutate(
    Effect_Type = "Marginal",
    Analysis_Clean = case_when(
      Analysis == "depth_alone" ~ "Depth",
      Analysis == "turbidity_alone" ~ "Turbidity",
      Analysis == "environmental" ~ "Environmental combined (Depth + Turbidity)",
      Analysis == "spatial" ~ "Spatial combined (Latitude + Longitude)",
      TRUE ~ Analysis
    )
  )

conditional_effects <- results_full %>%
  filter(Analysis %in% c("turbidity_after_depth", "spatial_after_env")) %>%
  mutate(
    Effect_Type = "Conditional",
    Analysis_Clean = case_when(
      Analysis == "turbidity_after_depth" ~ "Turbidity | Depth",
      Analysis == "spatial_after_env" ~ "Spatial | Environmental",
      TRUE ~ Analysis
    )
  )

Panel_B <- bind_rows(marginal_effects, conditional_effects) %>%
  select(Fraction, Effect_Type, Analysis = Analysis_Clean, 
         R2, Adj_R2, F_statistic, p_value, 
         Model_df, Residual_df, p_formatted) %>%
  arrange(Fraction, 
          factor(Effect_Type, levels = c("Marginal", "Conditional")),
          Analysis)

print(Panel_B)
write.csv(Panel_B, file.path(output_dir, "Panel_B_Marginal_Conditional_Effects.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Panel_B saved\n")

# ================== PANEL C: dbRDA AXIS INFORMATION ==================

cat("\n=== CREATING PANEL C: AXIS INFORMATION ===\n")

panel_C_data <- list()

for (fraction in names(all_models_dbrda)) {
  if ("environmental" %in% names(all_models_dbrda[[fraction]])) {
    
    model <- all_models_dbrda[[fraction]][["environmental"]]
    eigenvals <- eigenvals(model)
    total_inertia <- sum(eigenvals)
    
    cat("\n", fraction, ":\n")
    
    for (i in 1:min(length(eigenvals), 3)) {
      axis_var <- round((eigenvals[i] / total_inertia) * 100, 1)
      cumul_var <- round((sum(eigenvals[1:i]) / total_inertia) * 100, 1)
      
      cat(sprintf("  dbRDA%d: Eigenvalue = %.4f, Variance = %.1f%%, Cumulative = %.1f%%\n", 
                  i, eigenvals[i], axis_var, cumul_var))
      
      panel_C_data[[length(panel_C_data) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Fraction = fraction,
        Axis = paste0("dbRDA", i),
        Eigenvalue = eigenvals[i],
        Variance_Percent = axis_var,
        Cumulative_Percent = cumul_var,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }
}

panel_C_df <- do.call(rbind, panel_C_data)
write.csv(panel_C_df, file.path(output_dir, "Panel_C_dbRDA_axis_information.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Panel C saved\n")

# ================== PANEL D: VARIABLE LOADINGS ==================

cat("\n=== CREATING PANEL D: VARIABLE LOADINGS ===\n")

panel_D_data <- list()

for (fraction in names(all_models_dbrda)) {
  if ("environmental" %in% names(all_models_dbrda[[fraction]])) {
    
    model <- all_models_dbrda[[fraction]][["environmental"]]
    bp_scores <- scores(model, display = "bp")
    
    cat("\n", fraction, ":\n")
    
    if (!is.null(bp_scores)) {
      for (i in 1:nrow(bp_scores)) {
        var_name <- rownames(bp_scores)[i]
        dbRDA1 <- round(bp_scores[i, 1], 3)
        dbRDA2 <- if(ncol(bp_scores) > 1) round(bp_scores[i, 2], 3) else 0
        
        cat(sprintf("  %s: dbRDA1 = %.3f, dbRDA2 = %.3f\n", 
                    var_name, dbRDA1, dbRDA2))
        
        panel_D_data[[length(panel_D_data) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          Fraction = fraction,
          Variable = var_name,
          dbRDA1 = dbRDA1,
          dbRDA2 = dbRDA2,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
    }
  }
}

panel_D_df <- do.call(rbind, panel_D_data)
write.csv(panel_D_df, file.path(output_dir, "Panel_D_dbRDA_variable_loadings.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Panel D saved\n")

# ================== Panel_E: ANOVA BY TERMS (WITH RESIDUALS) ==================

cat("\n=== CREATING Panel_E: ANOVA BY TERMS ===\n")

extract_anova_by_terms_with_residuals <- function(model, fraction_name, model_type) {
  
  # Get ANOVA by terms
  anova_result <- anova(model, by = "terms", permutations = 999)
  
  # Convert to dataframe
  anova_df <- as.data.frame(anova_result)
  
  # Get total inertia for R² calculation
  total_inertia <- model$tot.chi
  
  # Add term names and clean up - KEEP RESIDUAL ROW
  anova_df$Term <- rownames(anova_df)
  anova_df <- anova_df %>%
    filter(Term != "Total") %>%  # Remove only Total row
    mutate(
      Fraction = fraction_name,
      Model_Type = model_type,
      Term_Clean = case_when(
        Term == "depth" ~ "Depth",
        Term == "turbidity_std_rank" ~ "Turbidity",
        Term == "latitude" ~ "Latitude",
        Term == "longitude" ~ "Longitude",
        Term == "Residual" ~ "Residual",
        TRUE ~ Term
      ),
      # Calculate R² manually from sum of squares
      R2 = SumOfSqs / total_inertia
    ) %>%
    select(Fraction, Model_Type, Term = Term_Clean, 
           Df, SumOfSqs, F_statistic = F, R2, P_value = `Pr(>F)`)
  
  return(anova_df)
}

# Extract ANOVA by terms for key models
Panel_E_list <- list()

for (fraction in names(all_models_dbrda)) {
  
  # Environmental model
  if ("environmental" %in% names(all_models_dbrda[[fraction]])) {
    model <- all_models_dbrda[[fraction]][["environmental"]]
    cat("  Extracting", fraction, "- Environmental model\n")
    Panel_E_list[[paste0(fraction, "_env")]] <- 
      extract_anova_by_terms_with_residuals(model, fraction, "Environmental (Depth + Turbidity)")
  }
  
  # Spatial model
  if ("spatial" %in% names(all_models_dbrda[[fraction]])) {
    model <- all_models_dbrda[[fraction]][["spatial"]]
    cat("  Extracting", fraction, "- Spatial model\n")
    Panel_E_list[[paste0(fraction, "_spatial")]] <- 
      extract_anova_by_terms_with_residuals(model, fraction, "Spatial (Latitude + Longitude)")
  }
  
  # Combined model
  if ("env_spatial_combined" %in% names(all_models_dbrda[[fraction]])) {
    model <- all_models_dbrda[[fraction]][["env_spatial_combined"]]
    cat("  Extracting", fraction, "- Combined model\n")
    Panel_E_list[[paste0(fraction, "_combined")]] <- 
      extract_anova_by_terms_with_residuals(model, fraction, "Environmental + Spatial")
  }
}

# Combine all ANOVA tables
Panel_E <- do.call(rbind, Panel_E_list)
rownames(Panel_E) <- NULL

# Round for cleaner display
Panel_E <- Panel_E %>%
  mutate(
    SumOfSqs = round(SumOfSqs, 6),
    F_statistic = round(F_statistic, 2),
    R2 = round(R2, 6),
    P_value = round(P_value, 4),
    P_formatted = ifelse(is.na(P_value), "", 
                        ifelse(P_value < 0.001, "< 0.001", 
                              sprintf("%.3f", P_value))),
    Significance = ifelse(is.na(P_value), "",
                         ifelse(P_value < 0.001, "***",
                               ifelse(P_value < 0.01, "**",
                                     ifelse(P_value < 0.05, "*", ""))))
  )

print(Panel_E)
write.csv(Panel_E, file.path(output_dir, "Panel_E_ANOVA_by_Terms.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Panel_E saved\n")

# ================== CREATE COMPREHENSIVE EXCEL WORKBOOK ==================

cat("\n=== CREATING COMPREHENSIVE EXCEL WORKBOOK ===\n")

wb_18S <- createWorkbook()

# Sheet 1: All Results Summary
addWorksheet(wb_18S, "All_Results")
writeData(wb_18S, "All_Results", results_full)

# Sheet 2: Panel A - Variance Partitioning
addWorksheet(wb_18S, "Panel_A_Variance_Part")
writeData(wb_18S, "Panel_A_Variance_Part", Panel_A)

# Sheet 3: Panel B - Marginal & Conditional Effects
addWorksheet(wb_18S, "Panel_B_Marginal_Cond")
writeData(wb_18S, "Panel_B_Marginal_Cond", Panel_B)

# Sheet 4: Panel C - Axis Information
addWorksheet(wb_18S, "Panel_C_Axis_Info")
writeData(wb_18S, "Panel_C_Axis_Info", panel_C_df)

# Sheet 5: Panel D - Variable Loadings
addWorksheet(wb_18S, "Panel_D_Loadings")
writeData(wb_18S, "Panel_D_Loadings", panel_D_df)

# Sheet 6: Panel E - ANOVA by Terms
addWorksheet(wb_18S, "Panel_E_ANOVA_Terms")
writeData(wb_18S, "Panel_E_ANOVA_Terms", panel_E)

# Save workbook
saveWorkbook(wb_18S, 
             file.path(output_dir, "18S_dbRDA_Comprehensive_Results.xlsx"), 
             overwrite = TRUE)

cat("\n✓ 18S Comprehensive Excel file created!\n")
cat("✓ File saved to:", file.path(output_dir, "18S_dbRDA_Comprehensive_Results.xlsx"), "\n")
cat("✓ Total sheets:", length(wb_18S$sheet_names), "\n")

# ================== SUMMARY STATISTICS FOR TEXT ==================

cat("\n=== KEY STATISTICS FOR MANUSCRIPT ===\n")

for (fraction in unique(Panel_A$Fraction)) {
  cat("\n", toupper(fraction), ":\n")
  
  env_r2 <- Panel_A %>% 
    filter(Fraction == fraction, 
           Analysis == "Environmental (Depth + Turbidity)") %>% 
    pull(R2)
  
  spatial_r2 <- Panel_A %>% 
    filter(Fraction == fraction, 
           Analysis == "Spatial (Latitude + Longitude)") %>% 
    pull(R2)
  
  combined_r2 <- Panel_A %>% 
    filter(Fraction == fraction, 
           Analysis == "Environmental + Spatial combined") %>% 
    pull(R2)
  
  cond_spatial_r2 <- Panel_B %>% 
    filter(Fraction == fraction, 
           Analysis == "Spatial | Environmental") %>% 
    pull(R2)
  
  cat(sprintf("  Environmental: R² = %.3f\n", env_r2))
  cat(sprintf("  Spatial: R² = %.3f\n", spatial_r2))
  cat(sprintf("  Combined: R² = %.3f\n", combined_r2))
  cat(sprintf("  Spatial beyond Environmental: R² = %.3f\n", cond_spatial_r2))
  
  env_terms <- Panel_E %>% 
    filter(Fraction == fraction, 
           Model_Type == "Environmental (Depth + Turbidity)",
           Term != "Residual")
  
  if (nrow(env_terms) > 0) {
    cat("  Individual contributions:\n")
    for (i in 1:nrow(env_terms)) {
      cat(sprintf("    %s: R² = %.3f, F = %.2f, p = %.3f\n",
                  env_terms$Term[i], env_terms$R2[i], 
                  env_terms$F_statistic[i], env_terms$P_value[i]))
    }
  }
}

cat("\n✓ All 18S tables and Excel workbook complete!\n")
```

# Create dbRDA biplots
```{r create dbRDA biplots}
# ================== CREATE dbRDA BIPLOTS (ALL_SAMPLES ONLY) ==================

cat("\n=== CREATING dbRDA BIPLOTS FOR ALL_SAMPLES ===\n")

# Biplot function
create_env_biplot <- function(fraction_name, model, model_name, plot_title) {
  
  # Get metadata for this fraction
  if (fraction_name == "all_samples") {
    meta_subset <- metadata_18S  # Change to metadata_ARMS for COI
  } else if (fraction_name == "sessile") {
    meta_subset <- metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction == "sessile", ]
  } else if (fraction_name == "motile") {
    meta_subset <- metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction %in% c("100", "500"), ]
  } else if (fraction_name == "100um") {
    meta_subset <- metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction == "100", ]
  } else if (fraction_name == "500um") {
    meta_subset <- metadata_18S[metadata_18S$fraction == "500", ]
  }
  
  # Extract site scores
  site_scores <- scores(model, display = "sites")
  
  # Handle single axis case
  if (ncol(site_scores) == 1) {
    site_df <- data.frame(
      dbRDA1 = site_scores[, 1],
      dbRDA2 = 0,
      sitelocality = meta_subset$sitelocality[match(rownames(site_scores), rownames(meta_subset))],
      depth = meta_subset$depth[match(rownames(site_scores), rownames(meta_subset))]
    )
  } else {
    site_df <- data.frame(
      dbRDA1 = site_scores[, 1],
      dbRDA2 = site_scores[, 2],
      sitelocality = meta_subset$sitelocality[match(rownames(site_scores), rownames(meta_subset))],
      depth = meta_subset$depth[match(rownames(site_scores), rownames(meta_subset))]
    )
  }
  
  # Extract environmental vectors
  env_df <- NULL
  try({
    env_scores <- scores(model, display = "bp")
    if (!is.null(env_scores) && nrow(env_scores) > 0) {
      if (ncol(site_scores) == 1) {
        env_df <- data.frame(
          variable = rownames(env_scores),
          dbRDA1 = env_scores[, 1],
          dbRDA2 = 0
        )
      } else {
        env_df <- data.frame(
          variable = rownames(env_scores),
          dbRDA1 = env_scores[, 1],
          dbRDA2 = env_scores[, 2]
        )
      }
      
      # Clean variable names
      env_df$variable_clean <- case_when(
        env_df$variable == "depth" ~ "Depth",
        env_df$variable == "turbidity_std_rank" ~ "Turbidity",
        env_df$variable == "latitude" ~ "Latitude",
        env_df$variable == "longitude" ~ "Longitude",
        TRUE ~ env_df$variable
      )
    }
  }, silent = TRUE)
  
  # Calculate variance explained
  eigenvals <- eigenvals(model)
  total_var <- sum(eigenvals)
  
  if (length(eigenvals) >= 2) {
    axis1_var <- (eigenvals[1] / total_var) * 100
    axis2_var <- (eigenvals[2] / total_var) * 100
  } else {
    axis1_var <- (eigenvals[1] / total_var) * 100
    axis2_var <- 0
  }
  
  # Calculate total R²
  r2_total <- sum(eigenvals) / model$tot.chi
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(site_df, aes(x = dbRDA1, y = dbRDA2)) +
    geom_point(aes(fill = sitelocality), size = 6, shape = 21, alpha = 0.8, stroke = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c12_sites, name = "Site") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.3) +
    labs(x = paste0("dbRDA1 (", round(axis1_var, 1), "%)"),
         y = paste0("dbRDA2 (", round(axis2_var, 1), "%)"),
         title = plot_title,
         subtitle = paste0(fraction_name, " - R² = ", round(r2_total, 3))) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      legend.title = element_text(size=20, color = "black"),
      legend.text = element_text(size=18, color = "black"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 22,color = "black", face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5)
    )
  
  # Add environmental vectors if available
  if (!is.null(env_df) && nrow(env_df) > 0) {
    arrow_scale <- max(abs(c(site_df$dbRDA1, site_df$dbRDA2)), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.8
    
    p <- p +
      geom_segment(data = env_df, 
                   aes(x = 0, y = 0, 
                       xend = dbRDA1 * arrow_scale, 
                       yend = dbRDA2 * arrow_scale),
                   arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm")), 
                   color = "#C74238", linewidth = 2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
      geom_text(data = env_df,
                aes(x = dbRDA1 * arrow_scale * 1.15, 
                    y = dbRDA2 * arrow_scale * 1.15, 
                    label = variable_clean),
                color = "#C74238", size = 6, fontface = "bold", inherit.aes = FALSE)
  }
  
  return(p)
}

# ================== OPTION 1: 1×3 PANEL (ALL_SAMPLES ONLY) ==================

# Create plots
plot_env_all <- create_env_biplot("all_samples", all_models_dbrda[["all_samples"]][["environmental"]], 
                                   "Environmental", "Environmental (Depth + Turbidity)")
plot_spatial_all <- create_env_biplot("all_samples", all_models_dbrda[["all_samples"]][["spatial"]], 
                                       "Spatial", "Spatial (Lat + Long)")
plot_combined_all <- create_env_biplot("all_samples", all_models_dbrda[["all_samples"]][["env_spatial_combined"]], 
                                        "Combined", "Environmental + Spatial")

# Create 1×3 panel
panel_1x3 <- grid.arrange(
  plot_env_all, plot_spatial_all, plot_combined_all,
  ncol = 3, nrow = 1,
  top = textGrob("dbRDA: All Samples", gp = gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold"))
)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Figure_dbRDA_AllSamples_1x3.pdf"), 
       plot = panel_1x3, width = 18, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# ================== OPTION 2: 3×3 PANEL (ALL/MOTILE/SESSILE) ==================

# Create all 9 plots
fractions_compare <- c("all_samples", "motile", "sessile")
plot_matrix <- list()

for (frac in fractions_compare) {
  plot_matrix[[paste0(frac, "_env")]] <- create_env_biplot(
    frac, all_models_dbrda[[frac]][["environmental"]], 
    "Environmental", paste0(frac, ": Environmental"))
  
  plot_matrix[[paste0(frac, "_spatial")]] <- create_env_biplot(
    frac, all_models_dbrda[[frac]][["spatial"]], 
    "Spatial", paste0(frac, ": Spatial"))
  
  plot_matrix[[paste0(frac, "_combined")]] <- create_env_biplot(
    frac, all_models_dbrda[[frac]][["env_spatial_combined"]], 
    "Combined", paste0(frac, ": Combined"))
}

# Create 3×3 panel
panel_3x3 <- grid.arrange(
  plot_matrix[["all_samples_env"]], plot_matrix[["all_samples_spatial"]], plot_matrix[["all_samples_combined"]],
  plot_matrix[["motile_env"]], plot_matrix[["motile_spatial"]], plot_matrix[["motile_combined"]],
  plot_matrix[["sessile_env"]], plot_matrix[["sessile_spatial"]], plot_matrix[["sessile_combined"]],
  ncol = 3, nrow = 3,
  top = textGrob("dbRDA Comparison: All Samples, Motile, and Sessile Fractions", 
                 gp = gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold"))
)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "Figure_S_dbRDA_3x3_Comparison.pdf"), 
       plot = panel_3x3, width = 18, height = 18, dpi = 300)
```



# --------------- GEO vs ENV DISTANCES ----------------------
REQUIREMENTS:
1. Biological data matrix (ASVs/OTUs as rows, samples as columns)
2. Sample metadata with fraction information
3. Pre-calculated environmental distance matrices
4. Pre-calculated geographic distance matrices (least distance between sites)

# Load geographic distance matricies
```{r}
distance_dir <- "path/to/folder"

# Geographic distances  
geo_dist_all_df <- read.csv(paste0(distance_dir, "/leastDist_km_ind.csv"), sep = ",", row.names = 1)
geo_dist_mot_df <- read.csv(paste0(distance_dir, "/leastDist_km_motile.csv"), sep = ",", row.names = 1)
geo_dist_sess_df <- read.csv(paste0(distance_dir, "/leastDist_km_sessile.csv"), sep = ",", row.names = 1)

# Convert to Matrix
geo_dist_all_matrix <- as.matrix(geo_dist_all_df)
geo_dist_mot_matrix <- as.matrix(geo_dist_mot_df)
geo_dist_sess_matrix <- as.matrix(geo_dist_sess_df)

# Convert to distance objects
geo_dist_all_dist <- as.dist(geo_dist_all_df)
geo_dist_mot_dist <- as.dist(geo_dist_mot_df)
geo_dist_sess_dist <- as.dist(geo_dist_sess_df)
```

# Load Env distance objects
```{r}
load("/Path/to/RdataFile")
```


# Calculate Bray-Curtis distances on all data subsets (4th root transformed)
# All Samples:
```{r}
b <- biotic_percs_18S_4th
b <- b[rowSums(b) > 0, ]
all_distance_matrix <- vegdist(t(b), method = "bray")
as.matrix(all_distance_matrix)
```

# Motile:
```{r}
b <- biotic_percs_18S_motile_4th
b <- b[rowSums(b) > 0, ]
mot_distance_matrix <- vegdist(t(b), method = "bray")
mot_distance_matrix <- as.matrix(mot_distance_matrix)
```

# Sessile
```{r}
b <- biotic_percs_18S_sessile_4th
b <- b[rowSums(b) > 0, ]
sessile_distance_matrix <- vegdist(t(b), method = "bray")
as.matrix(sessile_distance_matrix)
```

===============================================
 ------------ Standard Mantel Tests -----------
===============================================
All samples
Bray-Curtis distances: all_distance_matrix
Scaled Env data: sEnvMatrix_ind
Distance dataframe: env_dist_df
Scaled distance matrix: env_dist_df_dist
Geographic distance: geo_dist_all_df

Motile 
Bray-Curtis distances: mot_distance_matrix
Scaled Env data: sEnvMatrix_motile
Scaled distance matrix: env_dist_mot
Geographic distance: geo_dist_mot_df

Sessile
Bray-Curtis distances:sessile_distance_matrix
Scaled Env data:sEnvMatrix_sessile
Scaled distance matrix: env_dist_sess
Geographic distance: geo_dist_sess_df

Mantel test with environmental distance (vegan package) - all samples
# Prep data
```{r}
# Convert matrices into 'dist' objects
all_distance_dist <- as.dist(all_distance_matrix)
env_dist_df_dist <- as.dist(env_dist_df)
leastDist.km_ind_dist <- as.dist(geo_dist_all_df)

# motile
mot_distance_dist <- as.dist(mot_distance_matrix)
# sess
sess_distance_dist <- as.dist(sessile_distance_matrix)


#checks
class(all_distance_dist)
class(env_dist_df_dist)
class(leastDist.km_ind_dist)
class(mot_distance_dist)
class(sess_distance_dist)
```

# Run Mantel Test
```{r}
# Perform Mantel test between Bray-Curtis dissimilarity matrix and geographic distance with vegan on euclidean distances
# all samples
mantel_env_all <- vegan::mantel(all_distance_matrix, env_dist_df, method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
mantel_geo_all <- vegan::mantel(all_distance_matrix, leastDist.km_ind_dist, method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
# motile
mantel_env_mot <- vegan::mantel(mot_distance_dist, env_dist_mot, method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
mantel_geo_mot <- vegan::mantel(mot_distance_dist, geo_dist_mot_df, method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
# sessile
mantel_env_sess <- vegan::mantel(sess_distance_dist, env_dist_sess, method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
mantel_geo_sess <- vegan::mantel(sess_distance_dist, geo_dist_sess_df, method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# View results
print(mantel_env_all)
print(mantel_geo_all)
print(mantel_env_mot)
print(mantel_geo_mot)
print(mantel_env_sess)
print(mantel_geo_sess)
#print(mantel_env_ade)
#print(mantel_geo_ade)
```


=============================================
 ----------- Partial Mantel Tests -----------
=============================================
Control for geographic distance while testing the influence of environmental distances on community composition by using Partial Mantel tests. This will allow you to check whether environmental factors still have a significant correlation with Bray-Curtis dissimilarity after accounting for spatial autocorrelation. 
```{r}
# Partial Mantel test: controlling for geographic distance while testing environmental distance
# all
partial_mantel_env_geo <- mantel.partial(all_distance_matrix, as.matrix(env_dist_df), geo_dist_all_matrix, method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
# motile
partial_mantel_env_geo_mot <- mantel.partial(mot_distance_matrix, as.matrix(env_dist_mot), geo_dist_mot_matrix, method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
# sesstile
partial_mantel_env_geo_sess <- mantel.partial(sessile_distance_matrix, as.matrix(env_dist_sess), geo_dist_sess_matrix, method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# Partial Mantel test: controlling for environmental distance while testing geographic distance
# all
partial_mantel_geo_env <- mantel.partial(all_distance_matrix, geo_dist_all_matrix, as.matrix(env_dist_df), method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
# motile
partial_mantel_geo_env_mot <- mantel.partial(mot_distance_matrix, geo_dist_mot_matrix, as.matrix(env_dist_mot), method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
# sessile
partial_mantel_geo_env_sess <- mantel.partial(sessile_distance_matrix, geo_dist_sess_matrix, as.matrix(env_dist_sess), method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# View results
print(partial_mantel_env_geo)
print(partial_mantel_env_geo_mot)
print(partial_mantel_env_geo_sess)

print(partial_mantel_geo_env)
print(partial_mantel_geo_env_mot)
print(partial_mantel_geo_env_sess)
```

=====================================================================
---------- Multiple Regression on distance Matricies (MRM) ----------
=====================================================================
Applying log transformations to address the issue of scale differences between the scaled environmental distances (ranging from 0-4) and geographic distances (ranging from 0-300, with a heavy weigh on 0's from replicates with 0 minimum distance). Use already created dist. class matricies. 
```{r}
# library(ecodist)
# Apply log transformation to environmental distances
log_env_dist_all <- log1p(env_dist_df_dist) #all
log_env_dist_mot <- log1p(env_dist_mot) #motile
log_env_dist_sess <- log1p(env_dist_sess) #sessile

# Apply log transformation to geographic distances
# log1p to handle any zeros
log_geo_dist_all <- log1p(geo_dist_all_dist) #all
log_geo_dist_mot <- log1p(geo_dist_mot_dist) #motile
log_geo_dist_sess <- log1p(geo_dist_sess_dist) #sessile

# Convert dist objects to full matrices
all_distance_mat <- as.matrix(all_distance_dist)
log_env_dist_mat <- as.matrix(log_env_dist_all)
log_geo_dist_mat <- as.matrix(log_geo_dist_all)

# Convert dist objects to vectors
# all
bc_dist_vec <- as.vector(all_distance_dist)
env_dist_vec <- as.vector(log_env_dist_all) 
geo_dist_vec <- as.vector(log_geo_dist_all)
# motile
bc_dist_vec_mot <- as.vector(mot_distance_dist)
env_dist_vec_mot <- as.vector(log_env_dist_mot)
geo_dist_vec_mot <- as.vector(log_geo_dist_mot)
# sess
bc_dist_vec_sess <- as.vector(sess_distance_dist)
env_dist_vec_sess <- as.vector(log_env_dist_sess)
geo_dist_vec_sess <- as.vector(log_geo_dist_sess)

# Create matched dataframes
matched_data_all <- data.frame(
  bc_dist = bc_dist_vec,
  log_env_dist = env_dist_vec,
  log_geo_dist = geo_dist_vec)
# motile
matched_data_mot <- data.frame(
  bc_dist_mot = bc_dist_vec_mot,
  log_env_dist_mot = env_dist_vec_mot,
  log_geo_dist_mot = geo_dist_vec_mot)
# sessile
matched_data_sess <- data.frame(
  bc_dist_sess = bc_dist_vec_sess,
  log_env_dist_sess = env_dist_vec_sess,
  log_geo_dist_sess = geo_dist_vec_sess)

mrm_log_result_all <- ecodist::MRM(matched_data_all$bc_dist ~ matched_data_all$log_env_dist + matched_data_all$log_geo_dist, nperm = 9999) # all
mrm_log_result_mot <- ecodist::MRM(matched_data_mot$bc_dist_mot ~ matched_data_mot$log_env_dist_mot + matched_data_mot$log_geo_dist_mot, nperm = 9999) #motile
mrm_log_result_sess <- ecodist::MRM(matched_data_sess$bc_dist_sess ~ matched_data_sess$log_env_dist_sess + matched_data_sess$log_geo_dist_sess, nperm = 9999) # sessile


# View the results
print(mrm_log_result_all)
print(mrm_log_result_mot)
print(mrm_log_result_sess)
```


# Plot and export results
```{r}
# Load required libraries
# library(ggplot2)
# library(gridExtra)
# library(dplyr)

#  =========== EXTRACT AND EXPORT RESULTS AUTOMATICALLY  ===========

# Function to extract Mantel results
extract_mantel_results <- function(mantel_env_all, mantel_geo_all, 
                                  mantel_env_mot, mantel_geo_mot,
                                  mantel_env_sess, mantel_geo_sess) {
  
  # Extract values from Mantel test objects
  mantel_results <- data.frame(
    Dataset = c("All_species", "Motile", "Sessile", "All_species", "Motile", "Sessile"),
    Distance_Type = c(rep("Environmental", 3), rep("Geographic", 3)),
    Mantel_r = c(
      mantel_env_all$statistic,
      mantel_env_mot$statistic, 
      mantel_env_sess$statistic,
      mantel_geo_all$statistic,
      mantel_geo_mot$statistic,
      mantel_geo_sess$statistic
    ),
    P_value = c(
      mantel_env_all$signif,
      mantel_env_mot$signif,
      mantel_env_sess$signif,
      mantel_geo_all$signif,
      mantel_geo_mot$signif,
      mantel_geo_sess$signif
    ),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Add significance codes
  mantel_results$Significance <- ifelse(mantel_results$P_value < 0.001, "***",
                                       ifelse(mantel_results$P_value < 0.01, "**",
                                             ifelse(mantel_results$P_value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
  
  return(mantel_results)
}

# Function to extract Partial Mantel results
extract_partial_mantel_results <- function(partial_mantel_env_geo, partial_mantel_geo_env,
                                          partial_mantel_env_geo_mot, partial_mantel_geo_env_mot,
                                          partial_mantel_env_geo_sess, partial_mantel_geo_env_sess) {
  
  partial_mantel_results <- data.frame(
    Dataset = c("All_species", "Motile", "Sessile", "All_species", "Motile", "Sessile"),
    Test_Variable = c(rep("Environmental_controlling_Geographic", 3), 
                      rep("Geographic_controlling_Environmental", 3)),
    Partial_Mantel_r = c(
      partial_mantel_env_geo$statistic,
      partial_mantel_env_geo_mot$statistic,
      partial_mantel_env_geo_sess$statistic,
      partial_mantel_geo_env$statistic,
      partial_mantel_geo_env_mot$statistic,
      partial_mantel_geo_env_sess$statistic
    ),
    P_value = c(
      partial_mantel_env_geo$signif,
      partial_mantel_env_geo_mot$signif,
      partial_mantel_env_geo_sess$signif,
      partial_mantel_geo_env$signif,
      partial_mantel_geo_env_mot$signif,
      partial_mantel_geo_env_sess$signif
    ),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Add significance codes
  partial_mantel_results$Significance <- ifelse(partial_mantel_results$P_value < 0.001, "***",
                                               ifelse(partial_mantel_results$P_value < 0.01, "**",
                                                     ifelse(partial_mantel_results$P_value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
  
  return(partial_mantel_results)
}

# Function to extract MRM results
extract_mrm_results <- function(mrm_result_all, mrm_result_mot, mrm_result_sess) {
  
  mrm_results <- data.frame(
    Dataset = c("All_species", "All_species", "Motile", "Motile", "Sessile", "Sessile"),
    Variable = c("Environmental_Distance", "Geographic_Distance", 
                 "Environmental_Distance", "Geographic_Distance",
                 "Environmental_Distance", "Geographic_Distance"),
    Beta_Coefficient = c(
      mrm_result_all$coef[2,1],  # Environmental coefficient
      mrm_result_all$coef[3,1],  # Geographic coefficient
      mrm_result_mot$coef[2,1],  # Environmental coefficient
      mrm_result_mot$coef[3,1],  # Geographic coefficient
      mrm_result_sess$coef[2,1], # Environmental coefficient
      mrm_result_sess$coef[3,1]  # Geographic coefficient
    ),
    P_value = c(
      mrm_result_all$coef[2,2],  # Environmental p-value
      mrm_result_all$coef[3,2],  # Geographic p-value
      mrm_result_mot$coef[2,2],  # Environmental p-value
      mrm_result_mot$coef[3,2],  # Geographic p-value
      mrm_result_sess$coef[2,2], # Environmental p-value
      mrm_result_sess$coef[3,2]  # Geographic p-value
    ),
    Model_R2 = c(
      mrm_result_all$r.squared[1],
      mrm_result_all$r.squared[1],
      mrm_result_mot$r.squared[1],
      mrm_result_mot$r.squared[1],
      mrm_result_sess$r.squared[1],
      mrm_result_sess$r.squared[1]
    ),
    Model_P_value = c(
      mrm_result_all$r.squared[2],
      mrm_result_all$r.squared[2],
      mrm_result_mot$r.squared[2],
      mrm_result_mot$r.squared[2],
      mrm_result_sess$r.squared[2],
      mrm_result_sess$r.squared[2]
    ),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Add significance codes
  mrm_results$Significance <- ifelse(mrm_results$P_value < 0.001, "***",
                                    ifelse(mrm_results$P_value < 0.01, "**",
                                          ifelse(mrm_results$P_value < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
  
  return(mrm_results)
}


#  =========== RUN THE EXTRACTION AND EXPORT ===========


# Extract results (assumes your objects are named as shown)
mantel_results <- extract_mantel_results(mantel_env_all, mantel_geo_all,
                                         mantel_env_mot, mantel_geo_mot,
                                         mantel_env_sess, mantel_geo_sess)

partial_mantel_results <- extract_partial_mantel_results(partial_mantel_env_geo, partial_mantel_geo_env,
                                                         partial_mantel_env_geo_mot, partial_mantel_geo_env_mot,
                                                         partial_mantel_env_geo_sess, partial_mantel_geo_env_sess)

mrm_results <- extract_mrm_results(mrm_log_result_all, mrm_log_result_mot, mrm_log_result_sess)

# Export to CSV
write.csv(mantel_results, "18S_Mantel_Results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(partial_mantel_results, "18S_Partial_Mantel_Results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(mrm_results, "18S_MRM_Results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#  =========== CREATE AUTOMATED VISUALIZATIONS  ===========

# Create visualization data from your actual data objects
viz_data_all <- data.frame(
  BC_Distance = matched_data_all$bc_dist,
  Env_Distance = matched_data_all$log_env_dist,
  Geo_Distance = matched_data_all$log_geo_dist,
  Dataset = "All Species"
)

viz_data_mot <- data.frame(
  BC_Distance = matched_data_mot$bc_dist_mot,
  Env_Distance = matched_data_mot$log_env_dist_mot,
  Geo_Distance = matched_data_mot$log_geo_dist_mot,
  Dataset = "Motile"
)

viz_data_sess <- data.frame(
  BC_Distance = matched_data_sess$bc_dist_sess,
  Env_Distance = matched_data_sess$log_env_dist_sess,
  Geo_Distance = matched_data_sess$log_geo_dist_sess,
  Dataset = "Sessile"
)

# Combine data
viz_data <- rbind(viz_data_all, viz_data_mot, viz_data_sess)

# 1. Environmental effects plot
p1 <- ggplot(viz_data, aes(x = Env_Distance, y = BC_Distance, color = Dataset)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~Dataset, scales = "free") +
  labs(x = "Environmental Distance (log-transformed)",
       y = "Community Dissimilarity (Bray-Curtis)",
       title = "Environmental Filtering Effects",
       subtitle = paste0("Mantel r: All=", round(mantel_results$Mantel_r[1], 3), 
                        ", Motile=", round(mantel_results$Mantel_r[2], 3),
                        ", Sessile=", round(mantel_results$Mantel_r[3], 3))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("All Species" = "grey60", 
                                "Motile" = "#F68C30", 
                                "Sessile" = "#3B8C6E"))

# 2. Geographic effects plot
p2 <- ggplot(viz_data, aes(x = Geo_Distance, y = BC_Distance, color = Dataset)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~Dataset, scales = "free") +
  labs(x = "Geographic Distance (log-transformed km)",
       y = "Community Dissimilarity (Bray-Curtis)",
       title = "Dispersal Limitation Effects",
       subtitle = paste0("Mantel r: All=", round(mantel_results$Mantel_r[4], 3), 
                        ", Motile=", round(mantel_results$Mantel_r[5], 3),
                        ", Sessile=", round(mantel_results$Mantel_r[6], 3))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("All Species" = "grey60", 
                                "Motile" = "#F68C30", 
                                "Sessile" = "#3B8C6E"))

# 3. MRM Effect sizes from extracted data
effect_sizes <- data.frame(
  Dataset = rep(c("All Species", "Motile", "Sessile"), 2),
  Variable = c(rep("Environmental", 3), rep("Geographic", 3)),
  Coefficient = c(
    mrm_results$Beta_Coefficient[mrm_results$Variable == "Environmental_Distance"],
    mrm_results$Beta_Coefficient[mrm_results$Variable == "Geographic_Distance"]
  ),
  Significance = c(
    mrm_results$Significance[mrm_results$Variable == "Environmental_Distance"],
    mrm_results$Significance[mrm_results$Variable == "Geographic_Distance"]
  )
)

p3 <- ggplot(effect_sizes, aes(x = Dataset, y = Coefficient, fill = Variable)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Significance), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
            vjust = ifelse(effect_sizes$Coefficient >= 0, -0.5, 1.2), 
            size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(x = "Functional Group",
       y = "Standardized Coefficient (β)",
       title = "MRM Effect Sizes Comparison",
       subtitle = "Environmental distance dominates across all groups",
       fill = "Distance Type") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10, color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Environmental" = "#408BA8", "Geographic" = "#812A5A")) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.7)

# 4. Model R² from extracted data
r2_data <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("All Species", "Motile", "Sessile"),
  R_squared = c(
    unique(mrm_results$Model_R2[mrm_results$Dataset == "All_species"]),
    unique(mrm_results$Model_R2[mrm_results$Dataset == "Motile"]),
    unique(mrm_results$Model_R2[mrm_results$Dataset == "Sessile"])
  )
)

p4 <- ggplot(r2_data, aes(x = reorder(Dataset, R_squared), y = R_squared, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("R² = ", round(R_squared, 3))), 
            hjust = -0.1, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Functional Group",
       y = "Model R²",
       title = "Model Explanatory Power",
       subtitle = "Sessile communities are most predictable") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, color = "black")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("All Species" = "grey60", 
                                "Motile" = "#F68C30", 
                                "Sessile" = "#3B8C6E")) +
  ylim(0, max(r2_data$R_squared) * 1.1)

# Combine plots
combined_plot <- grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, 
                             layout_matrix = rbind(c(1,2), c(3,4)))

# Save plots
ggsave("18S_Distance_Relationships.pdf", combined_plot, 
       width = 16, height = 11, dpi = 300)

# Print summary
print("Results extracted and exported automatically!")
print("CSV files created:")
print("- 18S_Mantel_Results.csv")
print("- 18S_Partial_Mantel_Results.csv") 
print("- 18S_MRM_Results.csv")
print("")
print("Key findings:")
print(paste("Environmental Mantel r values:", paste(round(mantel_results$Mantel_r[1:3], 3), collapse = ", ")))
print(paste("Geographic Mantel r values:", paste(round(mantel_results$Mantel_r[4:6], 3), collapse = ", ")))
print(paste("Model R² values:", paste(round(r2_data$R_squared, 3), collapse = ", ")))
```


#================= UPDATED GEO VS ENV W/O PP_MEAN =======================

```{r}
#================= ENVIRONMENTAL VARIABLES SETUP FOR 18S ===============
# Create directory for outputs if it doesn't exist
dir.create("/Path/to/output", 
           showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Convert phyloseq sample_data to a regular dataframe
metadata_18S_df <- data.frame(as(metadata_18S, "matrix"))
cat("Metadata class after conversion:", class(metadata_18S_df)[1], "\n")
cat("Number of rows in metadata:", nrow(metadata_18S_df), "\n")

# Select specific environmental variables for 18S analysis
env_vars_wanted_18S <- c("depth", "turbidity_std_rank")
env_vars_available_18S <- intersect(env_vars_wanted_18S, colnames(metadata_18S_df))

cat("\n=== ENVIRONMENTAL VARIABLES FOR 18S ANALYSIS ===\n")
cat("Environmental variables requested:", paste(env_vars_wanted_18S, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Environmental variables available:", paste(env_vars_available_18S, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Convert environmental variables to numeric
metadata_18S_df$depth <- as.numeric(as.character(metadata_18S_df$depth))
metadata_18S_df$turbidity_std_rank <- as.numeric(as.character(metadata_18S_df$turbidity_std_rank))

# Function to create environmental distance matrices for a given subset of samples
create_env_dist_matrices <- function(subset_name, sample_indices = NULL) {
  # If no indices provided, use all samples
  if (is.null(sample_indices)) {
    current_metadata <- metadata_18S_df
  } else {
    current_metadata <- metadata_18S_df[sample_indices, ]
  }
  
  # Select available environmental variables
  env_vars_mantel_18S <- current_metadata %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(env_vars_available_18S)) %>%
    na.omit()
  
  cat(paste0("\n=== ", subset_name, " SUBSET ===\n"))
  cat("Environmental variables included:", ncol(env_vars_mantel_18S), "\n")
  cat("Variables:", paste(colnames(env_vars_mantel_18S), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("Samples with complete environmental data:", nrow(env_vars_mantel_18S), "\n")
  
  # Store sample names with complete environmental data
  env_samples_18S <- rownames(env_vars_mantel_18S)
  
  # Verify data types before correlation
  cat("Data types:", sapply(env_vars_mantel_18S, class), "\n")
  
  # Check correlations between environmental variables
  env_cor_18S <- cor(env_vars_mantel_18S)
  cat("\n=== ENVIRONMENTAL VARIABLE CORRELATIONS ===\n")
  print(round(env_cor_18S, 3))
  
  # Flag high correlations
  high_cor_pairs_18S <- which(abs(env_cor_18S) > 0.7 & env_cor_18S != 1, arr.ind = TRUE)
  if (nrow(high_cor_pairs_18S) > 0) {
    cat("⚠️ High correlations detected between some variables\n")
    for (i in 1:nrow(high_cor_pairs_18S)) {
      var1 <- rownames(env_cor_18S)[high_cor_pairs_18S[i,1]]
      var2 <- colnames(env_cor_18S)[high_cor_pairs_18S[i,2]]
      cat(sprintf("  %s vs %s: r = %.3f\n", var1, var2, env_cor_18S[high_cor_pairs_18S[i,1], high_cor_pairs_18S[i,2]]))
    }
  } else {
    cat("✓ All correlations below 0.7\n")
  }
  
  # Standardize environmental variables for distance calculations
  env_scaled_18S <- scale(env_vars_mantel_18S)
  cat("✓ Environmental variables standardized\n")
  
  # Calculate environmental distance matrix (Euclidean)
  environmental_dist_18S <- dist(env_scaled_18S, method = "euclidean")
  cat("✓ Environmental distance matrix created\n")
  
  # Create log-transformed version
  log_environmental_dist_18S <- as.dist(log1p(as.matrix(environmental_dist_18S)))
  cat("✓ Log-transformed environmental distance matrix created using log1p\n")
  
  # Summary of distance matrix
  cat("\nEnvironmental distance matrix summary:\n")
  cat("- Environmental distance: range", round(range(environmental_dist_18S), 3), "\n")
  cat("- Log environmental distance: range", round(range(log_environmental_dist_18S), 3), "\n")
  
  return(list(
    env_samples = env_samples_18S,
    env_vars = env_vars_mantel_18S,
    env_dist = environmental_dist_18S,
    log_env_dist = log_environmental_dist_18S
  ))
}

# Create distance matrices for all samples
all_env_dist_18S <- create_env_dist_matrices("ALL SAMPLES")

# Filter samples by fraction based on 18S dataset structure
samples_100_18S <- rownames(metadata_18S_df)[metadata_18S_df$fraction == "100"]
samples_500_18S <- rownames(metadata_18S_df)[metadata_18S_df$fraction == "500"]
samples_sessile_18S <- rownames(metadata_18S_df)[metadata_18S_df$fraction == "sessile"]
# Create motile samples by combining 100 and 500 fractions
samples_motile_18S <- c(samples_100_18S, samples_500_18S)

# Create distance matrices for each fraction
env_dist_100_18S <- create_env_dist_matrices("100 FRACTION", samples_100_18S)
env_dist_500_18S <- create_env_dist_matrices("500 FRACTION", samples_500_18S)
env_dist_sessile_18S <- create_env_dist_matrices("SESSILE FRACTION", samples_sessile_18S)
env_dist_motile_18S <- create_env_dist_matrices("MOTILE FRACTION", samples_motile_18S)

# Combine all distance matrices into one object for saving
env_dist_objects_18S <- list(
  all = all_env_dist_18S,
  f100 = env_dist_100_18S,
  f500 = env_dist_500_18S,
  sessile = env_dist_sessile_18S,
  motile = env_dist_motile_18S
)

# Save the combined object
save(env_dist_objects_18S, 
     file = "/Path/to/RdataFile")

cat("\n✓ Environmental distance matrices for all fractions saved to:\n")
cat("/Path/to/RdataFile")

# Print summary of created objects
cat("\n=== SUMMARY OF CREATED ENVIRONMENTAL DISTANCE MATRICES ===\n")
cat("All samples:", length(all_env_dist_18S$env_samples), "samples\n")
cat("100 fraction:", length(env_dist_100_18S$env_samples), "samples\n")
cat("500 fraction:", length(env_dist_500_18S$env_samples), "samples\n")
cat("Sessile organisms:", length(env_dist_sessile_18S$env_samples), "samples\n")
cat("Motile organisms (100+500):", length(env_dist_motile_18S$env_samples), "samples\n")
```


# Load Necessary Data and Geographic Distances
```{r load data and distances}
# Define path for geographic distance matrices
distance_dir <- "path/to/inputs"

# Load geographic distance matrices
geo_dist_all_df <- read.csv(paste0(distance_dir, "/leastDist_km_ind.csv"), sep = ",", row.names = 1)
geo_dist_mot_df <- read.csv(paste0(distance_dir, "/leastDist_km_motile.csv"), sep = ",", row.names = 1)
geo_dist_sess_df <- read.csv(paste0(distance_dir, "/leastDist_km_sessile.csv"), sep = ",", row.names = 1)

# Convert to matrices
geo_dist_all_matrix <- as.matrix(geo_dist_all_df)
geo_dist_mot_matrix <- as.matrix(geo_dist_mot_df)
geo_dist_sess_matrix <- as.matrix(geo_dist_sess_df)

# Convert to distance objects
geo_dist_all_dist <- as.dist(geo_dist_all_df)
geo_dist_mot_dist <- as.dist(geo_dist_mot_df)
geo_dist_sess_dist <- as.dist(geo_dist_sess_df)

# Load the new environmental distance matrices
load("/Path/to/RdataFile")

# Extract environmental distance matrices
env_dist_all_18S <- env_dist_objects_18S$all$env_dist
env_dist_motile_18S <- env_dist_objects_18S$motile$env_dist
env_dist_sessile_18S <- env_dist_objects_18S$sessile$env_dist

# Extract log-transformed matrices
log_env_dist_all_18S <- env_dist_objects_18S$all$log_env_dist
log_env_dist_motile_18S <- env_dist_objects_18S$motile$log_env_dist
log_env_dist_sessile_18S <- env_dist_objects_18S$sessile$log_env_dist

# Extract sample names
env_samples_all_18S <- env_dist_objects_18S$all$env_samples
env_samples_motile_18S <- env_dist_objects_18S$motile$env_samples
env_samples_sessile_18S <- env_dist_objects_18S$sessile$env_samples
```

# Prepare Community Distance Matricies
```{r community distance matricies}
# Helper function to prepare community matrices
prepare_community_matrix <- function(comm_matrix_or_dist, sample_subset) {
  # Check if it's already a dist object
  if(inherits(comm_matrix_or_dist, "dist")) {
    # Convert to matrix for subsetting
    comm_matrix <- as.matrix(comm_matrix_or_dist)
    # Get the intersection of matrix rownames and sample subset
    common_samples <- intersect(rownames(comm_matrix), sample_subset)
    # Subset the matrix
    comm_matrix_subset <- comm_matrix[common_samples, common_samples]
    # Convert back to distance
    return(as.dist(comm_matrix_subset))
  } else if(is.matrix(comm_matrix_or_dist)) {
    # Get the intersection of matrix rownames and sample subset
    common_samples <- intersect(rownames(comm_matrix_or_dist), sample_subset)
    # Subset the matrix
    comm_matrix_subset <- comm_matrix_or_dist[common_samples, common_samples]
    # Convert to distance
    return(as.dist(comm_matrix_subset))
  } else {
    stop("Community matrix/distance object is neither a matrix nor a dist object")
  }
}

# Get matched community distance matrices - assuming these variables exist from your ARMS 18S code
all_distance_dist_18S <- prepare_community_matrix(all_distance_matrix, env_samples_all_18S)
mot_distance_dist_18S <- prepare_community_matrix(mot_distance_matrix, env_samples_motile_18S) 
sess_distance_dist_18S <- prepare_community_matrix(sessile_distance_matrix, env_samples_sessile_18S)

# Match geographic matrices to environmental samples
geo_dist_all_matrix_matched <- geo_dist_all_matrix[env_samples_all_18S, env_samples_all_18S]
geo_dist_mot_matrix_matched <- geo_dist_mot_matrix[env_samples_motile_18S, env_samples_motile_18S]
geo_dist_sess_matrix_matched <- geo_dist_sess_matrix[env_samples_sessile_18S, env_samples_sessile_18S]

# Convert to distance objects
geo_dist_all_dist_matched <- as.dist(geo_dist_all_matrix_matched)
geo_dist_mot_dist_matched <- as.dist(geo_dist_mot_matrix_matched)
geo_dist_sess_dist_matched <- as.dist(geo_dist_sess_matrix_matched)

# Check dimensions
cat("\nMatrix dimensions check:\n")
cat("All samples - Community:", attr(all_distance_dist_18S, "Size"), "| Env:", attr(env_dist_all_18S, "Size"), "| Geo:", dim(geo_dist_all_matrix_matched)[1], "\n")
cat("Motile samples - Community:", attr(mot_distance_dist_18S, "Size"), "| Env:", attr(env_dist_motile_18S, "Size"), "| Geo:", dim(geo_dist_mot_matrix_matched)[1], "\n")
cat("Sessile samples - Community:", attr(sess_distance_dist_18S, "Size"), "| Env:", attr(env_dist_sessile_18S, "Size"), "| Geo:", dim(geo_dist_sess_matrix_matched)[1], "\n")
```

# Simple Mantel Tests
```{r}
# Perform Mantel tests (depth + turbidity only)
# All samples
mantel_env_all_18S <- vegan::mantel(all_distance_dist_18S, env_dist_all_18S, 
                                   method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
mantel_geo_all_18S <- vegan::mantel(all_distance_dist_18S, geo_dist_all_dist_matched, 
                                   method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# Motile
mantel_env_mot_18S <- vegan::mantel(mot_distance_dist_18S, env_dist_motile_18S, 
                                   method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
mantel_geo_mot_18S <- vegan::mantel(mot_distance_dist_18S, geo_dist_mot_dist_matched, 
                                   method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# Sessile
mantel_env_sess_18S <- vegan::mantel(sess_distance_dist_18S, env_dist_sessile_18S, 
                                    method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)
mantel_geo_sess_18S <- vegan::mantel(sess_distance_dist_18S, geo_dist_sess_dist_matched, 
                                    method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# View results
cat("\n=== SIMPLE MANTEL TEST RESULTS (DEPTH + TURBIDITY ONLY) ===\n")
cat("\n--- All Samples ---\n")
print(mantel_env_all_18S)
print(mantel_geo_all_18S)

cat("\n--- Motile Samples ---\n")
print(mantel_env_mot_18S)
print(mantel_geo_mot_18S)

cat("\n--- Sessile Samples ---\n")
print(mantel_env_sess_18S)
print(mantel_geo_sess_18S)
```

# Partial Mantel Tests
Partial Mantel tests (depth + turbidity only)
Environmental controlling for Geographic
```{r Partial Mantel Tests}
# All samples
partial_mantel_env_geo_18S <- vegan::mantel.partial(all_distance_dist_18S, env_dist_all_18S, 
                                                  geo_dist_all_matrix_matched, 
                                                  method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# Motile
partial_mantel_env_geo_mot_18S <- vegan::mantel.partial(mot_distance_dist_18S, env_dist_motile_18S, 
                                                      geo_dist_mot_matrix_matched, 
                                                      method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# Sessile
partial_mantel_env_geo_sess_18S <- vegan::mantel.partial(sess_distance_dist_18S, env_dist_sessile_18S, 
                                                       geo_dist_sess_matrix_matched, 
                                                       method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# Geographic controlling for Environmental
# All samples
partial_mantel_geo_env_18S <- vegan::mantel.partial(all_distance_dist_18S, geo_dist_all_matrix_matched, 
                                                  env_dist_all_18S, 
                                                  method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# Motile
partial_mantel_geo_env_mot_18S <- vegan::mantel.partial(mot_distance_dist_18S, geo_dist_mot_matrix_matched, 
                                                      env_dist_motile_18S, 
                                                      method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# Sessile
partial_mantel_geo_env_sess_18S <- vegan::mantel.partial(sess_distance_dist_18S, geo_dist_sess_matrix_matched, 
                                                       env_dist_sessile_18S, 
                                                       method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

# View results
cat("\n=== PARTIAL MANTEL TEST RESULTS (DEPTH + TURBIDITY ONLY) ===\n")
cat("\n--- Environmental controlling for Geography ---\n")
print(partial_mantel_env_geo_18S)
print(partial_mantel_env_geo_mot_18S)
```
